Includes direct discrimination (treating someone less favourably because of a protected characteristic) and indirect discrimination (apparently neutral practice that disadvantages persons with a protected characteristic).
- Victimisation: Adverse treatment of an individual for reporting harassment, assisting a complainant, or participating in an investigation.

Protected characteristics
LogiCore prohibits discrimination on grounds including, but not limited to: age, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, religion, belief, nationality, ethnic origin, race, political opinion, trade union membership, social origin, health status, and any other characteristic protected under Polish law or EU law.

Examples of prohibited behaviour (operational scenarios)
- Verbal abuse by a warehouse shift leader accusing an employee of theft in front of other staff without evidence.
- Repeated exclusion of a driver from route assignments because of religion or ethnicity.
- Persistent unwelcome sexual comments in a depot breakroom or via company messaging apps.
- Assigning overnight loading shifts only to pregnant employees or those with disabilities without objective justification.
- Threatening to remove forklift certification from an employee who reported safety breaches.
- Mocking an employee s accent or language skills during a morning briefing.
- Posting humiliating photos of colleagues from the loading bay on social media.

Reporting channels
Victims or witnesses should report incidents promptly through one or more of the following channels:
- Direct supervisor or line manager
- HR Business Partner: hrbp@logicore.pl
- Anonymous external whistleblower hotline provided by our vendor (details available on the intranet and at depots) for secure anonymous reports
- Central email for reports: zgloszenia@logicore.pl
- Where necessary, report to relevant authorities (e.g. PIP, police) or regulatory bodies
We encourage staff to report early. Reports may be made orally or in writing and will be treated in accordance with RODO and company confidentiality obligations.

Acknowledgement and investigation procedure
- Acknowledgement: All reports will be acknowledged by HR within 24 hours of receipt (business hours).
- Investigation team appointment: An appropriate investigation team will be appointed within 3 business days. The team will include HR Business Partner, a trained investigator (internal or external), and where appropriate a representative from the depot or legal counsel. For allegations involving managers in HR, an external investigator will be appointed.
- Investigation steps: The investigation will include interviews with the complainant, respondent and relevant witnesses, review of documentary and electronic evidence (shift logs, CCTV where legally admissible, email and messaging records), and collection of any other relevant evidence.
- Timelines: Investigations will be conducted promptly and conclude with a written outcome within 30 calendar days from appointment of the investigation team. If complex circumstances require more time, the parties will be notified of the extension and expected timeframe.
- Standard of proof: Findings will be based on the balance of probabilities.

Confidentiality and data protection
All parties involved in a report or investigation must keep information confidential to the extent possible and in compliance with RODO. Information will be disclosed strictly on a need-to-know basis. Breach of confidentiality without legitimate reason may lead to disciplinary action.

Protection against retaliation
LogiCore prohibits any form of retaliation or victimisation against individuals who make good faith reports, assist with an investigation or participate in proceedings, in line with Kodeks Pracy Art. 18 3e. Retaliation is grounds for disciplinary action up to and including dismissal.

Interim measures and support for victims
During an investigation the company may implement interim measures to protect parties, including temporary re-assignment, change of shifts, temporary suspension with pay pending investigation, modified reporting lines, or temporary leave. Support available to victims includes access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP) offering counselling and legal information, medical or occupational health referrals, and facilitated temporary transfer between depots where operationally feasible.

Consequences and disciplinary measures
If allegations are substantiated, disciplinary measures will be taken proportionate to the severity of the misconduct, up to and including:
- Verbal warning
- Written warning
- Suspension with or without pay (subject to applicable law)
- Termination of employment for cause. For gross violations, dismissal may be effected under Kodeks Pracy Art. 52 where culpable behaviour makes continuation of the employment relationship impossible.
The company may also pursue civil or criminal remedies where appropriate.

External ombudsperson
LogiCore engages an external, independent ombudsperson to provide confidential, impartial advice and to review processes. Contact:
External Ombudsperson Office, LogiCore Partner
Email: rzecznikk@logicore-ombud.pl
Phone: +48 22 555 0100
Role: confidential advisory support, systemic review, and escalation to Management Board if unresolved patterns arise. The ombudsperson is not a substitute for formal HR investigations but may help mediate in suitable cases.

Training and prevention
All employees will receive annual mandatory training on harassment, mobbing, discrimination and bystander intervention tailored to logistics operations. Managers and supervisors will receive additional training annually on handling reports, conducting risk assessments, and lawful investigatory procedures.

Monitoring, review and reporting
HR will maintain anonymised records of all reports, investigations, outcomes and remedial actions. Annual statistics and a summary report will be presented to the Management Board and Safety Committee, including recommendations for systemic improvements. This policy will be reviewed at least every two years or sooner following significant incidents or legal changes.

Responsibilities
- Employees: behave professionally, report suspected breaches, cooperate with investigations.
- Managers: take reports seriously, protect confidentiality, request HR assistance, apply interim measures.
- HR: manage reports, ensure compliance with RODO, appoint investigators, maintain records, provide support and training.

Implementation
This policy is effective immediately and is part of employment terms. Non-compliance with this policy may lead to disciplinary action in accordance with company regulations and applicable Polish law.

Contacts and further information
HR Business Partner: hrbp@logicore.pl
Reports email: zgloszenia@logicore.pl
Anonymous hotline: details on intranet and depot noticeboards
Employee Assistance Program: details on intranet and via HR
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. (Headquarters address), Warszawa. Depots: Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw, Poznan. Operations across Poland and Central Europe.

Approved by: Management Board, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Date of issue: [date]
Review date: [date or 24 months from issue]

title: Performance Review Process and Policy — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
1. Purpose and scope
This Performance Review Process sets out LogiCore Sp. z o.o.'s approach to managing, assessing and developing employee performance across all depots and offices (Warszawa HQ, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań). The policy supports operational excellence in Poland and Central Europe and aligns with applicable laws (Kodeks Pracy, RODO) and internal procedures (HR-POL-TERM for termination, BHP guidance, data protection rules).

2. Review cycle
- Quarterly check-ins: informal to semi-formal performance discussions take place in Q1, Q2 and Q3. These are documented in the HR system as check-in notes. Managers and employees jointly review progress on goals, development needs and wellbeing. Check-ins include a short self-assessment update from the employee prior to the meeting.
- Annual formal review: a comprehensive performance review is conducted in Q4 and constitutes the annual performance rating, compensation recommendations and promotion eligibility review. The Q4 review consolidates quarterly inputs and calibration outcomes.

3. Rating scale and behavioural descriptors
LogiCore uses a 5-point rating scale. Ratings reflect performance over the review period and behaviours consistent with company core values.
1 Unsatisfactory: Performance fails to meet minimum requirements. Repeated errors, safety or compliance breaches (e.g., ignoring BHP procedures, non-compliance with Prawo Farmaceutyczne where applicable), and inability to perform role duties without constant supervision.
2 Below Expectations: Performance is inconsistent and below role expectations. Requires frequent coaching; targets missed regularly; improvement required to meet operational standards.
3 Meets Expectations: Solid and reliable performance. Completes assigned work on time, complies with procedures (RODO, vehicle logs, UDT-specified maintenance), and contributes to team objectives. Eligible for inflation adjustment at annual review.
4 Exceeds Expectations: Consistently performs above expectations. Takes initiative, improves processes, mentors colleagues, contributes measurable improvements in safety, quality or productivity. Eligible for a pay increase of 3–5% at annual review.
5 Outstanding: Exceptional performance and leadership. Demonstrates sustained impact across the depot or region, innovative problem solving, role-model behaviour demonstrating LogiCore core values. Eligible for a pay increase of 5–8% and possible spot bonus.

4. Goal setting
Goals follow the SMART framework (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound). At the start of the cycle each employee must have a minimum of three individual goals and be assigned at least two team goals. Examples:
- Individual (driver): Reduce late deliveries by 15% in Q2 through route optimisation and weekly vehicle checks.
- Individual (warehouse operator): Increase picking accuracy to 99.5% by end of Q3 through cycle counting and standard work adherence.
- Individual (office/operations): Implement KPI dashboard and reduce invoicing discrepancies by 20% within 6 months.
- Team goals: Depot safety incident rate reduction by 25% year-on-year; improve cross-dock throughput by 10% in Q3.
All goals must include acceptance criteria and measurement metrics, stored in the HR system and referenced during each quarterly check-in.

5. Competency framework
Competencies combine role-specific skills and LogiCore core values. Managers assess both technical competencies and behavioural alignment.
Role-specific competencies:
- Driver: safe driving practices, vehicle maintenance documentation (UDT/technical checks), route planning, customer communication, load securement, compliance with Prawo o ruchu drogowym and transport documentation (CMR when applicable).
- Warehouse operator: picking & packing accuracy, forklift operation (where certified), stock control, adherence to BHP, handling of Dangerous Goods where applicable, knowledge of warehouse systems (WMS).
- Office/administrative/operations: order processing, invoicing accuracy, customer service, use of TMS/ERP systems, regulatory reporting, stakeholder communication.
Core values (LogiCore): Safety first (BHP), Customer focus, Continuous improvement (Kaizen mindset), Integrity & compliance (RODO, legal compliance), Teamwork.

6. Self-assessment and manager assessment
Employees complete a self-assessment in the HR system prior to the formal review meeting. Self-assessment must cover progress on SMART goals, examples of behaviours, development needs and career aspirations. Managers review self-assessments, prepare their evaluation and bring evidence to the review meeting.

7. Calibration sessions
Prior to Q4 finalisation, department heads and HR convene calibration sessions to align ratings, ensure consistency across depots and counter rating drift. Calibration reviews supporting evidence (KPIs, incident reports, customer feedback) and adjust proposed ratings where necessary to maintain fairness and legal defensibility.

8. Performance Improvement Plan (PIP)
- Trigger: A PIP is initiated automatically when an employee receives two consecutive quarterly ratings of 2 or below, or when a single instance raises significant compliance/safety concerns.
- Duration: Standard PIP duration is 90 calendar days.
- Structure: PIP defines clear, measurable targets (linked to SMART criteria), required actions, training resources, and success metrics. Weekly check-ins between employee and manager are mandatory and documented. HR monitors PIP progress and supports access to training and coaching.

9. PIP outcomes
- Successful completion: Employee meets PIP targets within 90 days. Performance returns to normal review cycle and employee status is updated in the HR system. Manager documents outcomes and next steps for sustained development.
- Unsuccessful completion: Failure to meet PIP targets results in HR review. Where termination is recommended, HR follows HR-POL-TERM procedures, consistent with Kodeks Pracy and internal policies, ensuring RODO compliance in handling employee records.

10. Link to compensation
Annual compensation reviews occur in Q4 following the formal review and calibration.
- Rating 3: Inflation-only adjustment (indexation) as defined for the year and local market.
- Rating 4: Recommended base salary increase of 3–5%, subject to budget and employment contract conditions (salary expressed in PLN for internal payroll; external EU contracts in EUR where applicable).
- Rating 5: Recommended base salary increase of 5–8% plus eligibility for a one-time spot bonus at manager discretion and budget availability.
All increases are subject to final approval by Finance and HR and must comply with employment contracts and applicable law.

11. Link to promotion
Promotion eligibility requires sustained high performance: minimum two consecutive years with annual ratings of 4 or higher, demonstrated mastery of role-specific competencies and available role openings. Promotions follow standard recruitment and internal mobility practices.

12. Documentation and data governance
All review documents, self-assessments, ratings, PIP records and calibration notes are stored in the HR system. Records are accessible to the employee, their direct manager and HR. Access respects RODO and internal data retention schedules. Copies for compliance (e.g., safety incidents) may be shared with relevant departments (BHP, Operations).

13. Appeal procedure
Employees who disagree with a formal Q4 rating may request a re-review by the skip-level manager within 14 calendar days of receipt of the written rating. The appeal must state grounds and submit any supporting evidence. The skip-level manager will review documentation, may conduct interviews, and issue a written decision within 14 days. Further escalation follows standard grievance procedures.

14. Roles and responsibilities
- Employee: complete self-assessments, participate in check-ins, meet goals, and engage in development.
- Manager: set SMART goals, document quarterly check-ins, conduct formal reviews, initiate PIP when required, and participate in calibration.
- Department head/Head of Ops: attend calibration sessions, ensure consistency and fairness.
- HR: administer the process, maintain records (RODO), advise on PIP and terminations (HR-POL-TERM), and ensure legal compliance.

15. Review of policy
This policy is reviewed annually or as required by changes in legislation (Kodeks Pracy, RODO) or business needs. Changes are approved by HR Director and communicated to all employees.

Effective date: This policy is effective upon publication and applies to all LogiCore Sp. z o.o. employees unless superseded by collective agreements or statutory requirements.

title: Klauzula informacyjna RODO – Informacja dla pracowników i kandydatów LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Works Council and Trade Union Consultation Procedures — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
Purpose and scope
This procedure sets out LogiCore Sp. z o.o.'s processes for consultation with employee representatives, trade unions and the Rada Pracowników (Works Council) in all operations in Poland and Central Europe. It applies to headquarters (Warszawa) and depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań and complements obligations under Polish law including Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o związkach zawodowych and Ustawa o radach pracowników.

Legal basis
- Kodeks Pracy (Art. 232, Art. 38, Art. 222, Art. 1042 and related provisions)
- Ustawa o związkach zawodowych (trade union rights and participation)
- Ustawa o radach pracowników (establishment, competencies and information rights)
- Ustawa o szczególnych zasadach rozwiązywania stosunków pracy w razie grupowych zwolnień — group redundancy rules
- RODO (data protection) for processing of employee personal data

Current union status at LogiCore
LogiCore recognises the following employee representation bodies at present: NSZZ "Solidarność" — zakładowa organizacja związkowa (registered), and an elected Rada Pracowników at sites where legally constituted. Additional unions or works councils will be recognised upon registration in accordance with Ustawa o związkach zawodowych and Ustawa o radach pracowników.

When consultation is mandatory
1) Individual termination of a union member (Art. 38 KP): Prior to dismissal of an employee who is a trade union representative or member where protected status applies, Management must initiate consultation with the relevant union. Consultation period: minimum 5 business days for written response unless urgent lawful grounds exist.
2) Group redundancies (Ustawa o szczególnych zasadach rozwiązywania stosunków pracy): When dismissals reach thresholds (e.g., 20 or more employees within 30 days at an establishment) the employer must consult trade unions/Rada Pracowników and relevant authorities. Obligatory information to be provided and consultation plan commenced without undue delay.
3) Changes to work regulations (Regulamin Pracy) (Art. 1042 KP): Proposed amendments require prior written consultation with trade unions and Rada Pracowników; company must allow reasonable consultation time and consider proposals.
4) Changes to remuneration rules (Regulamin Wynagradzania): Any material change to pay structures, bonus schemes or seniority rules must be consulted with recognised unions and Rada Pracowników.
5) Introduction of monitoring/CCTV (Art. 222 KP): Implementation of workplace monitoring or CCTV requires prior consultation and information to employees and representatives; data processing must comply with RODO and be registered/justified to PIP where applicable.
6) Changes to working time systems: Introduction or modification of shift patterns, on-call, flexible time, or any system affecting rest periods requires consultation per Kodeks Pracy and existing collective agreements.

Consultation timeline and process
- Notification: Management issues written notice to affected employee representatives/union(s) and Rada Pracowników including scope, rationale, impact assessment and supporting documents.
- Initial meeting: Within 7 business days of notice (5 business days for protected individual dismissal consultations), a joint meeting is convened to discuss proposals and alternatives.
- Written feedback: Representatives provide written comments within 10 business days of the initial meeting (or earlier if statutory period shorter).
- Follow-up: Management considers submissions and responds within 10 business days, documenting reasons for acceptance or rejection of proposals.
- Implementation: Changes are only implemented after completion of the consultation period unless mutually agreed otherwise or immediate operational necessity justified and documented.

Information rights of Rada Pracowników
The Rada Pracowników is entitled to quarterly reporting from Management on employment levels, health & safety (BHP), use of agency labour, training, and planned organisational changes. Reports must be provided in writing, with access to anonymised data where RODO restricts personal data disclosure. Extraordinary reporting is required on any consultation triggers listed above.

Dispute resolution
Where disagreements arise: 1) Good‑faith mediation led by an internal or external mediator; 2) Referral to Komisja Pojednawcza (conciliation commission) as per Kodeks Pracy; 3) If unresolved, parties may bring claims to Sąd Pracy (Labour Court). All stages must be documented and attempts at internal resolution exhausted.

Modification of Zakładowy Układ Zbiorowy Pracy (Collective Agreement)
Any proposal to modify the Zakładowy Układ Zbiorowy Pracy must be tabled in writing to the signatory unions and Rada Pracowników. Negotiations commence within 14 calendar days. Agreements require signatures of authorised union representatives and Management; disputes follow the dispute resolution route above.

Management responsibilities and training
Line managers and HR are responsible for: identifying consultation triggers, notifying representatives, providing full and accurate information, and preserving RODO and confidentiality. LogiCore will provide annual training for managers and employee representatives on consultation rights, collective bargaining basics, Art. 222 and 1042 KP obligations, and RODO compliance. Training records to be maintained by HR (PLN budget line for training costs).

Record keeping and confidentiality
All consultation records, minutes and correspondence will be held for a minimum of 5 years by HR; personal data processed during consultations will comply with RODO and internal privacy policies. CCTV implementation requires separate data protection impact assessment and PSP/UDT notifications where equipment implicates safety systems.

Review
This procedure will be reviewed biennially or following material legal change. For questions contact HR Director, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa.

title: Company Vehicle and Fuel Card Policy — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
1. Purpose and scope
This Company Vehicle and Fuel Card Policy sets out rules for the allocation, use and return of company vehicles and ORLEN Flota fuel cards at LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (headquartered in Warszawa; depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań). The Policy applies across Poland and Central Europe and complements obligations under Kodeks Pracy, RODO, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and applicable taxation (PIT) regulations.

2. Eligibility
Company vehicles and fuel cards may be assigned to: managers, sales personnel, fleet coordinators and on-call maintenance staff. Assignment decisions are made by the Fleet Manager in coordination with HR and the relevant department head.

3. Vehicle categories and intended users
- Passenger cars: allocated primarily to managers and senior staff for business travel and permitted private use within Poland. Engine capacity for PIT purposes is recorded in the vehicle file.
- Commercial vans: allocated to fleet coordinators and on-call maintenance staff for operational tasks and deliveries.
- Company pool vehicles: centrally managed vehicles available for short-term business use; private use is prohibited unless expressly authorised by Fleet Manager.

4. Private use and taxation
Private use of company passenger cars is permitted only within the territory of Poland. Private use is a taxable benefit under PIT rules and is settled as a ryczałt: PLN 250/month for cars up to 1600 cc and PLN 400/month for cars above 1600 cc. The payroll department will apply the ryczałt to the employee's PIT basis in accordance with tax law.

5. Driver obligations
All drivers must:
- Hold and present a valid driving licence appropriate for the vehicle category (B / C / CE as required) and notify HR of any restrictions, suspensions or convictions affecting licence status.
- Report any damage, theft or incident to the Fleet Manager and HR within 24 hours.
- Maintain the vehicle service schedule and present the vehicle for periodic inspections, MOT (Przegląd techniczny) and UDT checks where applicable.
- Keep the vehicle clean and secure; no smoking is permitted in any company vehicle (BHP rule).
- Ensure any dashcam (where fitted) remains operational and not covered or obstructed; tampering with recording equipment is prohibited.
- Comply with RODO when handling personal data recorded in-vehicle (e.g., dashcam footage) and follow Fleet Manager instructions for data access and retention.

6. Fuel card (ORLEN Flota) rules
- Fuel cards are issued by Fleet Manager; card limits: standard limit PLN 1,500/month; field staff (e.g., sales, maintenance with frequent travel) PLN 3,000/month. Limits may be adjusted for exceptional needs with Fleet Manager approval.
- Cards are PIN-protected. PIN must be kept confidential. Sharing PINs or cards is prohibited.
- Personal fuel purchases are strictly prohibited. Fuel cards are for company vehicles and business journeys only. Personal refuelling will be treated as unauthorised use and may result in reclamation of costs, disciplinary measures and reporting to HR.
- Lost or stolen cards must be reported immediately to Fleet Manager and blocked.

7. Accident and collision procedure
In the event of an accident the driver must:
1) Ensure safety of passengers and other road users; move to a safe location if possible.
2) Call emergency services (112) if there are injuries.
3) Take comprehensive photos of vehicles, scene, registration plates, and relevant road signs.
4) Exchange contact, vehicle and insurance details with other involved parties; do not admit fault on the scene.
5) Call the Fleet Manager as soon as practicable.
6) Complete an oświadczenie o zdarzeniu drogowym (collision statement) at the scene if possible.
7) Submit a written report to HR and Fleet Manager within 24 hours, including photos and copies of any police reports.
All personal data collected as part of an incident is processed in accordance with RODO and company data protection procedures.

8. Insurance and deductibles
LogiCore provides OC (liability), AC (comprehensive) and NNW (personal accident) insurance for company vehicles. In at-fault incidents the employee may be liable for an udział własny (deductible) of PLN 1,000. Reimbursement of the deductible and other costs will be handled through payroll or direct recovery as appropriate and may be subject to disciplinary action under Kodeks Pracy if reckless behaviour is established.

9. Return of vehicle on employment termination or reassignment
On termination of employment or reassignment the employee must return: vehicle, keys, fuel card, viaAuto toll device, parking cards and any documentation or accessories. The vehicle must be returned clean and with service records. Failure to return items will be charged at replacement cost and may lead to deductions in line with Kodeks Pracy and company procedures.

10. Alcohol and substance policy
LogiCore has a zero tolerance policy for alcohol or illegal drugs while operating company vehicles. Random testing may be conducted in accordance with Polish law and company occupational health procedures. A positive result or refusal to test will result in immediate removal of driving privileges, disciplinary measures up to termination and potential notification to relevant authorities.

11. Fines, penalties and traffic violations
Employees are personally liable for traffic fines, penalty points and violations incurred during vehicle operation unless a violation is demonstrably caused by a company system error. Unauthorised use of vehicles, fuel cards or toll devices will be treated as misconduct and may lead to financial reclamation and disciplinary action.

12. Misuse and disciplinary measures
Misuse of company vehicles or fuel cards (including private fuel purchases, permit violations, tampering with dashcam, failure to report incidents) will result in recovery of costs, suspension of privileges and disciplinary proceedings under Kodeks Pracy. Repeated or severe breaches may lead to dismissal.

13. Administration and contacts
Fleet administration: Fleet Manager, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa. HR queries: HR Department. Occupational health and testing are coordinated with the company's service providers in compliance with PIP and PSP guidance.

14. Review
This Policy will be reviewed at least annually or upon changes to applicable law (including Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, PIT or RODO). Non-compliance with legal requirements should be reported immediately to HR and Legal.

Approved by: Management Board, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Effective date: [dd.mm.yyyy]
Next review: [dd.mm.yyyy + 12 months]

title: Temperature Monitoring System Technical Specification — SensoGuard TM-400 Deployment for LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
1. Document purpose and scope

This technical specification defines requirements, configuration, integration and operational procedures for the Temperature Monitoring System (TMS) to be deployed across LogiCore Sp. z o.o. facilities (HQ Warszawa; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań). The system monitors all temperature-controlled zones using SensoGuard TM-400 wireless sensors and provides continuous logging, alarm handling, reporting and integrations required for GDP, HACCP and internal QMS compliance.

Applicable regulations and references: EU GDP Annex 9 (temperature mapping & monitoring), Prawo Farmaceutyczne, HACCP requirements, RODO (data protection), Kodeks Pracy (BHP responsibilities), Ustawa o transporcie drogowym (for in-transit considerations). Relevant Polish bodies: Główny Inspektorat Farmaceutyczny (GIF), Państwowa Inspekcja Pracy (PIP), Państwowa Straż Pożarna (PSP), UDT for equipment where applicable.

2. System overview

- Device family: SensoGuard TM-400 wireless temperature sensors.
- Purpose: continuous monitoring of all temperature-controlled zones (pharmaceutical storage, frozen goods, blast freeze, ambient warehouse, offices), with alarm thresholds, audit-ready logs and integrations with WMS and customer APIs (PharmaCorp).
- Top-level architecture: sensors → local gateways → secure MQTT broker → InfluxDB time-series storage → Grafana dashboards. Gateways buffer up to 72 hours offline and automatically resynchronize.

3. Sensor technical specifications (SensoGuard TM-400)

- Measurement range: -40°C to +80°C.
- Typical accuracy: ±0.3°C (factory calibrated at 25°C); worst-case accuracy statement per certificate.
- Resolution: 0.1°C.
- Battery: replaceable; nominal life 3 years under 5-minute logging interval and standard RF duty cycle.
- Ingress protection: IP67 (suitable for cold-room, freezer and wash-down sheltered environments; follow cleaning procedures below).
- Wireless: 2.4 GHz proprietary/LoRaWAN depending on site design (document per-site choice in rollout plan).
- Local status indicators: LED for battery and RF; supports remote health checks via MQTT retained status topics.

4. Zone configuration and targets

System will implement per-zone target setpoints and acceptable ranges. Each sensor is assigned to a zone in the central registry (QMS and inventory of measuring points). Zones and nominal setpoints:

- Pharmaceutical storage (Pharma Storage, depot-specific rooms): nominal 2–8°C. Target reference: 5°C nominal. WARNING threshold: ±1°C from target (i.e., <4°C or >6°C). CRITICAL: ±2°C (i.e., <3°C or >7°C). EMERGENCY: ±3°C or sensor failure (i.e., <2°C or >8°C).

- Frozen goods: nominal -18°C ±2°C. Target reference: -18°C. WARNING: ±1°C (>-19°C or <-17°C). CRITICAL: ±2°C (>-20°C or <-16°C). EMERGENCY: ±3°C or sensor failure (>-21°C or <-15°C).

- Blast freeze: nominal -30°C ±3°C. Target reference: -30°C. WARNING: ±1°C, CRITICAL: ±2°C, EMERGENCY: ±3°C or sensor failure.

- Ambient warehouse: nominal 15–25°C. Target reference: 20°C. Thresholds applied relative to target (WARNING ±1°C; CRITICAL ±2°C; EMERGENCY ±3°C).

- Offices: nominal 20–24°C. Target reference: 22°C. Thresholds as above.

Notes: For ranges specified (e.g., 2–8°C), EMERGENCY thresholds coincide with statutory limit exceedance. All thresholds are configurable per-zone but default to values above to satisfy GDP and HACCP requirements.

5. Data logging and protocols

- Sampling interval: every 5 minutes (configurable centrally; default 5 min to satisfy GDP and HACCP audit evidence).
- Transport: MQTT protocol from gateways to central MQTT broker. MQTT topics follow canonical namespace: logiCore/sensors/{site}/{zone}/{sensor_id}/telemetry.
- Security: TLS 1.2+ for MQTT over TCP; client auth via certificates and mutual TLS; OAuth2 token gating for API access to broker where applicable.
- Storage: InfluxDB (time-series DB) hosted in company private cloud; retention and downsampling policies implemented:
  - Raw 5-min samples retained for 3 years for Pharma-critical zones (GDP requirement for traceability)
  - Aggregated 15-min for 5 years, daily aggregates for 10 years.
- Time synchronization: Gateways maintain NTP sync with corporate NTP servers; timestamps stored in UTC and localized in Grafana views.

6. Alert thresholds and alarm classification

Classification (applied per-zone and per-sensor):
- WARNING: deviation of ±1°C from target. Record in log; notify shift supervisor (SMS). Auto-annotate event in InfluxDB.
- CRITICAL: deviation of ±2°C from target. Immediate notification to warehouse manager and quality manager via SMS + email. Trigger WMS integration (see §9) to place affected stock into auto-quarantine status.
- EMERGENCY: deviation of ±3°C from target, sustained excursion beyond configurable time window (default 10 minutes), or sensor failure (loss of communication >15 minutes / battery critical). Immediate phone call escalation to plant manager and customer notification (phone call to designated customer POC). If fire or safety risk suspected, notify PSP as per site emergency procedures.

All alarm events must be logged with event ID, timestamps, measured values, duration, assigned actions and resolution evidence (corrective actions, temperature stabilization evidence, calibrated instruments used for verification).

7. Notification chain and escalation matrix

- WARNING: immediate SMS to shift supervisor (first-line contact). If no acknowledgement within 15 minutes, escalate to warehouse supervisor (SMS + email).
- CRITICAL: SMS + email to warehouse manager and quality manager. WMS auto-quarantine invoked. If no acknowledgement within 10 minutes, phone call to warehouse manager and SMS to plant manager.
- EMERGENCY: automated phone call to plant manager plus immediate customer phone call (PharmaCorp real-time SLA). Also notify Quality Assurance (QA) and Regulatory Affairs. If physical danger or fire risk, follow BHP emergency procedures and notify PSP.

Messaging channels must comply with RODO: store contact numbers and communication logs in QMS and restrict access to authorized personnel. SMS gateway vendor must sign DPA and meet data minimization obligations.

8. Dashboarding, reporting and audit views

- Visualization: Grafana dashboards with real-time and historical views. Default dashboards per site and per-zone; drill-down to per-sensor views.
- Features: 1-min refresh for real-time; historical range selector; annotations for alarm events; overlay of setpoint ranges; exportable PDF/CSV for audit.
- Predefined reports: daily summary (automated at 06:00 CET), weekly compliance report, monthly temperature mapping summary. Reports stored in QMS and available for GIF/GDP audits.
- User roles: Viewer / Analyst / Admin with RBAC; access via corporate SSO (SAML/OIDC) and audited login per RODO.

9. Integration requirements

- WMS integration: On CRITICAL alarm the TMS must call WMS API (REST, mutual TLS) to move affected batches to Quarantine status and create an incident record. WMS shall return transaction ID which is stored with alarm event.
- Customer API: real-time feed to PharmaCorp as required by GDP. Provide JSON push (HTTPS, mutual TLS) and event webhook for CRITICAL/EMERGENCY events. Data fields include sensor id, zone, timestamp, measured value, alarm classification and corrective action tracking.
- Third-party systems: connect to company ERP for inventory linkage, and to maintenance CMMS to generate maintenance tickets (battery replacement, cleaning, firmware updates).

10. Calibration, maintenance and QA

Calibration:
- Interval: every 6 months for critical zones (Pharma, Frozen, Blast freeze); 12 months for non-critical ambient/office sensors unless site-specific requirements demand otherwise.
- Standard: calibration must be performed against NIST-traceable or equivalent reference standards. Calibration certificates shall include as-found/as-left data, technician name, date, and unique certificate ID.
- Storage: calibration certificates uploaded and archived in QMS (electronic) and linked to sensor asset record. Calibration history retained for inspection (minimum 5 years).

Maintenance:
- Battery replacement schedule: batteries replaced proactively at 30 months or when battery indicator reaches configured threshold (~20% remaining), whichever comes first.
- Sensor cleaning: quarterly cleaning for sensors in dusty/production areas; monthly for cold/contaminated areas; cleaning procedure documented in BHP and QMS (use of approved disinfectants consistent with IP67 rating).
- Firmware: gateway and sensor firmware updated per security policy. Gateways receive staged updates: test environment → pilot site → full rollout. Firmware updates logged in change control and require backup snapshot.
- Health checks: daily automated health check report listing sensor connectivity, battery status, calibration due, and communication latency.

11. Compliance and validation

- GDP Annex 9: annual temperature mapping study of all temperature-controlled areas (conducted by QA), retained evidence in QMS. TMS supports mapping data collection and report export for Annex 9 compliance.
- HACCP: TMS provides continuous monitoring to meet CCP verification requirements. Create and retain corrective action records for deviations.
- Prawo Farmaceutyczne and GIF expectations: maintain traceability, immediate notification of critical excursions affecting pharmaceutical product quality.
- RODO: personnel contact data and event logs stored with access controls; notifications to customers handled per data processing agreements.

12. Network architecture (text description)

Logical flow:
- Edge: SensoGuard TM-400 sensors publish measurements via local wireless to on-site Gateway devices (one per cold-room / per facility as determined by RF planning).
- Gateway: performs local validation, stores buffered telemetry (72-hour capacity), encrypts and forwards messages via MQTT over TLS to the central MQTT broker (hosted in corporate DMZ with hardened firewall and VPN/Direct Connect for site-to-cloud connectivity).
- Broker: central MQTT broker validates client certificates, routes telemetry to downstream consumers and writes retained/bridge messages to InfluxDB writer service.
- Storage: InfluxDB cluster receives time-series writes. Write API employs token-based auth. Retention and continuous queries manage downsampling.
- Visualization: Grafana reads from InfluxDB using read-only credentials (RBAC), presents dashboards to authorized users via SSO.

Security and network controls: sensors and gateways operate on segregated VLANs; gateways have firewall rules limiting outbound endpoints (MQTT broker, NTP, update servers). VPN or dedicated MPLS links used for site connectivity where required by SLA.

13. Disaster recovery and offline handling

- Gateway buffering: gateways buffer up to 72 hours of raw telemetry and event logs locally. On reconnection, gateway performs automatic resync with MQTT broker, preserving original timestamps.
- Data resiliency: InfluxDB backups run nightly with weekly full snapshot to offsite storage (encrypted). Grafana dashboard JSON exports retained in DR repository.
- Failover: MQTT broker and InfluxDB operate in HA configuration. In case of central outage, gateways continue local buffering and provide local web UI for on-site limited monitoring.

14. Testing, commissioning and acceptance criteria

- Pre-deployment: RF survey, sensor placement plan, risk assessment and mapping study baseline.
- Commissioning: each sensor acceptance test includes range verification, accuracy check against calibration reference, connectivity and event trigger test.
- Acceptance: system accepted when 100% of sensors operational for 7 days, alerts triggered and routed correctly, WMS and PharmaCorp API verified, and QA signs off per SOP.

15. Documentation and records

- All procedures (installation, calibration, maintenance, alarm handling) recorded in QMS. Change control and SOPs must reference this technical specification.
- Audit trail: all modifications to thresholds, users and system configuration logged with user ID and timestamp.

16. Responsibilities

- IT (department): deploy/operate MQTT, InfluxDB, Grafana and network components; maintain backups and perform firmware updates.
- QA/Quality Manager: approve zones, calibration schedule, handle regulatory notifications and GDP compliance.
- Warehouse Management: operational response to alarms, execution of quarantine procedures.
- Maintenance: battery replacements, cleaning and physical upkeep of sensors/gateways.

17. Annexes and further work

- Annex A: per-site sensor placement plan (separate document).
- Annex B: MQTT topic and payload schema (JSON schema document).
- Annex C: WMS and PharmaCorp API contract / Swagger definitions.

Document prepared for LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa. For questions contact IT Operations (it@logicore.pl) or Quality (qa@logicore.pl).

title: Fleet GPS Tracking System — Technical Specification for LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
Purpose and scope
This technical specification defines functional and non-functional requirements for the Fleet GPS Tracking System to be deployed by LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, with depots in Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw and Poznan. The system will operate across Poland and Central Europe and will support operations, compliance (RODO, Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Prawo Farmaceutyczne where applicable), and customer integrations. This document addresses hardware, installation, data collection, communications, platform integration, privacy and retention, API, and operational procedures.

1. Hardware device
- Core device: Teltonika FMC130 (LTE Cat 1). Device features used by LogiCore include GPS/GLONASS positioning, 3-axis accelerometer, digital and analog I/O, CAN bus reader, and dual SIM capability. Device firmware must be up to date and managed via Teltonika FOTA. The device is certified for commercial vehicle use and must be configured to match LogiCore data schema.
- Connectivity: LTE Cat 1 primary with fallback to 2G for legacy coverage where required. Dual SIM configuration: Orange (primary) and Play (secondary) for redundancy across Poland and EU corridors.
- Local storage: device configured to allow offline storage of at least 100,000 records. Local buffering must preserve timestamps, sensor state and CAN snapshots for later upload and de-duplication.

2. Installation and mechanical requirements
- Installation must be carried out by LogiCore approved certified technicians. Each installation will be accompanied by an installation record (installer name, certificate number, vehicle registration, VIN, device IMEI, mounting location) stored in the asset management database.
- Mounting: tamper-proof mounting with security screws and a sealed tamper switch connected to device digital input. Device enclosure protected from direct heat sources and water ingress according to vehicle class. For reefer units, sensors must be mounted to provide accurate air/cargo temperature sampling.
- Electrical: direct connection to vehicle CAN bus (ISO 15765 / manufacturer-specific) for fuel level, engine RPM, odometer and diagnostic data. Fuse and ignition sense wired to vehicle electrical harness according to UDT/PIP guidance for safe operation.
- Compliance: installations will respect Kodeks Pracy and RODO obligations regarding employee notification and collective consultation. Rada Pracowników consultation completed in accordance with Art. 22^2 KP. Documentation of consultation maintained with HR.

3. Reporting intervals and event rules
- Normal movement: position and telemetry reporting interval set to 30 seconds while vehicle speed > 5 km/h.
- Stationary: interval set to 5 minutes when vehicle stationary and ignition off (battery conservation mode). For stationary with ignition on (e.g., loading) interval configurable to 60 seconds to support operations visibility.
- Harsh events: on detection of harsh braking, acceleration, cornering or other defined thresholds via accelerometer/CAN, reporting frequency increases to 1 second for a pre-configured window (default 10 seconds) to capture high-fidelity event data.
- Event buffering: while offline, all events retained locally up to 100,000 records; upon reconnection, device auto-syncs to platform in chronological order with retry/back-off policy.

4. Data elements collected
The system will collect the following minimum data attributes per record:
- Timestamp (UTC) and local timezone
- GPS/GLONASS position (lat/lon), HDOP, satellites
- Speed (vehicle GPS speed) and heading
- Altitude
- Fuel level (CAN-derived where available)
- Engine RPM (CAN)
- Odometer (CAN) and cumulative trip distance
- Vehicle VIN, registration number and asset tag
- Door open/close sensor status (digital input)
- Reefer temperature reading(s) (for refrigerated units) with probe ID
- Seatbelt status (where sensor available)
- Harsh event type and vector (braking/acceleration/cornering), accelerometer peak values
- Ignition on/off and driver ID (NFC/RFID or tachograph link)
- Diagnostic trouble codes (DTCs) on request
- Device health (battery voltage, GSM signal, IMEI/SIM state)

All data elements will be tagged with collection method (GPS, CAN, sensor) and confidence score for downstream ingestion.

5. Geofencing and business rules
Geofence types supported and behaviours:
- Customer delivery sites: polygonal geofences created per customer; automatic ETA/arrival and departure notifications to WMS and customer portal. ETA calculation based on live speed, route, and historical travel time for the given corridor.
- Depot boundaries: geofences for depot operations (Katowice, Gdansk, Wroclaw, Poznan and Warszawa HQ) to manage check-in/out, security, and dock allocation.
- Rest areas: geofences for rest area compliance monitoring (driver breaks). Alerts sent to operations for non-compliance per Kodeks Pracy rest rules and driving-time rules under Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.
- Country borders: automatic fence lines along national boundaries to trigger tachograph and customs notification workflows when crossing into non-Poland jurisdictions; integration points for tachograph systems.
- Restricted zones: city center low-emission zones, environmental zones and customer restricted ingress areas with pre-defined penalties and routing restrictions. Alerts issued for breach and route re-planning.

Geofence events generate webhook and platform notifications: enter, exit, dwell, ETA threshold breached.

6. Driver behaviour scoring
Driver scoring algorithm will be applied per trip and aggregated weekly/monthly. Weighting factors:
- Speeding: 30% (based on speed limit dataset per road segment; repeated offences escalated)
- Harsh braking: 25% (count and severity)
- Harsh cornering: 20% (count and severity)
- Idling: 15% (minutes of ignition-on stationary vs. efficient idling thresholds)
- Seatbelt: 10% (instances of seatbelt off while moving)

Score formula: normalized sub-scores per category converted to 0-100 then weighted to compute final driver score. Thresholds for coaching, HR escalation and safety incentives are configurable via LogiCore Fleet Portal.

7. Privacy, employee rights and modes
- Data minimization and lawful basis: data collection limited to operational purposes, safety, legal compliance and contract performance. RODO assessments (DPIA) have been completed; data retention and processing conform to RODO obligations and internal records.
- Data retention: all personal-level tracking retained for 2 years as per RODO assessment and business requirement. After the 2-year retention period records are anonymized (irreversibly removed personal identifiers such as driver ID, tachograph link) and kept as aggregate operational metrics unless legal hold is required.
- Driver privacy mode: each vehicle equipped with a driver privacy mode button to be used during documented personal use hours. In privacy mode the device records only start/stop timestamps and vehicle-level odometer totals; precise position traces and CAN-derived personal data are suppressed. Privacy mode activation and duration are logged and subject to HR policy. Abuse of privacy mode will be auditable.
- Consultation: consultation with Rada Pracowników completed in accordance with Art. 22^2 Kodeks Pracy; records of consultation and adopted safeguards are maintained by HR and Legal.

8. Platform architecture and integrations
- Device management and firmware: Teltonika FOTA used for centralized firmware distribution and configuration. FOTA updates scheduled during off-peak hours with roll-back capability.
- Core portal: LogiCore Fleet Portal — custom dashboard built in Next.js for real-time monitoring, alerts, analytics, driver management, and integrations. Portal hosted on secure infrastructure with HTTPS, WAF, and role-based access (RBAC).
- WMS integration: bi-directional integration for real-time ETA updates and POD transfer. ETAs pushed to WMS via REST and webhooks.
- Customer portal: public-facing shipment tracking with limited visibility (shipment-level ETA and status). No raw driver position exposed.
- Langfuse: API monitoring and observability integration to track API performance and errors for all external endpoints.
- SAP: trip and cost allocation export to SAP for fuel, tolls and per-trip cost accounting. Transfer via secure API or SFTP with CSV/IDoc formats as required.

9. API and events
- REST API: standardized REST API endpoints for vehicle status, trip history, geofence management, and driver scoring. Responses JSON; pagination and filtering supported. Rate limits enforced per key.
- Webhooks: event-driven notifications for geofence enter/exit, ETA, harsh events, DTCs, device offline/online, privacy mode toggled. Destination endpoints configurable per customer with retry/back-off.
- Authentication: API key based credentials plus IP whitelist per client. Option for mutual TLS (mTLS) available for customers requiring elevated security.
- Audit logging: all API accesses are logged for at least 2 years for compliance review.

10. Resilience, security and backups
- Network redundancy: dual SIM (Orange + Play) for cellular redundancy across Poland and EU corridors. APN configurations segregated for management plane vs telemetry.
- Offline resilience: local device storage capacity of 100,000 records with auto-sync on network reconnection. Data integrity ensured via sequence numbers and checksums.
- Security: device-level secure boot and encrypted storage where supported. TLS 1.2+ for all communications. Keys managed by central KMS and rotated periodically.
- Backups: platform backups daily with geo-replication. Disaster recovery RTO and RPO documented in corporate DR plan.

11. Compliance and operational procedures
- Legal and regulatory: system operation follows RODO, Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, and Prawo Farmaceutyczne for temperature-controlled shipments. Interfaces for PSP, PIP and GIF reporting available on request for incident reporting.
- Maintenance: scheduled maintenance windows announced 72 hours in advance. Firmware and configuration changes follow change control and rollback procedures.
- Training: driver and technician training programs established for device use, privacy mode, and safety incentives. Installation technicians must present certification; records stored in asset management.

12. Acceptance criteria
- All Teltonika FMC130 devices successfully installed on pilot fleet with verified CAN telemetry, GPS accuracy within 5 meters, and offline buffering of at least 100k records.
- Geofence events trigger correct webhooks and ETA matches WMS within SLA (±10 minutes for regional routes).
- Driver privacy mode suppresses position visibility while logging start/stop and duration.
- API endpoints respond within agreed SLAs and Langfuse displays <2% error budget.

Appendices
- Appendix A: device parameter templates, CAN PID mapping per manufacturer, and installation checklist.
- Appendix B: privacy DPIA summary and Rada Pracowników consultation record.
- Appendix C: escalation and contact matrix for cross-border operations.

Prepared by: IT — LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa
Date: (document control maintained in ECM)

title: WMS Barcode Scanning Workflow — Technical Specification for LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
Purpose
This technical specification defines the barcode scanning workflows, hardware, integrations and error handling for the LogiCore Sp. z o.o. WMS (Warsaw HQ, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań). The objective is to standardise receiving, putaway, picking and shipping operations across Poland and Central Europe, ensuring compliance with RODO, Kodeks Pracy, Prawo Farmaceutyczne (where applicable), GDP requirements and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.

Scope
Applies to all warehouse personnel, IT, BHP and QA teams using handheld scanners and label printers in LogiCore operational sites. Covers scanner hardware selection, supported symbologies, step-by-step workflows for Receiving, Picking and Shipping, printer integration, offline behaviour, performance targets, and escalation rules with relevant Polish authorities (GIF, PSP, PIP, UDT) where required.

Hardware
- Primary handheld: Zebra TC52 (Android) with integrated 2D imager. Key specs: rugged IP67, 1.8 m drop rating, battery life ~14 hours (typical mixed-use). Device management via MDM (recommended: SOTI or Zebra StageNow + EMM). Accessories: vehicle mounts, multi-bay charging.
- Label printers: Zebra ZT411 (industrial) for 24/7 print stations; Zebra ZD421 (desktop) for packing benches. Templates implemented in ZPL. Printer integration via network (Ethernet/Wi‑Fi) and WMS print service.

Supported barcode formats
- GS1-128 (logistics labels, SSCC, GSRN elements)
- EAN-13 (retail units)
- QR Code (internal tracking, URLs, batch links)
- Data Matrix (pharmaceutical serialization pursuant to Prawo Farmaceutyczne and FMD/GDPR considerations)
All scanners must support multi-symbology decoding; WMS validates parsed GS1 element strings and checks digit where applicable.

Receiving workflow (detailed)
1) Dock assignment scan: Operator scans dock QR code or NFC tag to register arrival and assign dock to PO. WMS logs timestamp and operator ID (in accordance with RODO minimal data retention).
2) Trailer seal verification: Scan trailer seal barcode (if present) and capture seal number. If seal missing, trigger deviation report to QA and security.
3) CMR/document scan: Scan CMR or transport document QR/DataMatrix or photograph full CMR. OCR-assisted parsing allowed; scanned copy attached to inbound shipment record.
4) Pallet label scan (GS1-128 SSCC): Scan each pallet-level SSCC label. WMS verifies SSCC uniqueness and links to the inbound PO.
5) Quantity verification: For each SSCC or line, operator enters or scans carton/inner pack EAN-13 and confirms counted quantity. System compares count vs delivery note and flags variance > tolerance.
6) Quality sampling trigger: Default sampling rule 1:20 (one sample per 20 pallets) for general cargo. For pharmaceutical products subject to GDP/Prawo Farmaceutyczne, 100% sampling and quarantine until QA release. Sampling triggers a QA task, label for sampled unit and storage in quarantine location.
7) Damage photo capture: If damage observed, operator captures photos using TC52 camera; images attached to inbound record and forwarded to QA and claims team. PSP/fire documentation if hazardous.
8) WMS putaway assignment: After verification and QA clearance, WMS issues putaway location(s). Operator scans destination bin/location and SSCC to confirm putaway.

Pick workflow
1) Wave assignment: WMS issues pick waves to operators; operator scans wave QR at terminal to start.
2) Location scan: Operator scans source location barcode to confirm they are at the correct pick face.
3) Item scan: Scan item barcode (EAN-13, Data Matrix or internal SKU label). WMS verifies SKU/lot/expiry (for pharma) and allocation.
4) Quantity confirmation: Enter picked quantity; scanner verifies against pick list and decrements inventory in real time or queues in offline mode.
5) Bin scan (destination): Scan destination carton/label or staging bin barcode (for multi-line consolidation).
6) Short pick procedure: If stock is short, operator flags short pick on device. WMS will auto-reallocate picks from nearest replenishment location, create exception record and notify supervisor/warehouse planner. Short picks for GDP-regulated goods require immediate QA notification.

Shipping workflow
1) Order consolidation scan: Consolidator scans separate pick cartons or pallet SSCCs to build a shipment manifest in WMS.
2) Weight verification: Integrated scale verifies shipment weight. Accepted tolerance ±2%. If outside tolerance, trigger recount and supervisor alert.
3) Label print and apply: Print transport/retail labels via ZT411/ZD421 using ZPL templates. Operator scans applied label to confirm successful print and application.
4) Dock assignment scan: Scan assigned dock to record staging location and carrier assignment.
5) Trailer load scan: Scan each pallet SSCC as loaded into trailer. WMS verifies against manifest, locks loaded items and records load order.
6) Departure confirmation: Scan trailer seal (if applied), record departure time, driver ID and upload CMR/eCMR scans. Notify transport team and update carrier EDI if configured.

Error handling and escalation
- Unknown barcode: If scanner cannot decode or WMS does not recognise code, device presents manual entry screen (alphanumeric) and requires operator comment. Simultaneously create alert to supervisor and log event with operator ID. Supervisor action logged per Kodeks Pracy/RODO rules.
- Mismatched SKU: If scanned barcode does not match expected SKU, audible alarm generated on device, operation blocked. Requires supervisor override with reason and sign-off. All overrides require traceability and are subject to audit.
- Damaged label: If label unreadable, follow reprint workflow: operator captures photo, selects "Reprint Label", WMS generates new ZPL template print job (ZT411 or ZD421), prints replacement label, affixes to item and logs replacement reason. Old barcode must be decommissioned to avoid duplication.

Label printer integration
- Printers: Zebra ZT411 (industrial) and ZD421 (desktop). All templates built and stored as ZPL. WMS print service exposes REST API endpoints for print jobs, includes printer selection, label template name, dynamic fields (SSCC, GTIN, lot, expiry, shipment ID) and print quantity.
- Print confirmation: Scanner receives confirmation event from print service and requires operator scan of printed label to complete step.

Offline mode
- TC52 devices must support an offline scan queue storing up to 500 transactions (scans, quantities, photos). Queue persisted on-device encrypted storage per RODO.
- Auto-sync: On Wi‑Fi reconnect, device auto-syncs queue to WMS. Synchronisation order preserved (FIFO). Device retries with exponential backoff on transient errors.
- Conflict resolution: If server-side inventory changed during offline period, WMS will attempt auto-merge: accepted quantities up to available stock. Conflicts (over picks, duplicate SSCC) create "Conflict" exception record and are flagged for manual reconciliation by supervisor. For pharma/GDP goods, any conflict forces quarantine and QA review prior to release.

Performance targets
- Scan-to-response latency: <200 ms (round-trip) under normal LAN (Wi‑Fi 802.11ac) conditions.
- Picks per hour target: 120 picks/operator/hour (measured as average over shift) — based on standard pick face ergonomics and travel time assumptions. Target requires device firmware and WMS UI optimisations.
- Scan accuracy: >99.8% successful decode-to-accept rate. Maintain firmware updates and scanner optics cleaning schedule in BHP plan.

Security, Compliance and Logging
- All scan and user activity logs retained according to RODO and internal retention policy. Sensitive personal data minimised.
- Pharma handling compliant with Prawo Farmaceutyczne and GDP; serialisation data managed per regulations and available for GIF audits.
- Transport events logged per Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and CMR/eCMR requirements.

Implementation and Test Plan
- Phased rollout by depot: pilot in Katowice for 4 weeks, then Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań, final Warsaw HQ integration. Test cases include negative barcode scenarios, offline sync conflicts, sampling triggers and QA escalation.
- Acceptance criteria: all workflows execute with no critical defects, performance targets met in production Wi‑Fi, end-to-end print/scan cycle validated.

Responsibilities and Change Control
- IT (WMS team): implement scanner app, ZPL templates, backend sync rules and monitoring.
- Operations/Warehouse Manager: define sampling rules, training and BHP upkeep.
- QA: define pharma-specific flows and acceptance sampling.
- Change control: all changes via CAB; record per internal SOP and reference to Kodeks Pracy where role changes affect access rights.

Revision history: v1.0 initial release. Approved by IT Head and QA Manager.

title: Network Infrastructure Technical Specification — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
Purpose and scope
This document defines the Network Infrastructure Technical Specification for LogiCore Sp. z o.o. Headquarters: Warszawa; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań. The specification covers LAN, WLAN, WAN, Internet connectivity, network security, monitoring, physical requirements, and vendor support. It applies to all production warehouses and related IT assets operated by LogiCore across Poland and Central Europe and must be followed by IT, Operations, and contracted vendors.

Regulatory and compliance notes
All network and monitoring activities must comply with RODO (GDPR) for personal data processing, Prawo Farmaceutyczne and Główny Inspektorat Farmaceutyczny (GIF) requirements where pharmaceutical goods are handled (temperature and access logs), and applicable workplace regulations (Kodeks Pracy, BHP). CCTV deployment and retention periods must be documented according to RODO and local PIP guidance. Critical infrastructure physical protections must follow PSP recommendations for fire safety.

Overall design principles
- High availability and redundancy at core and WAN layers.
- Clear segmentation (VLAN) between OT, corporate, IoT, guest and management traffic.
- Centralized authentication and policy enforcement (NAC) with per-site enforcement points.
- Fast failover for links and devices to meet production SLA.
- Monitoring, alerting and documented runbooks for site IT teams and central NOC.

Wireless (WLAN)
- Hardware: Cisco Catalyst 9130 Access Points (Wi‑Fi 6 / 802.11ax).
- Coverage target: 99.8% geometric coverage with minimum received signal level -67 dBm in all operational warehouse areas (picking lanes, packing, staging, offices). Site surveys (predictive + on-site verification) required for each depot prior to installation.
- Capacity: design for worst-case concurrency of handheld scanners, mobile printers and operator devices with roaming support (fast BSS transition).
- PoE: all APs powered via PoE+ from access switches.
- SSIDs and segmentation: corporate (802.1X), OT WiFi for scanners (MAB or certificate as appropriate), guest captive portal limited to VLAN40, management SSID bound to VLAN50 and restricted by MAC/802.1X.

VLAN segmentation and addressing
Logical VLAN segmentation to minimize broadcast domains and apply security policies:
- VLAN 10 — OT (scanners, label printers, barcode devices)
- VLAN 20 — Corporate IT (workstations, laptops, admin systems)
- VLAN 30 — IoT (temperature sensors, environmental sensors, CCTV cameras)
- VLAN 40 — Guest (internet-only, captive portal)
- VLAN 50 — Management (network devices, consoles, NMS)
IP addressing scheme: 10.10.x.0/24 per VLAN per building. Third octet (x) mapping:
- Warszawa (WAR): VLAN10=10 , VLAN20=11 , VLAN30=12 , VLAN40=13 , VLAN50=14 -> e.g. OT WAR = 10.10.10.0/24
- Katowice (KTW): VLAN10=20, VLAN20=21, VLAN30=22, VLAN40=23, VLAN50=24
- Gdańsk (GDN): VLAN10=30, VLAN20=31, VLAN30=32, VLAN40=33, VLAN50=34
- Wrocław (WRO): VLAN10=40, VLAN20=41, VLAN30=42, VLAN40=43, VLAN50=44
- Poznań (POZ): VLAN10=50, VLAN20=51, VLAN30=52, VLAN40=53, VLAN50=54
Gateway addressing: .1 reserved for HSRP active, .2 for standby, .10-.50 reserved for infrastructure. DHCP scopes managed centrally with reservations for infrastructure and critical OT devices.

Network topology
- Core: redundant Cisco Catalyst 9300 stack at each site (stack/pair) forming the distribution/core layer for the warehouse. Stack configured for Layer 3 with HSRP for gateway redundancy.
- Distribution: modular distribution to each warehouse zone (receiving, storage, picking, packing) over fiber or aggregate copper. Redundant uplinks to core with LACP.
- Access: access layer switches supply PoE+ for cameras and APs; access layer designed for 1:1 or 1:2 oversubscription depending on zone requirements.
- Cabling: structured cabling (Cat6A for 10G where required, singlemode/multimode fiber between main closets and zone switches). All closet IDs documented in CMDB.

Power and physical environment
- Locked network closets for each site with controlled access (badge access, logging). Access lists must be maintained in HR/IT.
- Environmental monitoring: temperature & humidity sensors integrated into NMS and sent to PRTG; alarms for thresholds applicable to Prawo Farmaceutyczne for pharma storage zones.
- UPS on core and distribution: minimum 30 minutes runtime at full infrastructure load to allow orderly shutdown; service-tested quarterly.
- Fire suppression & PSP guidance: closets must comply with local fire/safety codes and PSP recommendations.

Internet and WAN
- Internet: dual-ISP model. Primary: Orange Business 500/100 Mbps. Backup: Polkomtel (Plus) 200/50 Mbps. Automatic failover managed by Cisco SD‑WAN edge devices with policy-based route preference and health probing.
- WAN between depots: MPLS (private) mesh/providers connecting Warszawa–Katowice–Gdańsk–Wrocław–Poznań with guaranteed 100 Mbps per site. MPLS used for latency-sensitive application traffic and secure transport between sites.
- SD-WAN overlay used for internet breakout and dynamic path selection. Failover SLAs: ISP failure → SD‑WAN route optimization and failover within 10 seconds.

Security
- Firewall: Palo Alto PA‑450 at each site for perimeter control and application filtering. Central management via Panorama for rule distribution.
- DNS security: Cisco Umbrella for recursive DNS filtering and threat intelligence.
- NAC: Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) for Network Access Control. Authentication model:
  - Corporate endpoints: 802.1X (EAP-TLS where possible) with device certificates and AD integration.
  - OT devices (scanners, printers without 802.1X support): MAB (MAC Authentication Bypass) with inventory and strict profiling. OT devices mapped to VLAN10 with ACLs preventing lateral access to Corporate VLAN20.
- Microsegmentation: apply ACLs and firewall rules between VLANs; management VLAN50 accessible only from authorized jump hosts.
- Logging & SIEM: logs forwarded to central log collector; retention policies set per RODO and company policy for auditability.

Monitoring and alerting
- PRTG Network Monitor deployed centrally with distributed probes at each site. Metrics: device availability, interface throughput, PoE usage, AP counts, RF health, temperature sensors, UPS status.
- Alerting: critical alerts to IT NOC via SMS + Slack integration; escalation matrix defined in runbooks. Dashboards per site available to IT and Operations managers.
- Reporting: monthly SLA reports, network utilization, and security incident summaries.

Availability and SLA
- Production network SLA: 99.9% uptime (maximum allowed downtime 43.8 minutes per calendar month) for core network and WAN used for operational systems.
- Redundancy mechanisms: HSRP on gateway (automatic failover within 3 seconds), redundant core stack, dual ISP with SD‑WAN.

Failover and incident procedures (summary)
- Primary core switch failure: HSRP takes effect; standby becomes active within 3 seconds. Notify NOC and dispatch field engineer per SmartNet.
- Site power loss: UPS provides 30 minutes runtime; safe shutdown of noncritical systems initiated after warning threshold; critical OT systems remain on battery-backed power.
- ISP outage: SD‑WAN detects path failure and reroutes traffic to backup link; expected convergence within 10 seconds. If both ISPs fail, failover to MPLS for inter-site traffic where policy allows.
- Runbooks: each site maintains runbooks for network restore, contact lists, and escalation (stored in CMDB and available offline).

Vendor support and lifecycle
- Core: Cisco SmartNet 24x7x4 for Cisco Catalyst 9300 stack (hardware replacement SLA 4 hours, remote support 24x7).
- Access layer: NBD (Next Business Day) hardware replacement for access switches.
- APs: covered under SmartNet or equivalent; spare APs (5% of deployed) maintained on-site or at nearest depot.
- Firewall support: Palo Alto support contract with 24x7 ticketing and 8x5 telephone support plus critical incident escalation.

Change control and documentation
- All network changes must be requested via IT Change Management, with CAB approval for production changes. Critical windows must be coordinated with Operations to avoid disruption to warehouse activities (picking/shipping).
- Inventory and configuration backups: CMDB entries for all devices, nightly configuration backups stored off-site encrypted in line with RODO requirements.

Appendix
- Site codes, VLAN mapping table, contact list, and detailed IP plan are maintained as annexes in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) and updated with each site change.

Approval
Prepared by: IT Infrastructure Team, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa.
Approved by: Head of IT and COO. Date and signature recorded in document control.

title: Transport Order Lifecycle — SAP to Delivery Technical Specification
text:
1. Purpose and scope
This document describes the end-to-end transport order lifecycle for LogiCore Sp. z o.o., covering Customer Order → SAP SD → Transport Order → WMS pick → Driver App → Loading → GPS tracking → Delivery → POD → SAP billing. It defines system interactions (SAP, WMS, Driver App, GPS), integration formats (IDoc ORDERS05, DESADV, INVOIC; RFCs), error handling, performance targets, regulatory considerations (RODO, Kodeks Pracy, Prawo Farmaceutyczne, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym), and operational procedures for depots in Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań and Central Europe runs.

2. High-level end-to-end flow
- Customer Order: Customer places order via EDI/portal. Order acknowledged to customer; order lands in SAP Sales & Distribution (SD) module as sales order. IDoc ORDERS05 inbound is used for EDI orders.
- SAP SD: Validation (credit, product availability via RFC), pricing, delivery scheduling. If RFC stock check fails, order is held and visible to Order Management queue.
- Transport Order: SAP creates Transport Order (TO) with planned route, vehicle requirements, temperature constraints (if any). TO exported to Transport Execution System (TES) and WMS.
- WMS pick: TO triggers pick wave, bin allocation, cartonisation, label generation (SSCC) and pallet build. Pick confirmation and pallet labels generated in WMS.
- Driver App: Driver receives assigned run via Driver App (React Native). App shows sequence, navigation (Google Maps), consignment details, special instructions (Prawo Farmaceutyczne requirements), temperature setpoints.
- Loading: WMS scan confirms loaded SKUs to SSCC. WMS sends loading confirmation to SAP to post Goods Issue (GI). Driver App receives notification to depart.
- GPS tracking: Vehicle sends real-time GPS updates per TECH-SPEC-002. ETA recalculation happens every 5 minutes. Customer notifications at 30/15/5 minutes pre-arrival automatically triggered.
- Delivery: Driver completes delivery in App (signature capture, photo POD, temperature log upload). App pushes POD and DESADV IDoc to SAP. WMS updates inventory and Customer posts Goods Receipt (GR) in SAP if integrative process configured.
- Billing: SAP generates invoice (IDoc INVOIC) upon confirmed delivery and GR. Invoices to EU customers issued in EUR; internal cost reports remain in PLN.

3. SAP integration details
- Inbound Orders: IDoc ORDERS05 inbound mapping to SAP SD Sales Order (VBAP). Mandatory segments: E1EDK01 (Document header), E1EDP01 (items), partner segments (E1EDKA1), E1EDK05 (dates). Validate VAT ID (EU reverse charge) and shipping conditions.
- Outbound Despatch Advice: IDoc DESADV generated after successful loading and WMS confirmation — maps SSCC (E1EDP19), pallet-level GTINs, batch/lot (MHD, if pharmaceutical), temperature logs attached as BINARY or referenced URI for RODO/minimisation.
- Outbound Invoice: IDoc INVOIC sent once delivery confirmed and GR (if required) posted in SAP. Tax logic per EU cross-border rules; currency EUR for external invoices. Internal accruals and costing recorded in PLN.
- RFCs: Real-time RFC Get_Stock_Availability and Get_Batch_Info called during order creation and prior to pick wave to ensure allocation. RFC responses must be sub-second in normal load; timeouts configured at 5s.
- ALE and EDI middleware: All IDocs pass through the middleware (SAP PO/PI or equivalent). Middleware responsible for transformation, enrichment and routing to external endpoints (WMS, customer EDI endpoints).

4. WMS interaction
- Trigger: Transport Order message (via RFC/IDoc or REST API) triggers WMS to create pick wave for associated sales orders and allocate bins.
- Pick Wave: WMS performs wave selection, assigns pickers, generates picklists and pack instructions. System supports split-pick for partial deliveries.
- Bin Allocation: WMS reserves bins/locations; must return SSCC and bin allocation to SAP for traceability.
- Labels: WMS generates SSCC pallet labels, parcel labels, and bespoke pharma cold-chain labels. Label templates maintained centrally.
- Loading Confirmation: WMS confirms loaded SSCCs via API which triggers SAP Goods Issue (MB51/MB1A logic through IDoc or RFC) and notifies Driver App.

5. Driver App architecture and features
- Tech stack: React Native for cross-platform iOS/Android with native modules for camera/OTG printer and secure storage.
- Assigned runs: App receives assigned run payload (JSON) containing stop sequence, order references, allowed re-route windows, temperature constraints, and special instructions (GIF notices for pharmaceuticals).
- Navigation: Integration with Google Maps SDK for routing and live traffic. App requests geolocation (RODO compliance) only for operational purposes; consent recorded.
- Delivery confirmation: Signature capture (vector), photo POD (jpeg with EXIF timestamp removed per RODO), barcode/QR scanning of SSCC, and temperature log upload (CSV or JSON). Temperature samples are stored with hash and optionally uploaded to SAP via REST or placed on WMS repository referenced in DESADV.
- Offline mode: App stores events locally and retries sync when connectivity restored. Conflicts resolved by timestamp; driver must confirm if mismatch.

6. Loading confirmation and SAP goods issue
- Process: WMS scan of SSCC triggers Loading Confirmation event. Middleware performs IDoc DESADV and RFC to SAP to post Goods Issue (VL02N/VL10 processes). A successful GI changes stock (MB05) and updates SAP status to 'Dispatched'.
- Notification: Upon GI, Driver App receives 'Ready to depart' and customer receives estimated arrival notifications.

7. GPS tracking and ETA
- Telemetry: Vehicle transmits position every 30s; heartbeat every 60s. Position ingestion follows TECH-SPEC-002 transport telemetry schema.
- ETA: ETA recalculated every 5 minutes or on route deviation >500m. Backend recalculation uses live traffic from Google and vehicle speed patterns.
- Customer notifications: Automated emails/SMS/push at 30, 15 and 5 minutes before arrival. Notifications include DO/SSCC summary. Customers may opt-out under RODO; opt-in is recorded.

8. Delivery, POD and billing
- Driver confirms delivery in App: App transmits POD (signature + photos + temperature log) to middleware and WMS. WMS marks consignee acceptance and returns confirmation.
- SAP Goods Receipt: For customers configured with GR workflow, WMS triggers SAP Goods Receipt at customer, or customer EDI may post GR which triggers invoice release.
- Invoice: Triggered by SAP upon DESADV+POD confirmation and GR (if applicable). INVOIC IDoc sent to customer EDI endpoint and accounting systems. External customer invoices in EUR.

9. Exception handling and workflows
- Partial delivery (split shipment): Driver app allows partial quantities. WMS updates remaining quantities and creates follow-up transport order. SAP splits sales order lines and posts partial GI; billing for partial deliveries per contract.
- Refused delivery: Driver captures refusal code, evidence (photo, signature), and reasons. Consignment marked 'refused' in WMS and SAP; return process initiated. Return Order created in SAP (RET), TO for return run scheduled.
- Temperature breach: Immediate quarantine workflow. App flags breach, uploads time-stamped temperature file, notifies Dispatch and Quality (GIF/Prawo Farmaceutyczne requirements). Cargo is quarantined at nearest depot; SAP batch status set to 'Blocked'. Quality team performs disposition within SLA.
- Address change (re-routing): Driver app supports address update with validation. Middleware recalculates route and checks regulatory constraints (e.g., cross-border permissions). Reassignment or surcharge handled per tariff.
- Vehicle breakdown: Driver marks vehicle as disabled. Dispatch reassigns remaining stops to another vehicle. Reassignment triggers new TO, WMS re-allocation and updated ETAs. Driver labour time/pauses are captured per Kodeks Pracy rules.

10. Message retry, dead letter and manual review
- Retry policy: Synchronous messages: 3 attempts with exponential backoff: 1s, 5s, 25s. Asynchronous messages: middleware queues with same retry pattern.
- Dead letter: After 3 failed attempts, message routed to Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) with full trace and payload. Automatic alert sent to Integration Team.
- Manual review: Integration team must resolve DLQ items within 4 hours (business hours SLAs). If unresolved, escalation to IT Manager and Ops (Dispatch) occurs.

11. Error states and resolution procedures
- SAP ORDERS05 inbound failure: Log in middleware, notify Order Management, attempt RFC stock check manually. If mapping error, developer fixes and reprocesses IDoc. If data error (missing VAT/partner), hold and notify customer-sales.
