- RFC stock timeout: RFC timeout 5s. On timeout, system places order in 'AVAILABILITY HOLD' queue. Auto-retry every 10 minutes for 3 attempts; manual check after 30 minutes.
- WMS allocation failure: WMS returns allocation error — middleware notifies Warehouse Supervisor with failed SKUs and suggested bins. If no allocation, TO remains 'Pending Pick'. SLA 2 hours to resolve before customer notification.
- Loading/GI mismatch: If WMS loaded SSCC but SAP GI fails, middleware retries. If persistent, put SSCC on hold and create incident. Dock supervisor performs manual reconciliation.
- Driver App offline: Events stored locally; sync attempted every 60s. If offline >30 minutes, alert Dispatch to call driver. Critical POD must be recovered within 4 hours.
- DESADV/INVOIC outbound failure: Same retry logic; DLQ escalation. Billing team notified if INVOIC not delivered within 24 hours.

12. Performance targets and SLAs
- Order-to-dispatch: Target <4 hours from Customer Order receipt to vehicle departure (goods issue), except where customer-specific lead times apply.
- Event visibility: System must reflect any event (loading, GPS, POD) within 30 seconds of occurrence in the centralized Event Bus/dashboard.
- Message processing: IDoc processing average <10s; RFC stock check sub-second; middleware SLA 99.5% uptime.

13. Data model (diagram description)
Entities and primary relationships (textual diagram):
- Customer (KUNNR) 1..* Orders (VBELN) — Sales Order lines (VBAP) 1..* Products (MATNR)
- Orders 1..* Deliveries (LIKP) — Delivery Items (LIPS) linked to SSCC/Pallet (SSCC table)
- Delivery 1..1 Transport Order (TO_ID) — TO contains Vehicle, Driver, Route, Stops
- Transport Order 1..* Stops — each Stop links to one or more Deliveries
- WMS_Pick (PICK_ID) linked to SSCC and Bin allocations (BIN_ID)
- Vehicle (VEH_ID) linked to GPS_Trace (timestamp, lat, lon, speed)
- POD (POD_ID) linked to Delivery, contains Signature, Photos, Temperature Logs (temp_samples[])
- Invoice (INVOICE_ID) linked to Sales Order(s) and Delivery(ies)
All entities include audit fields (created_by, created_at, updated_by, updated_at) and GDPR flags for personal data minimisation.

14. Security, privacy and regulatory compliance
- RODO: Only required personal data collected in Driver App and retained per retention policy. Photo PODs have EXIF removed; signature vectors stored encrypted at rest. Data subject access procedures defined.
- Prawo Farmaceutyczne & GIF: Cold-chain and batch traceability for pharmaceuticals must comply. Temperature logs and batch numbers preserved for minimum retention as per law.
- Ustawa o transporcie drogowym & Kodeks Pracy: Driver duty times, breaks and vehicle weight limits enforced; telematics used only for compliance and safety.
- UDT/PIP/PSP: Operational safety, loading equipment and fire safety notices maintained in depot SOPs.

15. Monitoring, alerting and operational runbook
- Central dashboard: Real-time events, DLQ monitor, ETA deviations, temperature breaches. Alerts configured for critical events (refused delivery, temperature breach, DLQ, driver offline >30m).
- Runbook: Step-by-step resolution per error state, contact lists (IT integration, Warehouse Supervisor, Dispatch, Quality), escalation windows (30m for critical incidents; 4h for DLQ).

16. Appendix and references
- TECH-SPEC-002: Telemetry and GPS schema reference for position updates and heartbeat.
- Legal references: RODO, Kodeks Pracy, Prawo Farmaceutyczne, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.
- Message formats: ORDERS05, DESADV, INVOIC segment mappings (refer to Integration Appendix A).

Revision history
- Version 1.0 — Initial release.

Approved by: IT Director, Operations Manager, Quality (GIF/Prawo Farmaceutyczne compliance)

Operational notes: External invoices are issued in EUR; internal reporting and depot cost accounting remain in PLN. All middleware logs are retained per retention policy and available for PIP/UDT audits when required.

title: Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity Plan — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. IT Systems
text:
1. Purpose and Scope
This Disaster Recovery (DR) and Business Continuity Plan (BCP) documents LogiCore Sp. z o.o. IT strategy for recovery of production systems following incidents that impact availability, integrity or confidentiality. Scope covers all production IT systems used in warehouse and transport operations across Poland and Central Europe: WMS (Warehouse Management System), ERP, corporate email, fleet tracking (GPS/telematics), temperature monitoring (cold chain sensors), and enterprise telephony (VoIP). This plan is maintained by IT (Dział IT) and aligned with ISO 22301 and RODO Art. 32 requirements for security of processing.

2. Objectives and Targets
Primary objectives are to restore critical services within agreed RPO/RTO targets and to ensure continuity of logistics operations across depots (Warszawa HQ, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań).
- Tier 1 (Critical): WMS, Fleet tracking
  - RPO: 1 hour
  - RTO: 4 hours
- Tier 2 (Business-critical): ERP, Email
  - RPO: 4 hours
  - RTO: 8 hours
- Tier 3 (Non-critical / Support): Reporting, Development environments
  - RPO: 24 hours
  - RTO: 48 hours
On-call staffing and working time for incident response follow Kodeks Pracy rules and internal HR policies.

3. Backup and Replication Strategy
Backup schedule and technology:
- Tier 1: Hourly snapshots (application + DB) retained according to retention policy. Snapshots using Veeam Backup & Replication; primary copy stored on-site on NAS; immutable copy replicated to Azure Blob Storage (region: Poland Central). Additionally, continuous replication of Tier 1 VMs/databases to DR site (Katowice) via Azure Site Recovery (ASR).
- Tier 2: Daily full backups plus incremental backups every 4 hours using Veeam. On-site NAS primary; encrypted offsite copy to Azure Blob Storage (Poland Central).
- Tier 3: Daily backups (full) retained per retention schedule; on-site with offsite copy to Azure.
Backup integrity is validated by automated verification jobs and manual monthly restores. All backup repositories containing personal data are managed in accordance with RODO Art. 32 (encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, logging).

4. Disaster Recovery Site
The designated DR site is the Katowice depot secondary data center (LogiCore Katowice). Katowice is provisioned to run Tier 1 workloads within RTO targets using pre-staged infrastructure, network routing, and capacity reservations in Azure (where required). Physical site meets BHP and PSP guidance for critical infrastructure and has UPS and backup generator capacity sized for critical racks.

5. Replication and Failover Mechanisms
- Tier 1: ASR-managed replication of virtual machines and databases to Katowice; failover orchestration runbooks maintained in IT runbook repository.
- Tier 2: Backups restored to alternate compute in Azure or to Katowice VMs as needed; DNS TTLs preconfigured for rapid swap.
- Tier 3: Restoration from backups to non-production hosts; manual validation.

6. DR Test Regime
- Tabletop exercises: Quarterly (scenario walkthrough with IT, Operations, Customer Service and Depot managers). Simulations recorded.
- Annual full failover test: Scheduled in May each year on a Saturday between 02:00—14:00. Full failover of Tier 1 workloads to Katowice with full validation steps executed. Tests follow change control and communication plan; rollback steps defined.
All tests are documented, and outcomes feed improvement actions.

7. Escalation and Contacts
Escalation chain for incidents impacting availability:
1) On-call IT Engineer (first responder) — phone + SMS cascade
2) IT Manager (Dział IT) — incident commander
3) CTO — executive escalation
4) External DR support (Capgemini) — per contractual SLA (24/7 emergency support)
On-call rota is maintained in HR and conforms to Kodeks Pracy. Contact phone numbers and secure escalation list are kept in the IT emergency contacts directory (access controlled).

8. Common Incident Scenarios and Immediate Actions
- Power failure at primary site: Ensure UPS active; start generator per depot SOP. If power cannot be restored within 30—60 minutes for critical systems, initiate ASR failover procedure to Katowice.
- Internet failure: Dual-ISP automatic failover configured at edge routers. If both ISPs fail, activate BGP-based emergency routing or failover to cellular link; escalate to Network Operations.
- Server/Host failure: Cluster failover and VM live migration where possible. If VM corrupted, restore from latest clean snapshot (Tier 1 snapshots hourly).
- Ransomware / Malware outbreak: Isolate infected segments immediately (network ACLs, VLAN quarantine); disable domain trust where required; do not pay ransom. Identify clean backup point (pre-infection) and restore to isolated DR environment; reinstall endpoints as needed. Notify DPO and fulfill RODO breach obligations if personal data exposed.
- Natural disaster / site loss: Declare disaster, trigger full site failover to Katowice. Execute DR runbooks and update stakeholders.

9. Recovery Procedures — Step-by-step for Tier 1 Systems
WMS Recovery (Tier 1):
1) Confirm incident and declare DR activation with IT Manager.
2) Verify replication health in Azure Site Recovery; identify latest consistent snapshot (<=1 hour RPO).
3) Initiate failover to Katowice using ASR runbook: bring up WMS application VMs and dependent DB clusters.
4) Change DNS entries or update load balancer to point to Katowice endpoints (pre-approved DNS TTLs reduce propagation time).
5) Validate database integrity, run application smoke tests, and restore any queued messaging (MQ) from snapshot.
6) Perform functional tests with warehouse team (pick/put scenarios) and confirm transactional consistency.
7) Notify users via Teams and SMS cascade when WMS is available for production.
8) Post-failover, monitor performance and begin data re-synchronization to primary site once available.

Fleet Tracking Recovery (Tier 1):
1) Confirm telemetry feed loss; check telemetry gateway and network status.
2) If primary site unavailable, route incoming telematics streams to Katowice ingestion endpoints (preconfigured in carrier/APN settings or via DNS swap).
3) Start vehicle tracking backend services from replicated VMs/containers in Katowice using latest replicated DB snapshot (<=1h).
4) Validate GPS feed processing, geofencing rules, and driver communications.
5) Coordinate with Operations and Carriers to validate live tracking and ETA calculations.
6) Repoint telematics platform endpoints back to primary when restored and reconcile buffered messages.

10. Communications Plan
Internal communications:
- Primary channel: Microsoft Teams incident channel (created per incident).
- Escalation channel: SMS cascade for on-call and management.
- Depot liaison: Dedicated Teams group with depot managers in Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań.
External communications:
- Customers: Use the customer notification template below. Customer Service owns distribution and follow-up.
- Carriers / 3PL partners: Use carrier notification template. Transport ops team to coordinate.
Customer notification template (to be adapted per incident):
Subject: LogiCore — Service Incident Notification
Body: Szanowni Państwo,
We inform you that on [date/time UTC] LogiCore experienced an incident affecting [WMS / tracking]. We are operating under our DR plan and estimate restoration within [RTO]. Immediate impacts: [short list]. We will provide hourly updates via [link/email]. Contact: incident@logicore.pl.
Carrier notification template:
Subject: Operational Notice — Tracking/Dispatch Impact
Body: Brief incident description, affected services, contingency instructions (manual manifests, alternative contact), expected resolution.
All external messages are reviewed by Customer Service and Legal before release when feasible.

11. Post-Incident Review and Continuous Improvement
- Root Cause Analysis (RCA) completed within 5 business days of the incident closure.
- Lessons Learned presentation delivered to Management Board (Zarząd) and relevant department heads within 10 business days.
- Action items prioritized and tracked in the CAPA register; responsible owners assigned with deadlines.
- Changes to runbooks, backup cadence, or architecture are implemented via standard Change Management.

12. Compliance and Governance
This plan supports continuity requirements under ISO 22301 and ensures technical and organizational measures consistent with RODO Art. 32 (confidentiality, integrity, availability of personal data). Backups and offsite replicas are encrypted and access-controlled. On-call and recovery activities respect Kodeks Pracy requirements. External DR engagements (e.g., Capgemini) are governed by contracts and SLAs that include data processing agreements (DPA) when handling personal data.

13. Roles and Responsibilities
- IT Manager: Incident commander, DR activation authority, liaise with management.
- On-call IT Engineer: First responder, initial triage, execute runbook steps.
- CTO: Executive escalation and business decisions (e.g., declare extended outage).
- Operations (Logistics): Validate operational continuity, run manual workarounds.
- Customer Service: External communications.
- DPO: RODO notifications and breach management.

14. Document Maintenance
The IT DR/BCP document is reviewed annually and after every DR test or major incident. Changes approved by CTO and stored in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB). Next scheduled annual review: May (aligned with full failover test).

Appendices
A. DR Test Schedule: Quarterly tabletop dates, Annual full failover — May (Saturday 02:00—14:00).
B. Contact Directory: Secure document (access-controlled) with on-call roster, IT Manager, CTO, Capgemini escalation.
C. Runbook Locations: Internal SharePoint/Confluence links to WMS and Fleet tracking recovery scripts and Azure runbooks.

This document is classified Internal. For questions contact: IT Manager — it.manager@logicore.pl.

title: Forklift Near-Miss — Incident Investigation Report (INC-2025-031)
text:
INCIDENT INVESTIGATION REPORT
Reference: INC-2025-031
Report Date: 2025-08-15
Prepared by: Aleksandra Nowak, Safety Officer (Inspektor BHP)
Location: Warehouse A, intersection of Aisle 14 and Cross-Aisle C, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa
Date/Time of Incident: 2025-08-14, 14:23
Severity: Near-miss (Level 2 on 5-point scale)
Personnel involved: Tomasz Kowalski (Forklift operator FK-012), Anna Wiśniewska (picker)

1. Executive summary
At 14:23 on 2025-08-14, forklift FK-012, operated by Tomasz Kowalski, rounded the blind corner at the intersection of Aisle 14 and Cross-Aisle C at approximately 12 km/h (posted limit at intersections: 5 km/h) and passed within an estimated 0.5 m of pedestrian picker Anna Wiśniewska, who was crossing from Picking Zone B. No contact occurred and there were no injuries. The event is classified as a near-miss (Level 2). Immediate containment and corrective actions were taken on the day of the incident. This report documents the investigation, root cause analysis (5-Why), contributing factors, witness statements, photographic evidence descriptions, corrective actions (with deadlines and responsibilities), lessons learned and sign-offs.

2. Detailed description of the event
On 2025-08-14 at approximately 14:23, FK-012 approached the intersection of Aisle 14 and Cross-Aisle C from Aisle 14. The operator was driving at an estimated 12 km/h. The speed limit in warehouse intersections is 5 km/h and operators are required to proceed with reduced speed and sound the horn when visibility is limited. The intersection is normally equipped with a convex safety mirror to increase visibility for both drivers and pedestrians; that mirror was absent at the time due to renovation activities in Zone C (mirror removed on 2025-07-28 and not reinstalled). Picker Anna Wiśniewska was crossing the intersection from Picking Zone B when the forklift rounded the corner. The operator applied emergency braking immediately upon sighting the pedestrian; the vehicle came to a stop with an estimated lateral clearance of 0.5 m. There was no contact and no medical treatment required. Work resumed after area was secured and a temporary corrective measure was implemented.

3. Immediate actions taken (same day)
- Area cordoned off briefly to conduct initial assessment and to ensure no ongoing hazard.
- Temporary convex mirror sourced from Depot Katowice and installed on 2025-08-14 (same day) at 15:10 to restore visibility at the intersection.
- Forklift operator FK-012 (T. Kowalski) removed from operational duties for remainder of shift; verbal warning issued and immediate debrief conducted by warehouse manager (M. Zieliński) and Safety Officer.
- Incident recorded in internal safety system and notified to Safety Committee (Komitet BHP).
- Witness statements collected and preliminary photographs taken (see section 7).

4. Root Cause Analysis — 5 Whys
Why did the forklift travel at excessive speed? Because the operator was driving to make up time; he estimated speed at ~12 km/h and did not reduce at the blind corner.
Why was the operator trying to make up time? Because the driver was behind schedule due to a late inbound delivery (manifest and unloading delayed by ~40 minutes).
Why was the corner blind? Because the convex safety mirror normally installed at Aisle 14 / Cross-Aisle C had been removed during Zone C renovation on 2025-07-28 and had not been reinstalled or substituted.
Why was the mirror not reinstalled? Renovation contractor completed structural work but handover documentation did not include reinstallation of safety fixtures; internal follow-up was delayed and not tracked in the renovation checklist.
Why was there no compensating control (e.g., traffic marshal or temporary signage)? Because the temporary risk assessment for renovation did not adequately identify the loss of the mirror as a critical control and no interim controls were assigned to a responsible person.
Root cause: combination of administrative oversight during Zone C renovation resulting in absence of a critical visual control (mirror), coupled with time pressure on operators due to late inbound delivery and inadequate temporary controls.

5. Contributing factors
- Absent convex mirror (removed 2025-07-28 during renovation; not reinstalled).
- Time pressure on operators arising from late inbound delivery and schedule recovery.
- Worn floor markings at the intersection reducing visual cues for vehicle/pedestrian segregation.
- Potential lapse in adherence to speed limits and horn use at blind corners.
- Incomplete renovation handover and lack of documented temporary controls (failure in contractor coordination).

6. Witness statements (summarised)
Witness 1: Piotr Malinowski (picker, witnessed from Picking Zone B) — Observed the forklift approach the corner faster than usual and heard the operator brake sharply. Confirmed mirror was absent and floor markings were faded. Reported that traffic is usually heavier at this hour due to afternoon picks and recent late deliveries increase pressure.
Witness 2: Ewa Jankowska (loading dock assistant) — Noted that the inbound truck arrived late and unloading took longer than planned; remembers a toolbox talk two weeks prior reminding staff about speed limits but said the absence of the mirror was known informally among staff and not yet resolved.

7. Photographic evidence (attached/described)
- Photo 1: Wide-angle view of intersection Aisle 14 / Cross-Aisle C showing absence of convex mirror and faded floor markings; timestamp 2025-08-14 14:40.
- Photo 2: Close-up of painted speed limit and pedestrian crossing lines at the intersection, showing wear and partial obliteration; timestamp 2025-08-14 14:42.
- Photo 3: Temporary convex mirror installed (same day) with bracket and mounting location annotated; timestamp 2025-08-14 15:15.
- Photo 4: FK-012 parked position after incident showing braking distance and wheel lock indicators; timestamp 2025-08-14 14:45.
(Photos retained in the secure incident folder; personal data handled in accordance with RODO requirements.)

8. Corrective actions and deadlines (responsibilities assigned)
1) Permanent convex mirror procurement and professional reinstallation at Aisle 14 / Cross-Aisle C — Responsible: Marek Zieliński (Warehouse Manager). Deadline: 2025-08-21. Action: procure approved convex mirror (UV-resistant, anti-glare), fixed to structural column per mounting standard; update site plans and renovation handover checklist to include safety fixtures.
2) Repaint floor markings and pedestrian crossing lines at intersection — Responsible: Facility Maintenance (coordinator: M. Zieliński). Deadline: 2025-08-28. Action: use high-visibility epoxy paint, add tactile markers where appropriate, implement reflective boundary strips.
3) Pilot installation of intersection speed sensors and audio warning pilot system at three highest-risk intersections (including this one) — Responsible: Health & Safety (Aleksandra Nowak) with Facilities procurement. Deadline: 2025-09-30. Action: procure, test and evaluate system to report speed violations to supervisors; pilot results to be presented to Management and Komitet BHP.
4) Supervisor/driver briefing and toolbox talk on speed limits, horn use and behaviour at blind corners — Responsible: Shift Supervisors and HR (Magdalena Kaczmarek). Deadline: 2025-08-18. Action: mandatory attendance for all drivers and pickers; record signatures.
5) Update renovation and maintenance SOPs to require documented temporary controls when safety fixtures are removed (e.g., temporary mirror, signage, traffic marshal assignment) — Responsible: Facilities Manager and Procurement. Deadline: 2025-08-31.
6) Review of scheduling and contingency planning for late inbound deliveries to reduce time pressure on drivers — Responsible: Operations Manager. Deadline: 2025-09-15.

9. Lessons learned / recommendations to Safety Committee (Komitet BHP)
- Ensure safety fixtures removed during renovation are tracked as critical items in the handover checklist and reinstalled before area returns to operational status.
- Time pressure is a recurring latent hazard — Operations should align scheduling buffers and contingency procedures to avoid recoveries that encourage unsafe driving behaviour.
- Low-cost, rapid interventions (temporary convex mirrors, clear temporary signage, escorting traffic marshals) are effective and must be mandated when visibility controls are compromised.
- Consider technology aids (speed sensors, audible warnings, telematics for forklifts) as a medium-term control to complement administrative rules.
- Reinforce RODO-compliant procedures for collecting witness statements and photo evidence; ensure incident records are stored and access-controlled.

10. Conclusion
This near-miss event was prevented from becoming a collision due to operator reaction and a degree of luck. The investigation identifies both immediate human factors (time pressure, speed) and systemic failures (absent mirror due to incomplete renovation handover, worn markings). Corrective actions have been defined with clear responsibilities and deadlines. Implementation and follow-up will be tracked by the Safety Officer and reported to senior management and Komitet BHP.

11. Signatures (electronic signatures on file)
Aleksandra Nowak — Safety Officer / Inspektor BHP — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — 2025-08-15
Marek Zieliński — Warehouse Manager — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — 2025-08-15
Magdalena Kaczmarek — HR Representative (HR Business Partner) — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — 2025-08-15

Distribution: Warehouse Manager (M. Zieliński), Safety Committee (Komitet BHP), HR (M. Kaczmarek), Operations Manager, Facilities, Incident File (secure, RODO-compliant storage).

title: Cold Storage Temperature Excursion — Incident Report (INC-2025-047)
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Incident Report
Incident reference: INC-2025-047
Date/time discovered: 2025-09-22, 06:15 (excursion began overnight, approx. 00:30)
Location: Cold Storage Unit 3 (C3), Warehouse B, Katowice depot
Prepared by: Tomasz Nowak, Warehouse Manager Katowice
Date prepared: 2025-09-22 11:30

1. Executive summary
On 2025-09-22 at 06:15 the night shift discovered a temperature excursion in Cold Storage Unit 3 (C3), Warehouse B, Katowice. Temperature records show a steady rise from target -18°C to -8°C over approximately six hours with a peak reading of -6.2°C at 05:45. Two product groups were affected: FreshFoods refrigerated dairy (3 pallets, 240 cases, batch FK-2025-09-187) and PharmaCorp insulin pens (1 pallet, 48 boxes, batch PH-INS-25Q3-004). Financial impact: FreshFoods yogurt declared write-off — EUR 12,400. PharmaCorp insulin (high-sensitivity pharma product) value EUR 87,500 placed under quarantine pending a formal GDP temperature deviation investigation per contractual requirements.

2. Incident timeline (chronological)
- ~00:30 — Initial compressor performance degradation begins (inferred from temperature trend and compressor telemetry).
- 02:17 — Cold room alarm triggered at -14°C (automatic alert sent to night shift supervisor). Notification log indicates alarm sent but no follow-up; supervisor mobile was set to silent overnight.
- 02:30–05:30 — Temperature continues to rise gradually from -14°C to approximately -8°C.
- 05:45 — Peak temperature recorded: -6.2°C (data logger: C3-LOG-01).
- 06:15 — Night shift operator (operator on duty) performs routine check following end-of-shift handover and discovers excursion; immediate notification to Warehouse Manager.
- 06:20 — Products in C3 placed on immediate quarantine; access restricted; quarantine tags applied to affected pallets.
- 06:30 — Backup compressor manually actuated (manual start procedure). Technician dispatched to site.
- 06:45 — Maintenance team on site; inspection indicates compressor bearing failure (Unit C3-COMP-02, Bitzer 4PCS-15.2Y). Isolation valve for backup compressor found closed — left in closed position since June maintenance intervention.
- 07:00 — FreshFoods (customer) notified by Logistics Customer Service; replacement shipment arranged and dispatched same day as agreed.
- 07:15 — PharmaCorp (customer) notified; contractual requirement for GDP deviation report communicated (PharmaCorp requested initial notification within 24 hours).
- 08:45 — Temperature restored to target -18°C and stabilized; continuous monitoring in place.

3. Temperature and monitoring data
- Target temperature: -18°C (frozen dairy / pharma storage condition per SOP).
- Start of deviation: ~00:30 (inferred from gradient in data logger and SCADA).
- Alarm threshold configured: -14°C; first alarm event at 02:17 (sent to night shift supervisor only).
- Ramp: -18°C → -8°C over ~6 hours.
- Peak: -6.2°C at 05:45 (C3-LOG-01, attached CSV & SCADA extract in annex).
- Alarm history: Single notification route to night supervisor mobile; no automated escalation or secondary contacts configured on alarm platform.

4. Products affected
- FreshFoods yogurt: 3 pallets (240 cases), batch FK-2025-09-187. Status: declared total loss; write-off approved. Financial loss (inventory): EUR 12,400.
- PharmaCorp insulin pens: 1 pallet (48 boxes), batch PH-INS-25Q3-004. Status: quarantined; GDP temperature deviation investigation required. Estimated inventory value at risk: EUR 87,500 (held as potential claim contingent on QA outcome).
Note: All affected product pallets are secured in quarantine location Q-4 (Warehouse B) with restricted access. Photographic record and chain-of-custody logged.

5. Root cause analysis (RCA)
Primary root cause: Mechanical failure of compressor bearing on Unit C3-COMP-02 (Bitzer 4PCS-15.2Y), resulting in progressive loss of cooling capacity and subsequent temperature rise.
Evidence: On-site mechanical inspection by Maintenance Manager and contracted Bitzer technician; bearing seizure and metal fatigue signs observed. Serial number and unit ID confirmed. Service report and photos attached to annex. Compressor failure timing correlates with SCADA telemetry.

Contributing factors:
- Preventive maintenance (PM) schedule not up-to-date: last PM performed 2025-08-08. Unit was due for monthly maintenance on 2025-09-08 and therefore was 14 days overdue at time of incident (monthly PM schedule). The overdue status contributed to unnoticed wear on bearing/lubrication checks.
- Maintenance backlog: vacancy of one HVAC/CR technician in Katowice maintenance team since 2025-07-01 negatively impacted adherence to PM schedule and response capacity.
- Alarm notification design: current alarm routing delivers critical high/low temperature alerts only to the assigned night shift supervisor (single point of contact); no escalation path or secondary recipients configured. Supervisor's phone was on silent overnight — no auto-failover.
- Backup compressor isolation valve left closed following a maintenance intervention in June 2025 (work order MNT-2025-06-17). Valve closure prevented automatic transfer/load sharing between compressors, necessitating manual activation and delaying cooling recovery.

6. Immediate containment actions
- 06:15 — Affected inventory isolated; quarantine tags applied; access logged (chain-of-custody established).
- 06:30 — Backup compressor manually started; manual cooling mitigation procedures executed per SOP WARE-CR-03.
- 06:40 — Maintenance team secured failed compressor, placed out of service; ventilation and defrost controls verified.
- 07:00 — FreshFoods notified; replacement shipment arranged; documentation of notification attached.
- 07:15 — PharmaCorp notified; advised of quarantine and next steps; GDP deviation procedure initiated (see Section 8).
- 08:45 — Temperature re-stabilized at -18°C; continuous monitoring and 15-minute data logging resumed.

7. Financial impact and insurance
- Inventory write-off: FreshFoods yogurt EUR 12,400 (booked on 2025-09-22 internal finance entry).
- Potential inventory at risk (quarantine): PharmaCorp insulin pens EUR 87,500 (pending QA release or write-off).
- Business interruption costs (internal): estimated additional labor & expedited shipment costs (PLN) — preliminary estimate PLN 6,800 (internal account; finalization pending).
- Insurance: Initial claim submitted to corporate insurer (policy: ColdStorageAllRisk 2024-2026). Claim reference: INS-LOGI-2025-089. Submission date: 2025-09-23. Status: Claim acknowledged; adjuster visit scheduled 2025-09-25. Supporting documentation (SCADA logs, photos, PM records, quarantine certificates) attached to claim. No payout approved to date.

8. Regulatory and customer obligations
- PharmaCorp: GDP temperature deviation investigation required — customer requested initial deviation report within 24 hours; full investigation report to follow per contract. LogiCore Quality (QA) assigned to lead and liaise with PharmaCorp QA and external Qualified Person as required.
- Notifications: No immediate report to GIF/PIP/PSP required by LogiCore; PharmaCorp will advise if submission to Główny Inspektorat Farmaceutyczny (GIF) is necessary under Prawo Farmaceutyczne. All handling of pharmaceutical goods to follow GDP and applicable regulations.
- Data handling: Notifications and records shared under RODO-compliant procedures; customer-sensitive data redacted in shared annexes as appropriate.

9. Corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) — owners and timeline
- CA-01: Immediate repair and replacement of failed bearing and full recommissioning of Unit C3-COMP-02. Owner: Piotr Zieliński, Maintenance Manager. Target completion: 2025-09-27. Verification: Commissioning report and vibration analysis.
- CA-02: Re-open review and update PM scheduler; implement automated PM reminders with escalation to Head of Maintenance if overdue >48 hours. Owner: Head of Engineering (Anna Borkowska). Deadline: 2025-10-05.
- CA-03: Fill HVAC/CR technician vacancy; initiate expedited recruitment and temporary contract with third-party certified technician to eliminate maintenance backlog. Owner: HR + Head of Maintenance. Recruitment start: 2025-09-23; target hire date: 2025-10-15.
- CA-04: Alarm escalation redesign: Configure alarm system to notify primary supervisor + two secondary contacts and automatic escalation to on-call Maintenance Manager after 10 minutes of unattended alarm; integrate SMS and email failover. Owner: IT & Warehouse Manager. Deadline: 2025-09-30.
- CA-05: Audit and corrective action for backup compressor valves and auto-transfer logic; ensure all isolation valves locked in service position with tamper-evident seals; update commissioning checklist. Owner: Maintenance Manager. Deadline: 2025-10-01.
- CA-06: GDP & pharma handling review: QA lead to perform formal GDP deviation analysis for PharmaCorp batch PH-INS-25Q3-004 and prepare final disposition recommendation (release/return/quarantine destruction) in coordination with PharmaCorp QA. Owner: Anna Kowalska, Head of Quality. Preliminary report: 2025-09-23 (24-hour initial report). Full investigation: 2025-09-30.
- CA-07: Staff training: Reinforce SOP for night shift response to cold-room alarms and escalation procedures (BHP & SOP update). Owner: Warehouse Manager. Training sessions completed by: 2025-10-06.

10. Evidence and attachments (annexes)
- Annex A: SCADA and data logger extract (C3-LOG-01) CSV (00:00–09:00 2025-09-22).
- Annex B: Maintenance work orders and PM history (Unit C3-COMP-02).
- Annex C: Photos of failed bearing, compressor serial plate, isolation valve position, and quarantined pallets.
- Annex D: Customer notification emails to FreshFoods and PharmaCorp.
- Annex E: Insurance claim submission (INS-LOGI-2025-089).

11. Recommendations and management remarks
Immediate priority is finalizing the GDP investigation for PharmaCorp goods to determine disposition and potential insurance subrogation. Strengthening alarm escalation and completing PM scheduler improvements are critical to prevent recurrence. Filling the maintenance vacancy and engaging temporary certified technicians during recruitment will reduce backlog risk.

12. Approval and sign-offs
Prepared by:
Tomasz Nowak
Warehouse Manager — Katowice Depot
LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Signature: ____________________  Date: 2025-09-22

Reviewed by:
Piotr Zieliński
Maintenance Manager
LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Signature: ____________________  Date: 2025-09-22

Quality Lead (Pharma/GDP):
Anna Kowalska
Head of Quality Assurance
LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Signature: ____________________  Date: 2025-09-22

Executive acknowledgement:
Marta Lewandowska
COO
LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Signature: ____________________  Date: 2025-09-23

Document classification: Internal — clearance level 2. Distribution list: Warehouse Katowice, Maintenance, QA, Customer Service, Finance, HR, Insurance Liaison. All annexes are retained in the incident folder INC-2025-047 and in LogiCore Document Management System (DMS) per RODO and internal record retention policies.

title: Driver Fatigue Incident Report — INC-2025-019
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Incident Report
Reference: INC-2025-019
Date/Time of incident: 2025-07-03, 16:47
Location: A8 motorway (Katowice direction), km 147.3 (okolice Kraków)
Prepared by: Logistics Department, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa (archiwum: Kraków depot copy)
Sensitive personal data handled according to RODO; distribution restricted to authorised personnel.

1. Executive summary
On 3 July 2025 at approximately 16:47 a vehicle operated by LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (MAN TGX 18.510, reg. KAT 5R78) loaded with chemical raw materials (ChemTrans shipment CT-2025-07-0342) exhibited a single lane drift onto the hard shoulder on A8 motorway (Katowice direction) at km 147.3 near Kraków. Lane Departure Warning (LDW) triggered and the vehicle dashcam recorded a 3‑second drift. Driver Paweł Nowak (employee since 2019; ADR certified; clean driving record) self-corrected and maintained control. No collision, no injuries, no product release. Immediate tactical measures were taken: vehicle pulled to nearest rest area; replacement driver dispatched from Kraków depot and arrived at 19:30; shipment continued under replacement driver.

2. Incident details
- Driver: Paweł Nowak (LogiCore employee since 2019). ADR certified. No prior disciplinary or accident history on company records.
- Vehicle: MAN TGX 18.510, registration KAT 5R78.
- Load: Chemical raw materials, ChemTrans shipment CT-2025-07-0342 (transport documentation carried in vehicle; Prawo Farmaceutyczne/GIF considerations observed where applicable by shipper).
- Circumstances: At 16:47 LDW alarm activated; dashcam footage shows vehicle drifting slowly to the hard shoulder for approximately 3 seconds before corrective steering input by driver returned the vehicle to the lane. No abrupt manoeuvres, no braking events recorded; ESC/ABS systems not triggered. No collisions.

3. Driver status at time of event
At the moment of the event driver reported being 11 hours on duty with 8.5 hours recorded as driving time (approaching daily driving limit). Tachograph and driver statements confirm that required breaks, as recorded on the tachograph, were taken. Driver reported poor sleep the previous night and that ambient temperature in cab was approximately 34°C at the time of the incident. Driver voluntarily stopped at next rest area and requested replacement.

4. Tachograph analysis (digital) — summary of duty day (detailed download attached to internal file)
Duty period: 2025-07-03 05:47 — 16:47 (11:00)
Recorded statuses (principal entries):
- 05:47–09:47 Driving: 4.00 h
- 09:47–11:17 Other work (loading paperwork/standby at origin): 1.50 h
- 11:17–12:02 Break/Rest (tachograph rest status): 0.75 h (45 min)
- 12:02–15:02 Driving: 3.00 h
- 15:02–15:17 Other work (paperwork, departure checks): 0.25 h
- 15:17–16:47 Driving: 1.50 h
Totals: Driving 8.50 h; Other work 1.75 h; Rest 0.75 h; Duty 11.00 h
Notes: The tachograph log shows the 45 min break recorded and distributed during the origin loading delay; the loading delay at the Płock refinery (see root cause section) included periods of both 'other work' (waiting/communication) and recorded rest (driver used part of hold for rest). Digital tachograph files have been exported and archived per company procedure (RODO-compliant access control).

5. Root cause analysis
Primary root cause: Operational re-planning by dispatcher following an unplanned loading delay at origin (Płock refinery). Loading at Płock was delayed 2.5 hours due to a quality hold from the shipper. The driver completed loading and was assigned the return run by the dispatcher without sufficient re-planning to mitigate cumulative on‑duty time and fatigue risk. Although tachograph records show compliance with minimum statutory breaks and EU Regulation 561/2006 driving limits were not exceeded, the late loading and immediate assignment of the return leg pushed the driver close to the daily cumulative duty threshold and increased fatigue risk.

6. Contributing factors
- Late loading at Płock refinery (2.5 h quality hold) not fully communicated in a way that triggered mandatory re-planning by dispatcher.
- Hot weather: ambient temperature approx. 34°C at time of event; confirmed by driver statement and regional weather station (Województwo małopolskie). Heat contributes to reduced vigilance.
- Driver-reported poor sleep the previous night (subjective factor) — documented in driver statement; not contrary to Kodeks Pracy but a BHP/fatigue risk factor.
- Operational pressure: tight turnaround expectations for return run.

7. Immediate actions taken
- Driver self-corrected; vehicle brought to nearest rest area and engine stopped for assessment.
- Driver was assessed for immediate impairment — no alcohol or drug indicators; breathalyser negative (company handheld device); medical check not required on site per PSP/PIP guidance as no injury occurred.
- Replacement driver instructed and dispatched from Kraków depot; replacement arrived 19:30 and took over the shipment. Chain-of-custody and ADR documents transferred to replacement driver. Shipment continued to destination under new driver control.
- Vehicle inspected for technical faults related to steering/tyres/brakes by depot mechanics upon return — no defects found.
- Incident logged in internal Safety & Quality register and external reporting obligations evaluated (no PSP/GIF notification required as no leak/contamination occurred).

8. Regulatory and compliance assessment
- EU Regulation 561/2006: Tachograph and driving/rest records indicate no statutory exceedance; the driver’s recorded driving time (8.5 h) is within national/EU limits for the day. No formal breach of Ustawa o transporcie drogowym or EU driving-time rules.
- Kodeks Pracy: Duty time and rest periods reviewed; no contravention identified, but Kodeks Pracy and company BHP expect active fatigue risk management beyond mere compliance.
- ADR: Vehicle and driver documentation (ADR) were valid; hazardous goods carriage protocols observed.
- RODO: Personal data and tachograph files handled and archived per internal RODO procedures.
Conclusion: No regulatory violation to be reported to Inspekcja Transportu Drogowego/PIP based on driving times; however, incident represents an operational fatigue risk event and a gap in fatigue management.

9. Corrective actions (short and medium term)
Immediate (within 7 days):
- Formal debrief with dispatcher involved; written reminder and retraining on contingency planning when origin delays exceed 1 hour.
- Amendment of dispatcher checklist to require explicit fatigue re-evaluation when accumulated on‑duty time approaches 10 hours.
- Update incident log and communicate safety bulletin to all drivers and dispatchers (BHP notice).

Medium term (30–90 days):
- Implement a fatigue risk assessment tool in the TMS (Transport Management System) that flags assignments where on-duty time after planned run would exceed 10 hours.
- Mandatory dispatcher training on fatigue management and legal framework (EU 561/2006, Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) — completion recorded in HR training matrix.
- Policy change: mandatory rest policy — if driver on-duty time exceeds 10 hours (regardless of driving time recorded) dispatcher must remove return assignment or arrange relief driver; this becomes formal company policy and is appended to SOPs.
- Incorporate heat-stress guidance into driver BHP handbook and encourage use of cab climate control, hydration breaks, and mandatory stop if driver reports poor sleep or high fatigue.

10. Lessons learned and preventive measures
- Compliance with driving-time rules alone is insufficient for fatigue risk management; operational planning must account for late-loading scenarios and driver well-being.
- Improve communication protocol with shippers (e.g., Płock refinery) to mandate earlier notification of holds and estimated delay durations.
- Reinforce culture where drivers can refuse continuation when fatigued without fear of reprisal (Company policy aligned with Kodeks Pracy protection and internal whistleblower procedures).

11. Notifications and records
- Internal: Safety & Quality, HR, Depot Managers (Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań, Kraków) notified.
- External: No mandatory regulatory reporting required to ITD/PSP/GIF based on available facts (no accident, no hazardous release). Legal team to confirm if further notifications necessary under shipper contractual terms.

12. Closure and follow-up
- Replacement driver completed delivery. Original driver was given a day off following event and scheduled for a fatigue awareness briefing before return to duty. Follow-up audit of dispatcher planning will be conducted within 30 days.

Prepared by: Logistics Manager, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa
Date: 2025-07-04
Document controlled under company incident management procedures. Distribution limited; contains personal data protected under RODO.

title: Loading Bay 6 — Slip and Fall Incident Report (INC-2025-055)
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Incident Report
Reference: INC-2025-055
Date/Time of incident: 11 October 2025, 11:35
Location: Loading Bay 6, Warehouse A, Warszawa depot
Reported by: Tomasz Nowak, BHP Officer
Prepared by: Anna Kowalska, Warehouse Manager

1. Summary
On 11.10.2025 at approximately 11:35, warehouse operator Jakub Majewski (tenure: 2 years) suffered a slip-and-fall incident at Loading Bay 6, Warehouse A, Warszawa depot. The worker slipped on a wet dock surface during heavy rain and fell from the dock edge to ground level (approx. 1.2 m). Injuries sustained: sprained right ankle and bruising to right hip. Immediate first aid was applied and the employee was taken to Szpital Bielański Emergency Department; X-ray was negative for fracture. Time lost from work: 3 working days; employee returned on modified duties for 2 weeks thereafter.

2. Facts and timeline
- 09:00 — Rain began; site records and CCTV confirm onset of heavy rain. No rain-related operational bulletin issued. Rain mat for external dock stored in maintenance room.
- 11:35 — Incident occurs. CCTV and witness statements (2 colleagues) confirm slip on pooled water adjacent to dock edge, loss of balance and fall to ground level (~1.2 m). Dock edge had no temporary barrier in place.
- 11:40 — First aid administered by on-shift first aider: cold compress applied to ankle/hip. BHP Officer notified.
- 12:10 — Employee transported by company vehicle to Szpital Bielański ED. X-ray performed; no fracture detected. Diagnosed: sprain (distorsio) of right ankle, contusion of right hip. Analgesia administered.
- 13:00 — Incident logged in Zakładowa Książka Wypadków; PIP (Państwowa Inspekcja Pracy) notified within 24 hours per Art. 234 Kodeksu Pracy. RODO procedures followed regarding medical data confidentiality.

3. Immediate causes and root cause analysis
- Direct cause: slip on wet dock surface resulting in uncontrolled fall from dock edge (~1.2 m).
- Root cause: drainage channel adjacent to Bay 6 was blocked by accumulated shrink-wrap debris causing water pooling on the dock surface. The blockage prevented normal runoff during heavy rain.
- Contributing factors:
  • Anti-slip rain mat, normally used during wet conditions, was stored in maintenance room and not deployed despite rain starting at 09:00; mat deployment is not included in the current shift handover checklist.
  • Drainage channel maintenance schedule not adhered to: records show last cleaning three weeks prior; required frequency is weekly per site maintenance plan.
  • No temporary barrier or high-visibility edge marking was in place at the time of incident.
  • Lighting at the dock measured at 230 lux (adequate); visibility not judged to be a direct factor.

4. First aid and medical outcome
- Onsite: cold compress applied; limb immobilised for transport. No haemorrhage or open wound.
- Hospital care: Szpital Bielański ED — X-ray negative for fracture; diagnosis: sprained right ankle (grade I-II) and bruising to right hip. Discharged with instructions for RICE, analgesics as needed, and follow-up with company occupational health as required.
- Return-to-work: employee off work for 3 working days. Returned with temporary modified duties (no climbing, no heavy lifting) for 2 weeks.

5. Notifications and legal compliance
- Incident recorded in Zakładowa Książka Wypadków per Kodeks Pracy.
- PIP (Państwowa Inspekcja Pracy) notified within 24 hours in accordance with Art. 234 KP. Documentation transmitted includes witness statements, CCTV stills, medical report (with employee consent) and preliminary investigation notes.
- RODO: personal and medical data handled in accordance with company RODO policy; access restricted to authorised personnel (BHP, HR, occupational health).

6. Corrective actions (immediate and planned)
Immediate (completed within 24 hours):
- Removal of shrink-wrap debris and full cleaning of drainage channel adjacent to Bay 6. Responsible: Maintenance Supervisor Piotr Lewandowski (completed 11.10.2025, 16:00).
- Deployment of temporary anti-slip mats and high-visibility cones at Bay 6. Responsible: Shift Supervisor (completed 11.10.2025, 14:00).
- Audit of all external dock drainage channels across Warszawa depot to identify similar blockages. Responsible: Maintenance Team (completed 12.10.2025).

Short term (1–14 days):
- Update shift handover checklist to include active deployment of rain mat when rainfall recorded or forecasted, and verification of dock drainage by incoming shift. Responsible: Warehouse Manager Anna Kowalska. Deadline: 22.10.2025.
- Training sessions for all warehouse operators and shift supervisors on wet-weather procedures and safe dock-edge practices. Responsible: BHP Officer Tomasz Nowak. Deadline: 31.10.2025.

Medium term (1–3 months):
- Implement weekly drainage cleaning schedule (formalised maintenance order, with digital verification and photos). Responsible: Maintenance Supervisor. Effective: 01.11.2025.
- Evaluate and trial permanent anti-slip surface coating for external dock edges (technical assessment and cost estimate). Responsible: Procurement & Maintenance. Report due: 15.11.2025.

Long term (3–6 months):
- Install automatic rain sensor linked system to trigger visual/audible alert and automated partial deployment of protective mats or mechanical covers, or electronic reminder to shift supervisor. Feasibility study and budget request prepared by Facilities by 31.12.2025.
- Consider installation of fixed guardrail or retractable barrier at Bay 6 dock edge where operationally feasible. Technical feasibility and safety assessment to be completed by 28.02.2026.

7. Preventive measures and recommendations
- Enforce weekly maintenance of dock drainage channels across all depots (Gdańsk, Katowice, Wrocław, Poznań, Warszawa) to comply with internal maintenance plan and reduce risk of pooling.
- Integrate rainy-weather procedures into standard operating procedures (SOP) and shift handover checklist (inclusion of mat deployment, drainage check, and temporary barriers).
- Procure additional anti-slip mats for immediate deployment and store spares at each loading area to avoid single-point storage dependency.
- Review PPE policy for wet conditions and require high-traction footwear for external dock operations during wet weather.

8. Responsible parties and monitoring
- Incident owner: Warehouse Manager Anna Kowalska — responsible for implementation of checklist changes, staff training and reporting progress to Logistics Director.
- Maintenance owner: Piotr Lewandowski — responsible for drainage cleaning schedule and maintenance verification.
- BHP owner: Tomasz Nowak — responsible for incident follow-up, PIP liaison, record keeping, and audit of preventive actions.
- HR & Occupational Health: manage return-to-work plan and recordkeeping under RODO.

9. Closure
This report constitutes the preliminary investigation; all corrective actions listed will be monitored and documented. A follow-up audit of Bay 6 will be performed 30 days after implementation of immediate and short-term actions. The incident is classified as an accident at work and will be used for continuous improvement of BHP procedures across LogiCore Sp. z o.o.

Prepared: 14.10.2025
Signatures (electronic): Anna Kowalska (Warehouse Manager), Tomasz Nowak (BHP Officer)

title: Chemical Leak During Unloading — Incident Report (INC-2025-008)
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Incident Report
Reference: INC-2025-008
Date/Time of incident: 2025-06-28, 09:15
Location: Loading Bay 2, Warehouse A, Warszawa depot
Prepared by: Dział Magazynowy / Warehouse Operations

1. Executive summary
At approximately 09:15 on 28 June 2025, during unloading of ChemTrans shipment CT-2025-06-0289 at Loading Bay 2 of Warehouse A (Warszawa depot), an approximate 2-litre leak of industrial degreaser (Henkel Bonderite C-AK 5050; Class 8 corrosive; UN 1760) was discovered on the dock floor. The leak originated from a hairline crack in the bottom seam of a 20 L steel drum. The spill was contained on site within 8 minutes using the SK-003 spill kit located at Bay 2. There were no injuries, no propagation to storm drains, and no measurable environmental release. The event is recorded as below reportable threshold under Rozporządzenie w sprawie substancji niebezpiecznych; no WIOŚ notification is required. Contaminated absorbents were removed and disposed of by contracted hazmat contractor EkoTeam.

2. Incident description — sequence of events
- 09:10 — ChemTrans vehicle CT-2025-06-0289 arrives at Loading Bay 2. Standard unloading procedures initiated.
- 09:14 — Warehouse operator (name withheld in accordance with RODO) begins pallet unloading. Pallet contained multiple 20 L drums of Henkel Bonderite C-AK 5050 stacked three-high on a single pallet.
- 09:15 — Operator notices wet packaging on the middle tier of the pallet while removing shrinkwrap. Visual inspection reveals a hairline crack along the bottom seam of one 20 L drum; liquid visible at the base and on the dock floor.
- 09:15–09:23 — Spill response initiated immediately: operator alerts Loading Bay supervisor and implements spill containment using SK-003 spill kit from Bay 2 (absorbent pads, neutraliser pads compatible with corrosive Class 8 substance, disposable PPE). Area isolated; immediate neighbouring bays temporarily closed for safety.
- 09:25 — EkoTeam (contracted hazmat) contacted by phone per emergency contractor list. Operator and supervisor document scene (photos taken and retained in incident folder).
- 09:40 — EkoTeam arrives and removes contaminated absorbents, cleans and neutralises affected dock surface, and transports waste off-site for disposal in accordance with applicable waste regulations.

3. Chemical information
- Product: Henkel Bonderite C-AK 5050 (industrial degreaser)
- Classification: Class 8 — corrosive
- UN number: UN 1760
- Quantity leaked: approximately 2 litres (from a single 20 L drum)

4. Immediate containment and mitigation
- Spill contained within 8 minutes of discovery (by 09:23) using SK-003 spill kit located at Bay 2 station. Containment measures included isolating the area, application of absorbent and neutraliser suitable for corrosive substances, and temporary transfer of adjacent pallets to prevent further contamination.
- All contaminated materials (absorbent pads, PPE, affected packaging) were collected, double-bagged, labelled, and removed by EkoTeam for authorised disposal. No waste entered external storm water systems — liquid collected into the internal dock collection sump and subsequently removed by EkoTeam. Confirmed by on-site supervisor that sump is isolated from municipal storm drains.

5. Personnel exposure and health
- The warehouse operator was wearing standard issued PPE (safety boots, chemical-resistant gloves, hi-vis vest). No skin contact with the product occurred. No inhalation symptoms reported. No first aid or medical treatment was required. Incident recorded in employee BHP file in accordance with Kodeks Pracy and internal BHP procedures.

6. Environmental and regulatory assessment
- Environmental impact: none observed. All liquid and contaminated materials contained on the dock surface and collected into the internal collection sump. No release to external environment or municipal drainage.
- Regulatory status: quantity and circumstances place this incident below the reportable threshold defined in Rozporządzenie w sprawie substancji niebezpiecznych. WIOŚ (Wojewódzki Inspektorat Ochrony Środowiska) was consulted informally and advised no formal notification required. No PSP (Państwowa Straż Pożarna) intervention was necessary beyond internal reporting. Records retained in incident folder for audit and potential GIF/UDT queries.

7. Root cause analysis
Preliminary investigation indicates primary root cause is inadequate packaging/stowage on the supplier side, combined with stacking method used for transport:
- The 20 L drum with the crack had been stacked three-high on a single pallet without edge protection, corner boards, or separator boards between drum tiers.
- During road transport, vertical loading shifts likely induced stress at the bottom seam of the lower drum, resulting in a hairline fracture and subsequent leak.
- No evidence of mechanical damage during unloading at the depot; damage consistent with fatigue from improper stacking.

8. Corrective and preventive actions (CAPA)
Immediate actions completed:
- Supplier (ChemTrans / product supplier) formally notified same day and provided with photographic evidence; request for investigation and confirmation of packaging standards sent by email and registered in supplier corrective action log.
- Affected pallet segregated and returned to supplier (per agreement) after EkoTeam sanitised remaining products on the pallet.
- On-site review of all chemical shipments in arrival bay; two additional pallets inspected that morning and no further defects found.

Preventive measures implemented / scheduled:
- Incoming inspection procedures for chemical shipments enhanced: mandatory visual inspection of drum seams, verification of edge protection/separator boards for stacked drums, and documentation check before acceptance. New inspection checklist added to WMS (warehouse management system).
- Updated stacking guidance issued to operations staff (Zakres BHP i instrukcje paletowania) prohibiting three-high stacking of 20 L drums without separator boards or approved edge protection. Training session scheduled for all warehouse personnel within 7 days.
- Supplier contract review initiated to add minimum packaging/protection requirements for liquid corrosives. Non-conforming suppliers to be flagged and subject to corrective action per procurement policy.

9. Documentation and follow-up
- Photographs, operator statements, and EkoTeam disposal documentation are filed in the incident folder (electronic copy retained on secure SharePoint per RODO requirements).
- Incident to be reviewed at next quarterly safety committee meeting (Komitet BHP) and discussed with procurement for supplier quality enforcement.
- Follow-up audit of incoming chemical shipments to be performed within 30 days to verify compliance with new inspection and stacking rules.

Prepared by: Warehouse Manager, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa depot
Date: 2025-06-28

Distribution: Dział Magazynowy, Dział BHP, Zakupy (Procurement), Quality Assurance, EkoTeam (contractor), Supplier (ChemTrans)

title: Minutes of the Monthly BHP Committee Meeting — Komisja BHP (Art. 237^12 KP) — October 2025
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Monthly BHP Committee Meeting (Komisja BHP per Art. 237^12 Kodeksu Pracy)
Date/time: 15 October 2025, 10:00–12:30
Location: Conference Room B, Warszawa HQ
Prepared by: Agnieszka Pawlak, BHP Inspector (Chair)
Attendees: Agnieszka Pawlak (BHP Inspector, Chair), Marek Zieliński (Warehouse Manager, Warszawa HQ), Katarzyna Duda (Fleet Manager), Piotr Grabowski (HR Business Partner), Stanisław Kowal (Trade Union Representative, NSZZ Solidarność), Ewa Michalska (Quality Manager), Robert Jankowski (Maintenance Supervisor), Dr. Joanna Wójcik (Occupational Physician — via phone)

Agenda
1) Review of September incidents
2) ISO 45001 audit preparation (TÜV Rheinland Polska, Nov 22–24)
3) Winter safety campaign (ice/snow, cold PPE)
4) PPE budget Q4 request (PLN 45,000)
5) Any Other Business (AOB)

1. Review of September incidents
Summary: In September 2025 we recorded three reportable events: two near-misses and one Lost Time Injury (LTI).
- INC-2025-031 (Near-miss) — Forklift close-proximity event. Description: Forklift FKL-12 entered aisles while pedestrian operator was performing shelf inspection; emergency stop used; no contact. Root cause preliminary: lack of segregation during pallet counting, limited sight-lines in racking bay A3. Corrective action issued: immediate implementation of temporary pedestrian exclusion during forklift operations in bay A3; install additional mirrors. Action: M. Zieliński to create exclusion schedule and install mirror (Owner: M. Zieliński; Deadline: 2025-10-22).
- INC-2025-033 (Near-miss) — Falling box from racking. Description: Box detached from top of partial pallet due to unsecured stretch wrap during manual build. No injuries. Root causes: inadequate securing procedures for partial pallets at high-level positions and lack of last-step QA check before putaway. Decision: Reinforce training on partial pallet securing; update SOP for putaway to require tape/staple and QA sign-off above 2m. Action: E. Michalska to update SOP and schedule toolbox talk (Owner: E. Michalska; Deadline: 2025-10-29).
- INC-2025-029 (LTI) — Manual handling, back strain, 5 lost days. Description: Picker sustained lower back strain during manual transfer of a 25 kg tote without mechanical aid. Lost 5 working days. Incident investigation notes: manual handling risk not adequately assessed for that task; PPE (back support belt) not consistently used. Kodeks Pracy obligations noted re: risk assessment and ergonomics. Action: Review manual handling assessments for all picking tasks >15 kg; trial mechanical aids (lift tables) in zone P2. Owner: R. Jankowski / M. Zieliński; Deadline: 2025-11-05. Occupational health follow-up (Dr. Wójcik) to advise on return-to-work program and RODO-compliant medical documentation (Owner: Dr. J. Wójcik; Deadline: 2025-10-22).
Statistics: For the period Jan–Sep 2025: Total reported incidents 18 (3 LTIs YTD), compared with Jan–Sep 2024: 26 incidents (6 LTIs). The downward trend in LTIs is encouraging but near-miss reporting remains lower than targeted frequency rate; committee reaffirms need to improve near-miss reporting culture (action: P. Grabowski to propose incentive/communication for near-miss reporting; Deadline: 2025-11-12).
Decisions: Close follow-up on INC-031 and INC-033 corrective actions; stronger manual handling controls and equipment trials.

2. ISO 45001 audit preparation — External audit by TÜV Rheinland Polska scheduled 22–24 November 2025
Update: Gap analysis completed 02 October 2025. Auditor assigned: TÜV Rheinland Polska. Key findings from gap analysis:
- Documented risk assessments require update to include recent changes to warehouse layout (bay A3 reconfiguration) and fleet routing.
- Training records (toolbox talks, induction) incomplete for contract temporary staff; RODO-aligned retention and access controls need formalisation.
- Emergency response plan needs annex for extreme cold / snow scenarios and integration with PSP contact list.
- Calibration and inspection evidence for lifting equipment (UDT-certified cranes, pallet jacks) partially missing in physical files (digital copies on quality drive incomplete).
Documentation checklist status:
- Policies: OK (minor edits pending)
- Risk assessments: 70% complete (owner: E. Michalska / M. Zieliński)
- Training records: 60% complete (owner: P. Grabowski)
- Contractor RAMS and permit-to-work: 40% complete (owner: R. Jankowski)
Actions agreed:
- Complete risk assessment updates (Owner: E. Michalska; Deadline: 2025-11-05).
- Consolidate training records and ensure RODO-compliant storage (Owner: P. Grabowski; Deadline: 2025-11-08).
- Complete contractor documentation and ensure insurance/certificate checks (Owner: R. Jankowski; Deadline: 2025-11-08).
- Prepare auditor welcome pack and site tour route (Owner: A. Pawlak; Deadline: 2025-11-12).
Decision: Committee approves budget reallocation for temporary admin support to compile files if required (limit PLN 6,000). Owner: Finance via HR (P. Grabowski to request); Deadline: 2025-10-24.

3. Winter safety campaign — Ice/snow clearing, grit stations, cold-weather PPE and anti-slip footwear assessment
Plan: Campaign runs 01 Nov 2025 – 31 Mar 2026. Key elements discussed:
- Ice/snow clearing schedule: Daily perimeter checks 06:00 and 14:00 at HQ and depots (Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań). Contracted contractor to supply heavy-duty snow blower on-call for >10 cm snowfall (contract review by Procurement).
- Grit station locations: Main gate, employee parking east, loading bay 3, pedestrian walk to office entrance. Temporary grit sacks will be staged at each depot. Owner for deployment: M. Zieliński coord. with depot managers; Deadline for staging: 2025-10-30.
- Cold-weather PPE issuance: Proposal to issue thermal jackets, insulated gloves, and chemical hand warmers to outdoor staff. Anti-slip footwear: assessment report commissioned for two types of anti-slip soles (replaceable studs vs. clip-on overshoes). Trial to be run with 20 pairs at HQ. Owner: P. Grabowski / M. Zieliński; Deadline for trial start: 2025-10-27; Trial review: 2025-11-17.
- Communication: Safety bulletin, posters, and mandatory toolbox talk on winter hazards and vehicle de-icing procedures. Owner: A. Pawlak / E. Michalska; Deadline: 2025-10-29.
Decision: Approve winter campaign plan. Action to finalise contractor SLA to include PSP/UDT notification thresholds for large snowfall events. Owner: K. Duda / Procurement; Deadline: 2025-10-28.

4. PPE budget Q4 — Request PLN 45,000 (detailed breakdown and approval condition)
Requested total: PLN 45,000 (Q4). Proposed allocation:
- Anti-slip footwear (trial + purchase) — PLN 15,000
- Thermal jackets (HQ+depots) — PLN 10,000
- Insulated gloves and liners — PLN 5,000
- Chemical hand warmers / consumables — PLN 1,500
- High-visibility winter coveralls — PLN 6,000
- Replacement/reserve PPE stock & disposables — PLN 7,500
Total: PLN 45,000
Discussion: Committee reviewed unit costs and necessity, with trade union input from S. Kowal stressing proper sizing and fit testing. Quality Manager requested standards be CE-compliant and meet EN ISO 20345 for footwear when applicable. Decision: Budget approved with condition that Procurement obtains minimum three written quotations per category, and that items meet specified EN/CE standards with warranty. Additionally, distribution plan to be recorded and sign-off collection sheet to be retained (RODO considerations for personal data of recipients). Owner for procurement: P. Grabowski with Procurement team; Deadline to place orders: 2025-10-31. Owner for distribution: M. Zieliński; Distribution completion Deadline: 2025-11-14.

5. Any Other Business (AOB)
- Contractor induction: R. Jankowski reported recent contractor lacked proper induction. Action: Implement fingerprint/ID scanning on sign-in kiosk and require up-to-date proof of training for all contractors. Owner: R. Jankowski / IT; Deadline: 2025-11-07.
- Fire drills: E. Michalska proposed annual full-site fire drill before winter. PSP notification and drill schedule to be agreed. Owner: E. Michalska; Deadline: 2025-11-10 for plan.
- Next meeting: Scheduled for 19 November 2025, 10:00–12:00, Conference Room B (final pre-audit review).

Summary of Action Items (owner + deadline)
- Install mirror + exclusion schedule for bay A3 — M. Zieliński — 2025-10-22
- Update putaway SOP & toolbox talk — E. Michalska — 2025-10-29
- Manual handling risk review and lift table trial — R. Jankowski / M. Zieliński — 2025-11-05
- Occupational health RTW plan and medical documentation — Dr. J. Wójcik — 2025-10-22
- Near-miss reporting incentive proposal — P. Grabowski — 2025-11-12
- Complete risk assessments for ISO45001 — E. Michalska — 2025-11-05
- Consolidate training records (RODO-compliant) — P. Grabowski — 2025-11-08
- Contractor RAMS complete & insurance checks — R. Jankowski — 2025-11-08
- Auditor welcome pack & site tour route — A. Pawlak — 2025-11-12
- Request temporary admin support budget reallocation (PLN 6,000) — P. Grabowski — 2025-10-24
- Stage grit stations at HQ and depots — M. Zieliński — 2025-10-30
- Anti-slip footwear trial (20 pairs) & review — P. Grabowski / M. Zieliński — trial 2025-10-27; review 2025-11-17
- Finalise contractor snow SLA (PSP/UDT thresholds) — K. Duda / Procurement — 2025-10-28
- Place approved PPE orders (3 quotes required) — P. Grabowski / Procurement — 2025-10-31
- Distribute PPE and retain collection records (RODO) — M. Zieliński — 2025-11-14
- Implement contractor sign-in control / induction improvements — R. Jankowski / IT — 2025-11-07
- Fire drill plan and PSP liaison — E. Michalska — 2025-11-10

Closure
Meeting closed at 12:30. Minutes to be circulated to committee and depot managers. Minutes contain personal health information summaries limited to occupational-health relevant facts in accordance with RODO and internal data protection rules. Document retained in quality drive and BHP records folder (access controlled).

Approved by:
Agnieszka Pawlak
BHP Inspector — Chair, Komisja BHP
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa HQ
Date: 15 October 2025

title: Q3 Operations Review — Minutes of Quarterly Operations Meeting, 08 Oct 2025
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Minutes: Quarterly Operations Review — Q3 2025
Data: 08.10.2025, 14:00–17:00
Miejsce: Board Room, Warszawa HQ

1. Obecni / Attendees
- Michał Nowicki — COO
- Anna Kowalczyk — VP Logistics
- Tomasz Jabłoński — VP Warehouse
- Katarzyna Duda — Fleet Manager
- Paweł Sawicki — Finance Controller
- Monika Szymańska — Customer Success Manager

2. Cel spotkania
Przegląd wyników operacyjnych za Q3 2025, ocena KPI, omówienie kwestii klientów strategicznych, decyzje dotyczące rozwoju siatki tras (ChemTrans), stan floty i planów wymiany, oraz wyznaczenie action items przed posiedzeniem Zarządu.

3. Q3 KPI Dashboard (podsumowanie)
- On-time delivery: 96.2% (target / cel: 98.0%) — omówiono przyczyny niespełnienia celu, głównie zdarzenia drogowe i obłożenie tras miejskich.
- Fleet utilization: 84% — wykorzystanie floty stabilne; rekomendowane korekty grafiku w sezonie świątecznym.
- Fuel cost: EUR 0.38 / km (+5% YoY) — wpływ wzrostu cen paliw, efekty hedgingu paliwowego będą monitorowane.
- Customer complaints: 45 (spadek 12% QoQ) — satysfakcjonujący trend poprawy obsługi, ale konieczność dalszej redukcji reklamacji krytycznych.
- Warehouse throughput: 12,400 pallets/week — wydajność magazynowa zgodna z planem operacyjnym.
- Picking accuracy: 99.7% — utrzymanie wysokiego poziomu zgodności z zamówieniami.

4. Finanse — wyniki Q3
- Revenue: EUR 4.2M (budget EUR 4.5M, -6.7% od planu). Główne odchylenia: niższe przychody z usług ad-hoc na rynku krajowym oraz opóźnione uruchomienia dwóch kontraktów w PL-CE.
- EBITDA margin: 10.2% — poniżej targetu strategicznego, ale akceptowalne przy obecnej dynamice kosztów paliwa i kosztów pracowniczych zgodnie z Kodeks Pracy oraz lokalnymi porozumieniami.
- Finance Controller (P. Sawicki) przedstawił krótką analizę przyczyn odchyleń i możliwych działań korygujących (optymalizacja tras, renegocjacja stawek paliwowych w kontraktach EUR, korekty kosztów stałych w PLN).

5. Klienci strategiczni — sprawy otwarte
- FreshFoods: 3 SLA breaches w Q3 — 2x spóźnione dostawy na trasie Wrocław (przyczyny: korekty rozkładu przeładunków + warunki drogowe), 1x problem z temperaturą ładunku. Kompensacja wypłacona: EUR 1,200. Customer Success (M. Szymańska) omówiła działania naprawcze: dodatkowe szkolenie kierowców, audyt procedur chłodniczych, wdrożenie dodatkowego monitoringu temperatury zgodnie z wymaganiami Prawo Farmaceutyczne dla ładunków „chilled”, oraz aktualizacja SLA.
- PharmaCorp: quarterly review pozytywne. Dyskusja o uruchomieniu 2 nowych tras od Q1 2026 — propozycja: stałe przewozy zabezpieczone procedurami GMP / zgodność z Prawo Farmaceutyczne i wymogami GIF przy obsłudze produktów wrażliwych. Warunkiem startu będzie finalizacja umów i potwierdzenie ubezpieczenia transportowego.

6. ChemTrans — rozszerzenie siatki tras
- Zarząd zaakceptował uruchomienie 2 nowych tras: Płock–Puławy oraz Płock–Tarnów.
- Wymogi personalne i sprzętowe: 2 dodatkowych kierowców z certyfikacją ADR, 1 dodatkowy pojazd ADR. Fleet Manager (K. Duda) potwierdziła dostępność kandydatów do rekrutacji i przybliżony czas wdrożenia.
- CapEx związany z zakupem pojazdu ADR: EUR 180,000 (jednorazowy wydatek inwestycyjny). Zaproponowano finansowanie z linii CAPEX CE-2025; Finance Controller do przygotowania szczegółowego CAPEX proposal dla Zarządu.
- Zgodność: reżim ADR i Ustawa o transporcie drogowym — wymagane aktualne dokumenty, badania ADR, oraz szkolenia BHP/RODO dla personelu zaangażowanego.

7. Flota — wymiany i innowacje
- 3 pojazdy planowane do wymiany w Q4 2025 — lista pojazdów i rekomendowane modele przedstawiona przez Fleet Manager.
- Pilot z elektrycznym samochodem ciężarowym (MAN eTGM) — prezentacja wyników wstępnej oceny techniczno-ekonomicznej (TCO). Key points: ograniczenie emisji i potencjalne oszczędności eksploatacyjne w ruchu miejskim, wyzwania: infrastruktura ładowania, zasięg, koszt CapEx i wpływ na bilans w PLN/EUR.
- Decyzja odnośnie pilota zostaje odroczona i skierowana do posiedzenia Zarządu (Board meeting) z pełnym business case przygotowanym przez K. Duda i P. Sawicki.

8. Ryzyko i zgodność
- RODO: przypomnienie o polityce ochrony danych dla systemów TMS/WMS, konieczność audytu dostawców IT przed końcem roku.
- Prawo Farmaceutyczne: przewozy PharmaCorp wymagają ścisłego nadzoru temperatury i dokumentacji; VP Warehouse (T. Jabłoński) odpowiada za procedury GMP w magazynach obsługujących ładunki farmaceutyczne.
- UDT/PIP/PSP: brak istotnych uwag inspekcji w Q3; nadal wymagane są regularne przeglądy techniczne i szkolenia BHP.

9. Customer issues — szczegóły i działania naprawcze
- FreshFoods: szczegółowy plan korygujący (SOP update) zaakceptowany — wdrożenie do 31.10.2025. Monitorowanie efektów: raport tygodniowy przez M. Szymańska.
- Reklamacje (45): rozbicie wg kategorii i propozycje redukcji (logistyka last-mile, jakość dokumentacji, zabezpieczenie ładunku). Cel: redukcja liczby reklamacji o kolejne 15% w Q4 2025.

10. Action Items (z przypisaniem odpowiedzialności i terminów)
- Paweł Sawicki: Przygotować szczegółowy CAPEX proposal dla zakupu pojazdu ADR (EUR 180K) i opcje finansowania; deadline: 20.10.2025.
- Katarzyna Duda: Rozpisać proces rekrutacji 2 kierowców ADR (job description, harmonogram szkoleń ADR, terminy badań medycznych); deadline: 31.10.2025.
- Tomasz Jabłoński: Zaktualizować SOP dla magazynów obsługujących farmaceutykę oraz harmonogram wdrożenia GMP; deadline: 15.11.2025.
- Monika Szymańska: Przekazać klientowi FreshFoods szczegółowy plan naprawczy, monitorować tygodniowy raport reklamacji; pierwszy raport: 08.11.2025 (cotygodniowo).
- Anna Kowalczyk: Przygotować propozycję korekt rozkładów tras dla poprawy on-time delivery (cel: >=98%); deadline: 31.10.2025.
- Katarzyna Duda i Paweł Sawicki: Finalizować pełny business case dla pilota MAN eTGM (TCO, analiza infrastruktury ładowania, rekomendacja); deadline: 05.11.2025 — prezentacja na Board meeting.
- Michał Nowicki: Zwołać sesję decyzyjną Zarządu dotyczącą inwestycji w pojazd ADR i decyzji o pilocie elektrycznym; termin posiedzenia: w terminie umożliwiającym zatwierdzenie do końca listopada 2025.

11. Decyzje podjęte
- Akceptacja rozpoczęcia tras ChemTrans (Płock–Puławy, Płock–Tarnów) pod warunkiem finansowania CapEx i zatrudnienia ADR personelu.
- Odroczenie decyzji o pilocie MAN eTGM — wymagany pełny business case.
- Zatwierdzenie budżetu na wypłatę kompensacji dla FreshFoods (EUR 1,200) oraz planu naprawczego.

12. Termin następnego spotkania
- Konsolidacyjne spotkanie operacyjne: 03.12.2025, 14:00, Board Room Warszawa — omówienie wyników implementacji action items i przygotowanie materiałów na posiedzenie Zarządu.

Zamknięcie
Spotkanie zamknięto o godz. 17:00. Minutes sporządził: Michał Nowicki (COO) — do zatwierdzenia przez uczestników. Dokument zawiera informacje operacyjne oraz rekomendacje zgodne z obowiązującymi przepisami (Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, ADR, RODO, Prawo Farmaceutyczne).

title: Minutes of the Board Meeting — Decision on Fleet Electrification Investment
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Minutes (excerpt)
Meeting: Board of Directors — Agenda item 4: Fleet Electrification Investment Decision
Date/Time: 2025-10-22, 09:00–13:00
Location: Board Room, Warszawa HQ

Attendees (in person):
- Krzysztof Mazur, CEO
- Małgorzata Witek, CFO
- Michał Nowicki, COO
- Anna Kowalska, Board Member
- Piotr Zieliński, Board Member
- Jan Nowak, Board Member
- Paweł Rutkowski, VP Logistics (invited)
- Ewa Borkowska, Fleet Manager (invited)

Attendees (remote, participated and voted):
- Katarzyna Lewandowska, Board Member
- Tomasz Grabowski, Board Member

Secretary: Magda Sobczak, Corporate Secretary

Agenda item 4: Fleet Electrification Investment Decision
Proposal presented by COO Michał Nowicki (09:45–11:15):
- Capital expenditure: EUR 2,400,000 for acquisition of 12 MAN eTGM 26.360 E electric trucks. Purchase to be executed in three tranches over an 18‑month phasing: 4 units Q2 2026, 4 units Q4 2026, 4 units Q1 2027.
- Business case summary (TCO analysis prepared by Finance/Operations):
  - Variable cost per km: Diesel truck (current fleet average) EUR 0.38/km vs Electric EUR 0.22/km. Figures include energy/fuel, maintenance, tyres differential and planned warranty/servicing assumptions for battery warranty period.
  - Break‑even on incremental investment: projected in year 5 of operation assuming current diesel prices and anticipated electricity tariffs. Sensitivity analysis included +/-20% fuel/electricity scenarios and residual value uncertainty.
  - Strategic drivers: alignment with EU Green Deal decarbonisation targets, increasing customer demand for low‑emission transport (notably FreshFoods RFP sustainability clauses), anticipated tightening under Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and EU regulations.

Subsidy and financing (presented by CFO Małgorzata Witek):
- Grant application submitted to NFOŚiGW (Narodowy Fundusz Ochrony Środowiska i Gospodarki Wodnej) under national+EU co‑financing programmes. Potential grant up to 40% of eligible CapEx -> up to EUR 960,000. Decision expected by Q1 2026.
- Net company CapEx exposure (if subsidy awarded): EUR 1,440,000. If subsidy not awarded, company exposure: EUR 2,400,000. Options for bridge financing and leasing alternatives were presented.

Charging infrastructure (presented by Fleet Manager E. Borkowska):
- Proposal to install six (6) DC fast chargers, 150 kW each, split between Warszawa HQ depot (4 chargers) and Katowice depot (2 chargers). Total estimated cost: EUR 420,000 (procurement + civil works + grid connection). Preferred vendor/installer: Ekoenergetyka‑Polska (proposal under commercial review). Estimates include necessary upgrades to medium voltage connection and transformer works; final scope subject to distribution operator (DSO) requirements and Ustawa Prawo Energetyczne compliance.

Operational considerations & risks (discussion led by VP Logistics P. Rutkowski):
- Range: quoted WLTP/realistic operating range for MAN eTGM is approximately 300 km per charge in operational configuration vs ~800 km range for conventional diesel tractor. This presents route viability issues for intercity and trunk runs (notably Gdańsk corridor and some Poznań–Wrocław rotations).
- Charging time: typical DC fast charge ~2 hours to 80% vs diesel refuelling ~10 minutes. Operational planning required (shift planning, charging windows, possible night charging). Impact on driver duty cycles and Kodeks Pracy considerations for shift scheduling noted; engagement with Zespół HR/BHP required.
- Cold weather impact: conservative planning assumes up to -20% range reduction at low ambient temperatures; additional battery thermal management energy consumption accounted for in TCO stress tests.
- Resale value: secondary market for heavy electric trucks is immature; residual value risk included in scenario analysis. Warranty coverage and buyback options to be negotiated with manufacturer.
