- Regulatory/compliance: adherence to RODO for telematics data, Prawo Farmaceutyczne considerations for temperature‑controlled loads, and UDT/PiP notifications where applicable for charging installations and work safety (BHP).

Pilot deployment & timeline (approved staging proposal):
- Phase 1 (Pilot): First 4 vehicles delivered Q2 2026 to Warszawa depot. Deployed on urban and peri‑urban FreshFoods routes with daily ranges below 200 km in order to validate energy consumption, charging patterns and drivers' acceptance.
- Phase 2: Next 4 vehicles Q4 2026 to Katowice depot, serving regional distribution in Śląskie voivodeship and supporting intersite transfers where feasible.
- Phase 3 (conditional): Final 4 vehicles Q1 2027 to be deployed for longer intercity rotations only if pilot data demonstrates sufficient real‑world range and operational reliability. Intercity deployment contingent on either improved battery performance, route reconfiguration, or in‑route opportunistic charging availability.

Decision, vote and conditions (recorded at 12:45):
- Motion: Approve the proposed CapEx EUR 2.4M for acquisition of 12 MAN eTGM 26.360 E electric trucks, proceed with formal contracts subject to conditions below, and award charging infrastructure contract to Ekoenergetyka‑Polska pending final commercial and DSO approvals.
- Vote result: approved 8 in favour, 2 against; therefore motion carried.
- Conditions attached to approval (unanimous among majority):
  1. Finalisation of NFOŚiGW grant decision confirming at least part of the 40% subsidy. If grant awarded at or above 30% threshold, proceed. If grant <30% or denied, Board to reconvene for re‑approval of revised financing structure.
  2. Pilot evaluation report after 6 months of Phase 1 operation (data pack to include energy consumption per route, downtime for charging, driver feedback, maintenance events, TCO update and residual value assumptions). Continued roll‑out contingent on satisfactory pilot performance.
  3. Execution of contracts to include battery warranty terms, manufacturer uptime commitments, and contractual clauses addressing residual value risk and service level agreements (SLA) for charging infrastructure installation and maintenance.

Dissenting view (recorded):
- Two Board Members voted against (Katarzyna Lewandowska and Tomasz Grabowski — dissent recorded). Primary rationale: concern that current range (300 km) and cold‑weather degradation will not meet operational needs on Gdańsk route and northern Pomorskie rotations. They requested additional analysis of route reconfiguration, interim dual‑fuel strategies or extended-range leasing options prior to committing full order.

Action items and responsibilities:
- CFO (M. Witek): Confirm financing path and prepare contingency plan if NFOŚiGW grant is below threshold. Target: detailed financing memo by 2025‑11‑20.
- COO (M. Nowicki) & Fleet Manager (E. Borkowska): Finalise pilot operational plan, driver training programme, telematics configuration (RODO review), and charging time slot scheduling. Deliver pilot SOPs by 2026‑02‑15.
- VP Logistics (P. Rutkowski): Produce route‑level energy consumption estimates and provisional reconfiguration proposals for Gdańsk route to present at pilot evaluation.
- Legal & Procurement: Negotiate terms with MAN (purchase contract) and Ekoenergetyka‑Polska (installation contract) including warranties, penalties, and SLAs. Submit draft contracts for Board review by 2026‑01‑30.
- Corporate Secretary (M. Sobczak): Circulate these minutes and schedule Board reconvening contingent on subsidy decision.

Closure: Meeting item concluded at 13:00. Next review: Pilot evaluation meeting scheduled for Q4 2026 (exact date to be set following Phase 1 commencement).

Minutes prepared by: Magda Sobczak, Corporate Secretary
Approved by: Krzysztof Mazur, CEO (signature on file)

Distribution: Board members (full), Executive Team, VP Logistics, Fleet Manager, CFO Office, Legal & Procurement
Confidentiality: Internal — restricted. Contains commercially sensitive procurement and strategy information. Compliance notes: treatment of personal data under RODO; technical data and contract drafts classified as restricted per company policy.

title: Weekly Logistics Huddle — Dispatch Standup Notes (03.11.2025)
text:
Date/Time: Monday 2025-11-03, 08:00-08:15 — Standing at dispatch board
Location: Warszawa HQ dispatch board (quick standup)
Present: dispatch team (4), fleet coordinator, warehouse shift lead

Quick headline summary (keepers):
- Drivers available: 28 of 32 (3 on planned leave, 1 sick — Paweł Nowak, expected back Wednesday). We are therefore short 4 drivers from full rota but coverage is OK for today with some double-ups on longer runs.
- Peak season alert: volume forecast +35% for weeks 47-51 (mid-November to mid-December). Temporary agency drivers (4) start Wednesday — 3 from Adecco, 1 from Randstad. We must block induction/BHP slots and update RODO consent forms for temps before they get keys/access.
- Route change: R-017 (Warszawa–Łódź) closed Tue–Thu for bridge works near Rawa Mazowiecka. Detour via S8 adds approx +45 minutes. Affected manifests rerouted and customers notified. Expect knock-on timing shifts for afternoon returns.
- Vehicle status: KAT-5R78 in workshop (brake pads), ETA back Tuesday. WAW-3T42 annual inspection (przegląd) booked Thursday at TÜV Rheinland Polska.
- Customer request: FreshFoods asked to move Kraków store delivery window earlier to 04:30 (from 05:00) starting next week. Checking driver availability and duty time considerations (Kodeks Pracy breaks/rest). Will confirm by Wed.
- Open tech issue: trailer GPS tracker on WAW-NZ12 intermittent. IT ticket #4521 is open with vendor; tracker firmware/logs sent. Keep this trailer off high-risk loads until resolved.

Notes from the standup (informal):
- Roll call and quick mood check — everyone present, coffees were in hand. Short, practical items only.
- Paweł Nowak is off sick (fever). He reported he is seeing doctor and should be fit to return Wednesday. Dispatch will not assign him until his fit note is provided as per Kodeks Pracy procedures. Fleet coordinator to log sick leave in HR system and flag for payroll.
- Three drivers on planned leave (annual leave) this week; their routes covered by floaters and two shorter suburban runs were swapped. We still have 28 drivers available today; note that with the peak forecast we need to avoid letting any further planned leave roll into weeks 47–51 unless exceptional.

Peak season actions (weeks 47–51):
- Temporary staff: 3 from Adecco, 1 from Randstad start Wednesday. Warehouse shift lead to book induction slots with BHP (safety induction and site rules) for Wed morning (08:00–10:00) and Wed afternoon (14:00–16:00) to cover all four temps. Fleet coordinator to arrange vehicle/key induction and distribute PPE (odblaskowe kamizelki, rękawice). HR to ensure RODO consent and copies of driving licences are uploaded to personnel folder.
- Crew rostering: dispatch will produce draft rota by Tuesday EOD that adds temps into early morning Kraków/Lodz runs as needed. We need to ensure compliance with driving hours and breaks (Kodeks Pracy and applicable EU rules). If necessary, use depot flex at Katowice and Wroclaw to absorb overflow.
- Fuel and ad hoc equipment: expect higher fuel draw; procurement to pre-authorise two additional fuel cards per depot for weeks 47–51. Warehouse lead asked to check pallet wrap and load securement stock levels — reorder if below min par.

R-017 closure: Tuesday–Thursday
- Details: bridge works near Rawa Mazowiecka will close R-017 for structural works. Detour via S8 adds ~45 min per trip depending on traffic. This affects all Warszawa–Łódź runs and return legs.
- Actions: dispatch already updated manifest ETAs; customers on those lanes have been notified (email and SMS) and briefed on potential early morning delays. Drivers on Tuesday–Thursday have been given revised route maps and told to set alternative fuel stops if needed. Fleet coordinator to log extra mileage for reimbursement/maintenance checks.
- Note: suggest avoiding tight back-to-back appointments on those legs Tue–Thu; if warehouse needs to delay departures by 15–30 min to avoid peak S8 congestion, communicate to customers asap.

Vehicle updates and workshop:
- KAT-5R78: in Katowice workshop for brake pads replacement; mechanic estimates back Tuesday morning. Dispatch to hold a spare trailer for its planned load if delay extends.
- WAW-3T42: przegląd annualny booked Thursday at TÜV Rheinland Polska (Warszawa). Plan is to send it in early Thursday and use WAW spare (WAW-2P11) as substitute for morning runs. Fleet coordinator to confirm booking and drop-off/pick-up times.
- Regular checks: tyre pressure and load securement spot checks to be done this week by fleet coordinator across all depots.

Customer notes / ad hoc requests:
- FreshFoods (Kraków stores): requested earlier delivery window moving to 04:30 starting next week. Dispatch to check who can do earlier starts without breaching rest rules. If not enough drivers, suggest trial for two days with overtime pay cleared by finance. Warehouse to prepare loads earlier (03:30 ready) if drivers available.
- Other customers: no new SLA penalties flagged this week. Keep log of any delays related to R-017 closure for potential contract leniency discussions.

IT / equipment issues:
- Trailer WAW-NZ12 GPS tracker intermittent. IT ticket #4521 opened; vendor replied asking for last 72h logs — sent by fleet coordinator. Until stable, classify this trailer as 'low visibility' and avoid high-value loads. If tracker fails again, swap trailer.
- Handheld scanners: a couple of units in Poznań depot reported lag. Warehouse lead to collate serial numbers and open RMA if hardware.

Safety / compliance reminders:
- BHP induction for temps must include cold-chain protocols and Prawo Farmaceutyczne reminders for pharma loads if they will handle medical shipments. BHP and warehouse lead to sign off inductions and submit attendee list.
- RODO: temporary staff must sign data handling and privacy forms before being given access to manifests and customer details.

Action items (short list):
1) Fleet coordinator: confirm KAT-5R78 return Tuesday and WAW-3T42 przegląd drop-off/pick-up times (due Tue EOD). (Owner: Marek)
2) Warehouse shift lead: arrange BHP induction slots Wed morning/afternoon; prepare early loading window for FreshFoods if drivers available (Owner: Kasia)
3) Dispatch: draft rota for weeks 47–51 incorporating 4 temps; publish provisional shifts by Tue 12:00. (Owner: Monika)
4) HR: upload temp documents, RODO consents, and validate licences before Wed start. (Owner: Ania)
5) IT: follow up on ticket #4521, request hardware swap if vendor cannot stabilise signal in 48h. (Owner: Tomek)

Next check-in: quick midweek huddle Wednesday 07:45 to confirm temp inductions complete and Paweł's status. If any major changes (vehicle delays, driver shortages) we escalate to operations manager.

End of standup. Short, focused, action-led. Keep the board updated and add any new issues to the digital Kanban. Thanks team.

title: IT Steering Committee — Meeting Minutes: WMS Upgrade (SAP EWM 9.5 → SAP S/4HANA EWM 2023)
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — IT Steering Committee
Meeting Minutes: WMS Upgrade (SAP EWM 9.5 → SAP S/4HANA EWM 2023)
Date: 30 October 2025
Time: 14:00–16:00
Location: Conference Room A, Warszawa HQ (hybrid — SAP vendor representative remote)
Prepared by: Beata Zając, IT Manager

1. Meeting purpose
Przeprowadzenie przeglądu projektu migracji WMS z obecnego SAP EWM 9.5 do SAP S/4HANA EWM 2023; potwierdzenie harmonogramu pilota i wdrożenia, budżetu, kluczowych ryzyk oraz kryteriów go/no‑go przed planowanym pilotem w magazynie Katowice (Warehouse B).

2. Attendees
- Rafał Kowalski — CTO
- Beata Zając — IT Manager (minutes taker)
- Damian Lis — WMS Product Owner
- Tomasz Jabłoński — VP Warehouse
- Sylwia Nowak — Change Manager
- SAP (vendor representative) — remote
Regrets: none

3. Agenda
- Overview of technical approach and EWM 2023 capabilities
- Pilot plan: Warehouse B Katowice (January 2026)
- Full rollout plan: all sites (March 2026) with parallel run 2 weeks/site
- Budget review (EUR 180K total)
- Risks & mitigations (scanner firmware, integrations, training)
- Data migration scope and validation
- Go/no‑go criteria and decision timeline
- Action items and next steps

4. Executive summary
CTO opened the meeting emphasising strategic importance: move to S/4HANA EWM 2023 to achieve real‑time inventory visibility, mobile‑first UI, improved pick path optimization (estimated 15% efficiency gain) and future integration with automation (AMR). Vendor presented high‑level architecture and integration points. Project remains on the targeted timeline but several critical dependencies must be resolved before go/no‑go (notably integration tests and scanner RF framework compatibility).

5. Timeline (confirmed)
- Pilot: Warehouse B, Katowice — start January 2026
- Full rollout: all depots (Warszawa HQ, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań) — March 2026
- Parallel run: 2 weeks per site (production|parallel) during each site cutover
- Note: final go/no‑go decision scheduled for December 2025 IT Steering Committee meeting (date to be confirmed)

6. Budget (approved baseline)
Total budget: EUR 180,000
- License uplift (SAP): EUR 60,000
- Implementation partner (contracted services): EUR 90,000
- Internal costs (IT hours, travel, change management): EUR 30,000
Finance will track in EUR for contracts; internal chargebacks to sites will be converted to PLN per monthly FX rate for internal reporting.

7. Key improvements / expected benefits
- Real‑time inventory visibility across Poland and Central Europe nodes
- Pick path optimization: estimated 15% efficiency gain (baseline established by Warehouse KPIs)
- Mobile‑first UI for warehouse operators (improved ergonomics on Zebra devices)
- Foundation for future automation integration (AMR robots, conveyor controls)
- Better analytics and reporting in S/4HANA

8. Risks & mitigations (discussion)
- Scanner firmware compatibility (Zebra TC52): identified as P1/P2 risk. Requirement: perform tests with new SAP RF framework and Zebra firmware. Owner: Damian Lis / Logistics IT. Deadline for test plan: 15 November 2025. Mitigation: create fallback RF profile and keep a pool of updated TC52 devices for pilot.
- Staff retraining: estimated 8 hours per user × 85 users = 680 training hours. Change Manager (Sylwia) to prepare training plan, materials and schedule; target completion before pilot: 10 January 2026. Use train‑the‑trainer model at each site.
- Integration points with custom fleet app: 12 API endpoints to retest (fleet allocation, ETA, telemetry). Owner: Beata Zając + vendor. All 12 endpoints to pass regression by 30 November 2025.
- Data migration completeness: large volumes (see section 9). Validation scripts and reconciliation needed. Owner: implementation partner with Damian oversight.
- Regulatory / data protection: migration of personal data (operators, drivers) must comply with RODO and internal data retention policies. Legal to be consulted as needed.

9. Data migration scope
- 450,000 location records (bin locations, zones)
- 12,000 material masters (with batch/serial attributes where applicable)
- 3 years transaction history (goods movements, inventory adjustments, counts)
Data migration approach: extract → transform → load with reconciliation reports. Snapshot taken 7 days prior to pilot. Full migration dry run to be completed by 15 December 2025. Migration acceptance criteria: zero critical discrepancies and sample sign‑off from Warehouse (Tomasz Jabłoński) on reconciliation reports.

10. Integration and testing
- Integration test suite includes 12 vendor/custom API endpoints; cross‑system scenarios; performance tests for peak load. All integration tests must pass (no failures) prior to go/no‑go.
- User Acceptance Testing (UAT): 5 business days scheduled during pilot readiness phase; business users to execute core flows (receipts, picking, packing, shipping, returns).

11. Go / No‑Go criteria (agreed)
- All 12 integration tests pass (100%)
- UAT completed: minimum 5 business days with documented test cases and business sign‑off
- No P1 defects open; P2 defects must have agreed mitigation/rollback
- Data migration validated with reconciliation reports and sample verification
- Training for pilot site users complete

12. Decision (current)
The Committee did not approve an immediate go decision. Decision: go/no‑go deferred to the December 2025 IT Steering Committee meeting, pending completion and verification of integration test results, final migration dry run, and completed UAT plan. Formal go/no‑go meeting will re‑evaluate status against the criteria in section 11.

13. Action items (owners & deadlines)
1) Integration test execution (12 endpoints) — Owner: Beata Zając / IT Integration Lead; Deadline: 30 November 2025. Status: in progress.
2) Zebra TC52 RF framework test plan & firmware validation — Owner: Damian Lis; Deadline: 15 November 2025. Deliverable: test report and fallback profile.
3) Training plan and schedule (680 hours) + training materials — Owner: Sylwia Nowak; Deadline: 10 January 2026. Deliverable: train‑the‑trainer roster and LMS content.
4) Migration dry run (locations, material masters, transaction history) & reconciliation — Owner: Implementation partner (SAP partner) with Damian oversight; Deadline: 15 December 2025. Deliverable: reconciliation report and sample sign‑offs.
5) UAT test case pack and schedule (5 business days) — Owner: Damian Lis & Tomasz Jabłoński; Deadline: 01 December 2025.
6) Budget tracking & FX conversion process — Owner: Finance (cc: Beata Zając); Deadline: 10 November 2025. Deliverable: monthly financial tracker.
7) Vendor contract confirmation of SLA for post‑go support and P1 response — Owner: Rafał Kowalski; Deadline: 20 November 2025.
8) Pilot readiness checklist (infrastructure, network, AMR future hooks) — Owner: IT Ops; Deadline: 05 January 2026.
9) Prepare December go/no‑go package (integration test results, migration report, UAT sign‑off, risk register) — Owner: Beata Zając; Deadline: 10 December 2025.

14. Next meeting
- IT Steering Committee (go/no‑go review): December 2025 (exact date TBC). Chair: Rafał Kowalski.

15. Closing
CTO closed the meeting at 16:00 emphasising that the upgrade delivers strategic benefits (efficiency, real‑time control, automation readiness) but must be executed with discipline. All owners confirmed awareness of deadlines. Minutes to be shared with Logistics leadership and Finance. Note on compliance: all data handling to follow RODO and internal security policies; any escalation to be routed through CTO.

Distribution: Attendees, Logistics leadership, Finance, Legal (for RODO confirmation), IT Ops, SAP implementation partner.

End of minutes.

title: Works Council Negotiation Update — Zakładowy Układ Zbiorowy Pracy (Runda 4)
text:
CONFIDENTIAL — Do użytku wyłącznie stron negocjacji; nie do rozpowszechniania poza uczestnikami

LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (siedziba: Warszawa) — Aktualizacja negocjacji Związków Zawodowych
Spotkanie nr: Runda 4 — Zakładowy Układ Zbiorowy Pracy dla pracowników magazynowych
Data i czas: 2025-10-25, 10:00–12:00
Miejsce: Sala konferencyjna HR, drzwi zamknięte
Okres obowiązywania proponowanego Układu: 2026–2027

Uczestnicy:
- Joanna Grabowska — Dyrektor HR, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
- Michał Nowicki — VP Operations, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
- Mec. Katarzyna Wiśniewska — Radca prawny (reprezentacja pracodawcy)
- Stanisław Kowal — Przedstawiciel NSZZ "Solidarność" (Zakładowy związek zawodowy)
- Maria Lewandowska — Przedstawicielka OPZZ (Zakładowy związek zawodowy)

Przedmiot spotkania:
Kontynuacja negocjacji Zakładowego Układu Zbiorowego Pracy (CBA) dla personelu magazynowego. Runda 4 — omówienie postulatów związków oraz stanowiska pracodawcy, wstępne porozumienia oraz zaplanowanie następnych kroków w procesie negocjacyjnym.

Ramowe postulaty związków (stan początkowy):
- Podwyżka płac o 5,5% (postulat główny) — żądanie związków wobec stawki bazowej.
- Dodatkowe 2 dni urlopu wypoczynkowego rocznie.
- Podwyższenie dodatku nocnego z obecnych 25% do 35% stawki godzinowej.
- Gwarancja utrzymania miejsc pracy przez 3 lata (klauzula bezpieczeństwa zatrudnienia).

Stanowisko pracodawcy (oferta wyjściowa i uzasadnienie):
- Propozycja podwyżki zasadniczej: 3,2% — uzasadniona prognozą inflacji GUS oraz koniecznością zachowania równowagi kosztowej całego łańcucha operacyjnego. Oferta spójna z analizą finansową oraz budżetem wynagrodzeń na 2026.
- Dodatkowy 1 dzień urlopu (jednorazowa zmiana w Tabeli Urlopowej).
- Dodatkowe wynagrodzenie za pracę w porze nocnej: 30% (podwyższenie wobec aktualnych 25%, kompromis między postulatem związkowym a możliwościami budżetowymi).
- Brak możliwości wprowadzenia trzyletniej gwarancji zatrudnienia — sprzeczne z koniecznością elastyczności operacyjnej (sezonowość, zmienne zlecenia międzynarodowe, obowiązek wykonywania przewozów zgodnie z Ustawą o transporcie drogowym oraz umowami z klientami). Pracodawca wskazał również ograniczenia wynikające z Kodeksu Pracy i zasad prowadzenia restrukturyzacji.

Postęp rozmów (wynik rundy 4):
1) Dodatki nocne — porozumienie: strony zgodziły się na podwyższenie dodatku nocnego do 30% (zobowiązanie wpisane do redakcji Układu). Jest to zapis akceptowany przez obie strony; implementacja przewidziana od 01.01.2026 z rozliczeniem wyrównawczym za miesiąc wdrożenia.
2) Urlop wypoczynkowy — porozumienie: przyznanie 1 dodatkowego dnia urlopu rocznie dla wszystkich pracowników objętych Układem, zgodnie z zapisami Kodeksu Pracy i procedurą wnioskowania o urlop. Wejście w życie: 01.01.2026.
3) Elastyczne harmonogramy — porozumienie: wprowadzono mechanizm harmonogramowania z wyprzedzeniem 2 tygodni (14 dni kalendarzowych) jako zasadę obowiązującą w standardowych warunkach. Zapewniono też zapis o możliwości krótszego wyprzedzenia w przypadkach siły wyższej lub nagłych potrzeb operacyjnych (z zachowaniem RODO w komunikacji danych osobowych pracowników oraz zgodnie z przepisami o czasie pracy zawartymi w Kodeksie Pracy).
4) Pytanie o gwarancje zatrudnienia — pozostaje otwarte: pracodawca utrzymuje stanowisko sprzeciwu wobec 3-letniej gwarancji; strony uzgodniły jednak zapis dotyczący konsultacji w przypadku planowanych zwolnień (procedura informowania i konsultacji zgodnie z Kodeksem Pracy i praktykami dialogu społecznego).
5) Wynagrodzenia — kwestia nierozstrzygnięta: po kolejnych ustępstwach obie strony zmodyfikowały propozycje. Związek obniżył oczekiwanie do 4,5% (z 5,5%), pracodawca podniósł swoją ofertę do 3,5% (z 3,2%). Nadal istnieje różnica 1,0 punktu procentowego, którą strony kwantyfikują finansowo.

Finansowe implikacje luki płacowej:
- Strony wyliczyły przybliżony wpływ ekonomiczny różnicy 1 p.p. na budżet: ok. 420 000 PLN rocznie (wyliczenie oparte na bieżącej masie wynagrodzeń dla personelu magazynowego; wartość orientacyjna). Oznacza to, że przejście z propozycji pracodawcy 3,5% do postulowanego przez związki 4,5% generowałoby dodatkowy koszt rzędu ~420k PLN rocznie.
- Przy obecnie uzgodnionych elementach pozapłacowych (dodatek nocny 30%, 1 dzień urlopu dodatkowego) koszt wdrożenia oszacowano i uwzględniono w projekcji budżetowej HR na 2026. Szczegółowa tabela wpływu na koszty wynagrodzeń dostępna jest w dziale Finansów (dokumentacja wewnętrzna) i została przedstawiona kierownictwu operacyjnemu.

Ocena ryzyk i stanowisko pracodawcy:
- Pracodawca utrzymuje, iż dalsze zwiększanie propozycji procentowej ponad 3,5% wymaga rekompensaty w innych obszarach lub przesunięć budżetowych, co może wpłynąć na inwestycje operacyjne (magazyny w Katowicach, Gdańsku, Wrocławiu i Poznaniu) i zdolność do wywiązywania się z umów z kluczowymi klientami w kraju i regionie CEE.
- W kontekście żądania gwarancji zatrudnienia na 3 lata, pracodawca zwrócił uwagę na obowiązki wynikające z prowadzenia działalności logistycznej — sezonowość, ryzyko zmiany wolumenów transportowych (Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) oraz konieczność zachowania elastyczności zgodnie z zasadami rynku.
- Przedstawiono też oczekiwanie, że porozumienie zostanie zgodne z Kodeksem Pracy, RODO (w zakresie przetwarzania danych osobowych uczestników negocjacji i pracowników objętych Układem) oraz wewnętrznymi procedurami BHP i Prawo Farmaceutyczne będzie stosowane tam, gdzie dotyczy transportów farmaceutycznych.

Ustalenia proceduralne i następne kroki:
1) Runda 5 negocjacji (ostateczna oferta zarządu) — termin: 2025-11-08. Zarząd przedstawi sfinalizowaną ofertę finansowo-prawną wraz z zaproponowanymi zapisami do Tekstu Układu.
2) Konsultacje z członkami związków — termin: 2025-11-12 do 2025-11-15. Związki zorganizują spotkania konsultacyjne w zakładach (Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań) i zbiorą stanowiska członków.
3) Czas na ewentualne mediacje/uzgodnienia techniczne: w dniach 2025-11-16 do 2025-11-25 (w zależności od potrzeby). W przypadku impasu strony dopuszczają możliwość konsultacji z mediatora społecznym.
4) Docelowy termin podpisania Układu: 2025-11-30 (celem jest sfinalizowanie porozumienia i wejście w życie 01.01.2026, obowiązywanie planowane na lata 2026–2027).

Rekomendacje HR i Zarządu:
- Przygotowanie ostatecznej oferty pracodawcy w rundzie 5: utrzymanie 3,5% jako propozycji płacowej z możliwością wprowadzenia mechanizmu indeksacji w oparciu o aktualizowane dane GUS (klauzula korygująca w przypadku istotnej odchyłki inflacyjnej ponad prognozę).
- Włączenie klauzuli dotyczącej procedury konsultacyjnej w przypadku planowanych redukcji etatów (ma na celu częściowe złagodzenie postulatu gwarancji zatrudnienia przez strony, bez wiążącego zapisu o blokadzie zwolnień na 3 lata).
- Zapewnienie komunikacji RODO: protokół zawiera minimalne dane personalne, notatki będą przechowywane zgodnie z polityką danych osobowych LogiCore oraz obowiązującym prawem (RODO).

Notatka końcowa:
Runda 4 przyniosła znaczące postępy w zakresie świadczeń pozapłacowych i organizacyjnych (dodatek nocny 30%, 1 dzień urlopu dodatkowego, 14-dniowe wyprzedzenie harmonogramów). Kluczowa różnica pozostaje w obszarze procentowej podwyżki płac — luka 1 punktu procentowego (ok. 420 000 PLN/rok). Strony ustaliły harmonogram dalszych działań i dążą do podpisania porozumienia do 30 listopada 2025.

Dokument sporządzony przez: Joanna Grabowska, Dyrektor HR
Zatwierdzone przez: Michał Nowicki, VP Operations; Mec. Katarzyna Wiśniewska, Radca prawny
Podpisy stron negocjacyjnych: przedstawiciele NSZZ "Solidarność" i OPZZ (protokół z podpisami zostanie dołączony do ostatecznej wersji Układu po zakończeniu rundy 5).

Ważne zastrzeżenie prawne: dokument zawiera informacje poufne dotyczące procesu negocjacyjnego oraz danych pracowniczych; rozpowszechnianie poza stronami negocjacji może naruszać prawa LogiCore Sp. z o.o. oraz obowiązujące przepisy (Kodeks Pracy, RODO). Niniejszy materiał udostępniony jest wyłącznie stronom zawierającym rozmowy zbiorowe i ich pełnomocnikom.

CONFIDENTIAL — not for distribution outside negotiating parties

title: Inbound Goods Receiving - Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)
text:
SOP Number: SOP-WH-001
Version: 4.2
Effective Date: 2025-01-15
Responsible: Warehouse Manager (LogiCore Sp. z o.o.) — applicable to all receiving staff
Locations: LogiCore depots — Warszawa (HQ), Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań
Applicable Area: All inbound shipments to all LogiCore warehouses (Poland and Central Europe operations)

1. Purpose
This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) establishes the standardized process for receiving inbound goods at all LogiCore Sp. z o.o. warehouse locations to ensure quality, accuracy, safety, and traceability. The SOP aligns with ISO 9001:2015 requirements for control of externally provided processes, GDP Guidelines (Annex 5) for pharmaceutical shipments, the HACCP plan for food-grade zones, and Polish regulations (Prawo Farmaceutyczne, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, RODO, Kodeks Pracy).

2. Scope
This SOP applies to all receiving activities for inbound shipments handled by LogiCore Sp. z o.o. including palletized freight, parcels, reefer freight, ADR/hazardous goods, and cross-docks. Applies to staff in all depots and to third-party carriers delivering to our facilities.

3. References
- ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.4 (Control of externally provided processes, products and services)
- GDP Guidelines (Good Distribution Practice) — Annex 5 (pharmaceutical receipts and temperature-controlled handling)
- HACCP plan (LogiCore Food-Grade Zones)
- Prawo Farmaceutyczne (Pharmaceutical Law)
- Ustawa o transporcie drogowym
- RODO (GDPR) — data handling for driver and carrier documents
- Kodeks Pracy — employee safety and BHP requirements
- UDT/PIP/PSP guidance where applicable for equipment and fire safety

4. Responsibilities
- Warehouse Manager: overall responsibility for implementation, compliance, training records, periodic review and management of exceptions and claims. Liaison with Sales, Quality (QC), SAP/WMS admin, and Legal.
- Receiving Team Leader: day-to-day execution, briefing staff, ensuring WMS/SAP transactions, raising exceptions and quarantine actions.
- Receiving Staff: perform checks, scans, physical handling, documentation, and report issues immediately.
- QC Officer: performs quality checks, sampling per GDP/HACCP, authorizes release from quarantine.
- SAP/WMS Administrator: maintains system interfaces (SAP MM movement 101), dock scheduling (TMS/WMS), SSCC handling.
- Safety (BHP) Officer: ensures safe handling, forklift competency, and ADR compliance.

5. Definitions
- SSCC: Serial Shipping Container Code
- CMR: International consignment note used in road transport
- ADR: Regulations concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road
- WH-* forms: internal LogiCore warehouse forms
- Quarantine: temporary status preventing stock putaway until cleared by QC

6. Pre-arrival Requirements (T-minus procedures)
- T-12 hours: Transport order received from SAP/TMS (transport order must appear in SAP by T-12 hours). Receiving team checks SAP/TMS for transport and order details.
- Dock slot: Dock slot booked in WMS (auto or manual) — ensure dock availability and equipment allocation.
- Receiving brief: Receiving Team Leader briefs team on expected load, cargo types (pharma, food, ADR), required PPE (BHP), sample plans (HACCP/GDP), and assigns roles.
- Documentation expected: CMR, delivery note, invoice (if applicable), ADR papers for dangerous goods, temperature logs from carrier (if reefer), seals documentation.

7. Procedure — Step-by-step (numbered steps)
1) Vehicle arrival — check-in at gate. Verify carrier identity and driver ID. Record vehicle registration, driver name, carrier company. Collect transport documents: CMR, delivery note, ADR papers if applicable. Ensure RODO compliance when recording personal data; do not store unnecessary driver personal information.
2) Security and safety check — confirm vehicle matches manifest; ensure no visible leaks or hazardous drips and that driver has necessary ADR documentation for hazardous loads (Ustawa o transporcie drogowym reference).
3) Dock assignment — WMS auto-assigns dock based on cargo type and zone (reefer, ambient, food-grade, hazmat segregated bay). Communicate assignment to driver and yard staff.
4) Seal verification (QC) — inspect trailer seal(s). Record seal number(s) on form WH-REC-001 and in WMS. Compare seal number(s) with number on CMR/delivery note. Photograph seal and trailer door. If seal is missing/damaged, follow exception handling; take photos. (QC checkpoint)
5) Trailer inspection (QC) — open trailer; inspect floor condition, interior cleanliness, presence of pests or droppings, and for temperature-controlled units perform temperature check (reefer units — record reading on WH-TEMP-001). Perform odor check for contamination. If pests or contamination present, document and quarantine load. (QC checkpoint)
6) Unloading preparation — ensure forklift operators have valid licenses (up-to-date training WH-TRAIN-001), check lifting equipment and pallet jacks (UDT and maintenance records), position handlers. Confirm pallet configuration and handling instructions on delivery note.
7) Unloading — unload goods by forklift for palletized goods or manual handling for parcels. Count pallets/cases during unloading and mark as unloaded on WH-REC-001. Ensure safe handling per Kodeks Pracy and BHP procedures.
8) Quantity verification (QC) — WMS operator scans each pallet SSCC and compares scanned SSCC counts against delivery note and SAP transport order. Record discrepancies immediately. Use WMS handheld and SAP interface to validate. (QC checkpoint)
9) Quality sampling (QC) — perform sampling per product type: standard goods: 1 in 20 random inspection (min 5 items); pharmaceuticals: 100% inspection per GDP Guidelines Annex 5 and Prawo Farmaceutyczne; food goods: follow HACCP sampling plan and temperature profiles. Complete WH-QC-001 sampling form. Hold samples for 30 days or as specified in HACCP/GDP. (QC checkpoint)
10) Damage assessment — inspect pallets, cases, and packaging. Photograph all damage, tag affected pallets with WH-DMG-001 (Damage Report). Log damage codes in WMS. Notify carrier and record time/date of notification. If damage may constitute safety hazard, isolate immediately.
11) Temperature logging (QC) — for temperature-controlled goods, record arrival temperature on WH-TEMP-001. Compare with acceptable range defined in the product master (WMS/SAP). If outside range, apply quarantine and immediate notification to Quality and Customer. Document with photographs and carrier temperature data. (QC checkpoint)
12) Quarantine procedure — if goods fail QC, temperature, or are contaminated/damaged, create quarantine record in WMS using WH-QUAR-001, move to designated quarantine area, and notify QC and Warehouse Manager. Do not putaway quarantined stock.
13) WMS booking — post goods receipt in WMS (Goods Receipt). WMS posting triggers SAP goods movement (MB01/101 movement) via interface. Verify SAP movement completed and GR is posted against purchase order or transport order.
14) Labeling — affix internal barcode/SSCC labels if missing. For pharma and food, include batch/expiry/temperature stickers per regulations (Prawo Farmaceutyczne and HACCP).
15) Putaway assignment — WMS assigns storage location based on product attributes: ABC classification, temperature requirement, hazmat segregation, FIFO/FEFO rules, and stock rotation. Ensure hazardous materials stored according to ADR segregation rules and local fire code (PSP) requirements.
16) Physical putaway — move pallets to assigned storage location using approved equipment. Update WMS location scan to complete putaway.
17) CMR signing — Driver to sign CMR. Receiving staff sign CMR acknowledging receipt as per documents. Retain copy 2 in LogiCore records, return copy 1 to driver. Ensure signatures comply with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and company retention policy.
18) Documentation filing — attach scanned copies of CMR, delivery note, ADR papers, seal photos, WH-TEMP-001, WH-QC-001, WH-DMG-001 and WH-REC-001 to WMS/SAP transaction and share with customer when required. Retain records according to retention schedule (minimum 5 years for transport & quality records unless otherwise specified by contract or law). Handle driver personal data per RODO — minimal storage and secure access.
19) Carrier notification and claims initiation — for over-delivery, short delivery, wrong goods, or damage, open exception case in WMS and notify Carrier, Customer Service and Legal. Use WH-CLAIM-001 form. Follow timelines in carrier contract (charges in EUR for EU operations where applicable; internal cost records in PLN).
20) Reconciliation — Receiving Team Leader reconciles physical counts with SAP inventory report; investigate any discrepancies immediately; escalate unresolved discrepancies to Warehouse Manager within same shift.
21) Release from quarantine — QC Officer reviews quarantined items after QC testing. If cleared, sign WH-QUAR-001 and release in WMS for putaway; if rejected, coordinate return to sender or disposal per customer instructions and Prawo Farmaceutyczne/HACCP rules.
22) Hazardous goods specific steps — validate ADR documentation, placarding, segregation and storage. Record training of handlers (WH-TRAIN-001) and ensure fire safety (PSP) and UDT compliance for any specialized equipment.
23) End-of-day reporting — Receiving Team Leader submits daily receiving report to Warehouse Manager including counts, exceptions, temperature logs, and QC results. Upload all forms to the document repository (controlled under RODO and company retention policy).
24) Post-receipt actions — update customer notifications (ASN/EDI) and close transport order in TMS. Coordinate with Inventory Control for any stock adjustments or quality holds.

8. Quality Checkpoints (QC)
- Step 4: Seal verification — photo evidence and documentation.
- Step 5: Trailer inspection — internal cleanliness, pest check, initial temperature.
- Step 6: Quantity verification — SSCC scanning vs delivery note.
- Step 7: Quality sampling — as per standard, GDP, HACCP.
- Step 9: Temperature logging — arrival temperature recorded and compared to acceptable range.
QC failures must generate WH-QUAR-001 and be escalated immediately.

9. Exception Handling (detailed)
- Short delivery: Record short-ship on WH-REC-001 and WMS; sign CMR with 'short' annotation; notify carrier and customer; open exception case in WMS; Warehouse Manager to follow-up with Carrier and Purchasing.
- Over-delivery: Accept only with documented customer approval; otherwise hold in quarantine and notify Sales/Customer for disposition.
- Wrong goods: Do not putaway. Document with photos, log in WMS, quarantine, notify Customer Service and Carrier for return instructions. Retain evidence for claim.
- Damaged goods: Photograph, tag with WH-DMG-001, quarantine and notify Carrier and Quality. If damage compromises safety (e.g., leak, suspect contamination), invoke emergency procedures and notify PSP if hazardous.
- Temperature breach: Quarantine product immediately, document with WH-TEMP-001 and carrier temperature log, notify QC and Customer. For pharmaceuticals, follow GDP Annex 5 re-test and disposition. For food, follow HACCP corrective action plan.
- ADR or regulatory non-compliance: Halt acceptance; notify Safety Officer and Warehouse Manager; follow legal obligations under Ustawa o transporcie drogowym and report to PSP/PIP if required.

10. Forms and Templates (List and usage)
- WH-REC-001: Receiving Checklist and Record (seal, counts, signatures)
- WH-TEMP-001: Temperature Log for incoming refrigerated/frozen loads
- WH-QC-001: Quality Sampling and Inspection Record
- WH-DMG-001: Damage Report and Photo Log
- WH-QUAR-001: Quarantine Tag and Release Form
- WH-CLAIM-001: Carrier Claim / Shortage / Over-delivery form
- WH-TRAIN-001: Training and Competency Record
- WH-ADR-001: ADR Compliance Checklist
- SAP/WMS Interface Doc: WH-SAP-INT-001 — describes 101 goods movement mapping
All forms must be completed electronically where possible and attached to the WMS transaction.

11. Record Retention and Data Protection
- Transport and quality records retained per contractual and statutory requirements; minimum retention period 5 years for transport and QC records unless otherwise specified.
- Personal data (driver ID) handled per RODO: records limited, encrypted storage, and access limited to authorized personnel.

12. Training Requirements
- Mandatory induction training for all receiving staff covering SOP-WH-001, BHP (Kodeks Pracy), RODO awareness, basic HACCP principles for food zones, GDP awareness for pharma handling, and ADR basics for handlers of dangerous goods.
- Equipment training: forklift certification, pallet jack, and reefer handling. Records maintained in WH-TRAIN-001.
- Annual refresher training and assessments; change-control training upon SOP revision. Warehouse Manager to schedule and document training.
- Specific roles: QC Officer — GDP/HACCP certified training; ADR handlers — valid ADR driver/handler certification; SAP/WMS users — system transaction training.

13. Performance Indicators
- Receiving accuracy (%): target >= 99.5% SSCC match
- Timeliness: unloading to GR post within 2 hours for standard loads
- Temperature compliance: 100% of temp-sensitive loads recorded and within range at arrival
- Exception rate: <1% of inbound shipments resulting in claims

14. Audit and Review
- Internal audits conducted quarterly per ISO 9001:2015. QC to perform random weekly checks on at least 5 inbound shipments per depot.
- SOP review cycle: annually or sooner after significant process or regulatory change. Version control logged; current version 4.2 effective 2025-01-15.

15. Related Policies
- LogiCore Quality Manual
- HACCP Plan (Food-Grade Zones)
- GDP Compliance Manual (Pharma)
- Data Protection Policy (RODO)
- Health & Safety (BHP) Policy

16. Contacts and Escalation
- Warehouse Manager (Warszawa HQ): primary contact for escalations
- QC Manager: quality and quarantine approvals
- Safety Officer (BHP): ADR and safety incidents
- SAP/WMS Admin: interface and posting issues
- Legal/Claims: for carrier claims and contract disputes

Approval:
Warehouse Manager: [signature]
Quality Manager: [signature]
Effective Date: 2025-01-15
Document Custodian: LogiCore Quality Department

Notes:
LogiCore operates across Poland and Central Europe; contractual terms for carrier claims and billing are handled in EUR for EU customer contracts; internal cost accounting for receiving operations maintained in PLN. All operations must comply with national regulators (GIF for pharma, PIP for labour inspections, UDT for equipment, PSP for fire safety) and contractual customer requirements.

title: Standard Operating Procedure — ADR Dangerous Goods Loading (Załadunek Towarów Niebezpiecznych)
text:
SOP Number: SOP-TR-002
Version: 3.1
Effective Date: 2025-03-01
Responsible: Fleet Manager + DGSA (Doradca ADR)
Location: LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Centrala Warszawa; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań

1. Purpose
This SOP defines mandatory procedures for loading ADR (dangerous goods) consignments onto vehicles operated by LogiCore Sp. z o.o., in order to ensure compliance with ADR 2023, applicable Polish law (Ustawa o przewozie towarów niebezpiecznych), Rozporządzenie w sprawie warunków technicznych pojazdów oraz national safety regulations. The SOP establishes pre-loading checks, loading sequence, documentation requirements, departure checks, emergency procedures during loading and record keeping requirements.

2. Scope
Applies to all employees, contractors and subcontractors involved in preparation, loading, securing and dispatch of dangerous goods transported by road from any LogiCore depot across Poland and Central Europe. Includes international operations where ADR applies.

3. Responsibilities
- Fleet Manager: overall responsibility for vehicle compliance, scheduling, ensuring vehicle Świadectwo dopuszczenia pojazdu ADR is valid and available, and that drivers hold required zaświadczenie ADR. Maintains records and liaises with DGSA.
- DGSA / Doradca ADR: provides technical advice, approves load plans for complex shipments, monitors segregation and packing conformity, verifies emergency instructions and training compliance, performs periodic audits.
- Site Supervisor / Loading Master: executes pre-loading checks, supervises loading sequence, completes checklists, applies door seals, and ensures documentation accompanies the vehicle.
- Driver: verifies personal ADR certificate (zaświadczenie ADR), checks vehicle equipment in cabin and cargo area, signs transport documents and emergency instructions.
- Loader(s): conduct packaging inspections, apply segregation rules, stow and secure packages according to EN 12195 and DGSA instructions.

4. Legal and Regulatory References
- ADR 2023 (European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road)
- Ustawa o przewozie towarów niebezpiecznych (Poland)
- Rozporządzenie w sprawie warunków technicznych pojazdów i zakresu ich niezbędnego wyposażenia
- ADR 8.1.4 (extinguishers) and ADR 8.1.5 (vehicle equipment)
- ADR 5.4.1 (transport document content)
- ADR Table 7.5.2.1 (segregation/incompatibilities)
- EN 12195 (load restraint and lashing criteria)
- RODO (personal data handling for documentation)
- Local fire and emergency services guidance (PSP)

5. Definitions
- ADR: Dangerous goods regulations for road transport
- DGSA (Doradca ADR): certified Dangerous Goods Safety Adviser
- Świadectwo dopuszczenia pojazdu ADR: vehicle ADR approval certificate
- Zaświadczenie ADR: driver ADR training certificate
- Instrukcje pisemne: Emergency instructions in writing (written instructions for drivers)

6. Pre-loading Checks (detailed)
All items below must be completed and recorded on the loading checklist prior to any loading operations commencing. The Site Supervisor and Driver must sign off each completed item.
6.1. Legal/administrative checks
- Verify booking and order details match the transport order, including UN numbers, proper shipping names, classes, packing groups, net mass and quantity.
- Confirm route constraints and any jurisdictional restrictions for transit countries. Ensure required permits are obtained.

6.2. Vehicle ADR certification
- Confirm Świadectwo dopuszczenia pojazdu ADR is valid and present in vehicle (paper or certified electronic copy). Check expiry date. If expired, vehicle must not be loaded.
- Verify vehicle type approval and any special equipment (e.g., tank vehicle certificates) per ADR and Rozporządzenie w sprawie warunków technicznych pojazdów.

6.3. Driver ADR certificate
- Verify driver holds valid zaświadczenie ADR covering the class(es) of dangerous goods to be loaded. Certificate must be carried (physical or recognised digital copy). The certificate must cover any limited quantities, special provisions or requirements.

6.4. Vehicle equipment check (per ADR 8.1.5)
Ensure all required equipment is present, serviceable and readily accessible. Record serial numbers/inspection dates where applicable.
- 2 warning triangles
- 2 self-standing orange warning signs (samonośne znaki ostrzegawcze) if required by loading configuration
- Orange plates (tablice ostrzegawcze) front and rear when required (e.g., materiały niebezpieczne wymagające oznakowania)
- Wheel chock (klocek pod koło)
- Drain seal (zaślepka spustu) for tank vehicles or IBCs where applicable
- Collection container and shovel (pojemnik zbiorczy / łopata) for small spill containment
- Eye-wash bottle or eye-rinse solution and basic first aid items
- High-visibility warning vest (kamizelka odblaskowa)
- Torch (latarka) with charged batteries
- Appropriate protective gloves (chemical-resistant)
- Other PPE as required by SDS (karta charakterystyki) of cargo

6.5. Fire extinguishers (ADR 8.1.4)
- Confirm presence of hand-held fire extinguisher(s): minimum 2 kg extinguisher in cabin (extinguisher dry powder ABE, or suitable class per cargo) AND appropriate additional extinguisher(s) sized for cargo: typically 6 kg or 12 kg as required by type and quantity of goods and ADR requirements. Inspect pressure gauges, seals, and service dates.

6.6. Placards and UN number plates
- Confirm placards and orange plates showing relevant UN numbers are correct, legible and fitted in required positions. For tank vehicles and vehicles carrying multiple dangerous goods, verify that number plates correspond to actual consignments.

6.7. Load area condition
- Check cargo area is clean, dry, free of residues incompatible with incoming goods, and that forklift/truck loading surfaces are serviceable. Any previous contamination must be cleaned and documented.

6.8. Packaging and container pre-checks
- Verify that packages, IBCs and tanks are appropriately marked with UN numbers, proper shipping name, and handling marks. Packaging must be free of obvious defects prior to loading.

7. Loading Sequence (step-by-step)
All loading to be performed under supervision of Site Supervisor and in accordance with DGSA guidance when necessary.
7.1. Segregation check (ADR Table 7.5.2.1)
- Prior to stowage, consult ADR Table 7.5.2.1 for incompatibilities. Segregate classes (e.g., oxidizers vs. flammables, acids vs. bases) and do not co-load packages that are incompatible unless appropriate segregation is physically enforced (separate compartments or impermeable barriers) and documented.
- Special attention for forbidden combinations: e.g., class 1 not near class 7 unless specific conditions met. Document rationale on load plan.

7.2. Packaging integrity check
- Inspect every package for damage, deformation, corrosion, leakage, wet stains, bulging, loose closures, compromised seals or taped repairs. Check UN packaging codes and maximum gross weight markings.
- For bulk tanks and IBCs: check valves and fittings are closed and sealed. Verify drain plugs and vents are operational and secured.
- Reject and quarantine any compromised package; contact shipper and DGSA for disposition instructions.

7.3. Stowage and securing (EN 12195)
- Place heavier and stronger packages on the bottom; position packages to comply with segregation and stability rules. Maintain centre of gravity low and centred.
- Use dunnage, timber blocking, edge protectors and lashing according to EN 12195 to achieve required lashing capacity (LC) and blocking & bracing. Lashing calculations must consider dynamic forces (acceleration, deceleration, cornering) and cargo mass.
- For palletised dangerous goods, secure with straps and shrink-wrap as appropriate, while ensuring access to labels and handling marks is not obscured.
- Use certified lashing equipment only; inspect webbing, chains and tensioners for wear and expiry. Record certificates for lashing equipment used.
- For tank vehicles, ensure tanks are properly supported and secured; use tank restraints as specified by DGSA and vehicle manual.

7.4. Load plan documentation
- Prepare a Load Plan which includes: vehicle registration, driver name and zaświadczenie ADR number, DGSA name (if involved), full list of items (UN number, proper shipping name, class, packing group, number of packages, gross/net mass per item), stowage positions, segregation notes, certificates for tank/containers, lashing method and equipment used, photos of load (mandatory for hazardous loads), and signatures of Site Supervisor and Driver.
- Load Plan must be attached to Transport Document and a copy retained in depot records.

7.5. Door seal application and recording
- After loading and securing, apply tamper-evident door seal (plomba) and record seal number on transport document and in electronic system. Note time, date and name of person applying the seal. If an approved mechanical seal or cable seal is used, record the seal specification and serial number.

8. Documentation Requirements
8.1. Transport document (ADR 5.4.1)
The transport document must accompany the vehicle and include at minimum the following information in language(s) understandable to the driver and the countries transited:
- Proper shipping name (nazwa właściwa przewozowa)
- UN number
- Class and subsidiary risk(s)
- Packing group (if applicable)
- Quantity (net mass, volume) and number of packages
- Type of packaging and/or tank code
- Special provisions and limited quantities (if applicable)
- Name and address of consignor and consignee
- Emergency telephone number
- Technical name (if required by ADR column 14)
- Vehicle registration number and specified vehicle approval (Świadectwo dopuszczenia)
- Any tunnel restriction code
- Signature of consignor or their agent certifying proper packing
8.2. Emergency instructions in writing (instrukcje pisemne)
- Carried in the vehicle and in languages required: language of driver, loader and all countries transited. Instructions to follow ADR 5.4.3 and contain immediate actions (first aid, fire, spillage), firefighting measures, isolation distances, and emergency telephone number.
8.3. Container/tank packing certificate
- If using containers/tanks loaded by third parties, obtain Container Packing Certificate or Cargo Transport Unit Packing Certificate where applicable. For exempted inner packagings or IBCs, ensure applicable certificates or declarations are present.
8.4. Safety Data Sheet (SDS / karta charakterystyki)
- SDS for each dangerous good must be available at the loading site and carried electronically or in print where required by company policy and RODO considerations.

9. Departure Checks (before vehicle leaves site)
- Verify transport document and instrukcje pisemne are present and signed.
- Confirm driver has read and signed load plan and acknowledges emergency instructions.
- Ensure vehicle placards, orange plates, and door seals are correctly fitted and documented.
- Final walk-around by Driver and Site Supervisor to confirm no visual leaks, no unsecured cargo, lashing tension correct and wheel chock stowed or fitted as required.
- Confirm route, permitted times and any local restrictions. If any discrepancy discovered, do not depart until resolved and signed off by Fleet Manager or DGSA.

10. Emergency Procedures During Loading
All incidents must be reported immediately to the Site Supervisor, Fleet Manager and DGSA and to emergency services (PSP) if required.
10.1. Small spill (no immediate threat to people)
- Isolate area; stop loading; prevent ignition sources; wear PPE (gloves, goggles, respirator if required).
- Use absorbent materials and collection container; collect residues into labelled, sealed containers for disposal according to waste regulations.
- Decontaminate area; record incident and notify DGSA. If contaminated water may reach drains, apply spill kits and notify municipal authorities.

10.2. Large spill or release
- Evacuate non-essential personnel to safe distance; establish exclusion zone per SDS and instrukcje pisemne; contact emergency services (PSP) and provide UN number, proper shipping name and quantity.
- If fire risk, only trained personnel with appropriate PPE and extinguishers should attempt firefighting; otherwise await PSP instructions.
- Prevent entry to waterways; deploy booms if trained and equipment available. Document all actions and retain samples where safe and authorised.

10.3. Fire during loading
- Activate emergency procedures, sound alarm, evacuate and assemble at muster point. If safe, attempt initial firefighting with approved extinguishers following instrukcje pisemne; do not fight large fires.
- Provide details to PSP on arrival: class of product, UN number, quantity, tank/packaging type, hazards (e.g., toxic, explosive, oxidizing), and presence of other incompatible substances.

10.4. Exposure / inhalation / contamination of personnel
- Remove contaminated clothing, flush exposed skin/eyes with water for at least 15 minutes, provide first aid and seek medical attention. Inform emergency services and DGSA. Record incident and report to PIP and other agencies if required by law.

11. Training and Competence
- All drivers and loaders engaged in ADR operations must hold appropriate ADR training and current zaświadczenie ADR. Refresher training per ADR schedule and internal company requirements must be provided.
- DGSA will maintain training records and deliver site-specific briefings for complex consignments.

12. Record Keeping
- All completed loading checklists, load plans, transport documents, emergency instructions, photos of loaded vehicle, door seal records, inspection certificates (vehicle, extinguishers, lashing equipment), and incident reports shall be retained for a minimum of 5 years in accordance with ADR 1.8.3.3 and company records policy.
- Records containing personal data shall be stored and processed in compliance with RODO; access limited to authorised personnel.

13. Audit and Compliance
- The DGSA and Fleet Manager will conduct periodic audits of ADR loading procedures and equipment. Non-conformities will be corrected with documented corrective actions.
- Any deviations from this SOP must be approved in writing by Fleet Manager and DGSA prior to loading.

14. Revision History
- Version 1.0: Initial SOP
- Version 2.0: Updated to ADR 2021
- Version 3.0: Revised segregation and documentation procedures
- Version 3.1 (current): Effective 2025-03-01 — updated references to ADR 2023, clarified EN 12195 lashing requirements and door seal recording, strengthened retention period and multilingual emergency instruction requirements.

15. Annexes (available on company intranet)
- ADR Loading Checklist (SOP-TR-002-CHK)
- Load Plan Template (SOP-TR-002-LP)
- Emergency Contact List and Reporting Flow (SOP-EM-001)
- DGSA Approval Form

Approval:
Fleet Manager: _______________________  Date: __________
DGSA (Doradca ADR): ___________________  Date: __________

This SOP must be available at all LogiCore depots and supplied to third-party loaders engaged in preparation of ADR shipments. Non-compliance may result in disciplinary action and legal sanctions under Polish and international transport law.

title: SOP-QA-003 Cold Chain Verification and Control — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
SOP Number: SOP-QA-003
Version: 2.4
Effective Date: 2025-06-01
Prepared by: Quality Assurance — LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa
Applicable to: all temperature-controlled shipments (pharmaceuticals subject to GDP, HACCP food products, and other temperature-sensitive cargo) across Poland and Central Europe

1. Purpose
To define uniform procedures for verification, monitoring, documentation and disposition of temperature-controlled shipments handled by LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ensuring compliance with Good Distribution Practice (GDP), HACCP requirements, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, RODO (personal data protection) and applicable Polish regulatory guidance (GIF, PIP, UDT where relevant).

2. Scope
This SOP applies to all employees, contractors and carriers engaged in pickup, transit, and delivery of temperature-controlled shipments from all LogiCore depots (Warszawa HQ, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań) and to third-party recipients in Poland and Central Europe.

3. Definitions and Temperature Zones
- Chilled: 2–8°C (target setpoint 5°C)
- Frozen: -18°C ±2°C (target setpoint -18°C)
- Deep Freeze: -25°C ±3°C (target setpoint -25°C)
- Data logger: SensoGuard TM-400 used in every temperature-controlled shipment
- Minor deviation: deviation less than 2°C from target and duration less than 30 minutes
- Major deviation: deviation greater than 2°C from target and/or duration greater than 30 minutes
- QP: Qualified Person at customer (pharmaceutical recipient)

4. Responsibilities
- Driver/Carrier: perform pickup temperature checks, install SensoGuard TM-400, ensure photographs and forms completed, maintain vehicle setpoint, respond to in-transit alerts.
- Dispatch: monitor real-time alerts (per TECH-SPEC-001), coordinate corrective actions, notify Quality Manager for deviations.
- Depot/Warehouse staff: verify condition at handover, remove/return data loggers at delivery to dispatch, store records.
- Quality Manager (QM): lead deviation investigations, authorize release from quarantine, maintain GDP records.
- Customer/QP: final disposition authority for pharmaceutical product deviations per GDP.

5. Materials and Forms
- Data logger: SensoGuard TM-400 (see TECH-SPEC-001 for alert thresholds and technical requirements)
- Pickup/temperature form: QA-TEMP-002
- GDP deviation form: QA-GDP-001
- Temperature log printout (generated from SensoGuard at delivery)
- Calibration certificates (NIST-traceable via PCA where applicable)

6. Procedure
6.1 Preparation prior to pickup
- Ensure vehicle and refrigeration unit are operating and alarm setpoints configured per TECH-SPEC-001 and shipment requirements (chilled/frozen/deep freeze). Document vehicle setpoint.
- Verify SensoGuard TM-400 battery level and calibration status. Do not place an out-of-calibration logger in service.

6.2 Pickup verification
- At pickup location the driver or depot representative must: 
  a) Confirm shipment temperature zone and target setpoint (chilled: 5°C; frozen: -18°C; deep freeze: -25°C).
  b) Install one SensoGuard TM-400 inside the consignment where it best represents product temperature (adjacent to product, not in door or air stream). Use secondary logger for large shipments as required by TECH-SPEC-001.
  c) Measure and record temperature at pickup using the SensoGuard display and/or vehicle monitoring system. Record reading on QA-TEMP-002. Photograph data logger display and the loaded pallet/tray showing product and logger (photograph metadata/time must be retained).
  d) Reject shipment at pickup if temperature reading deviates more than 2°C from the target setpoint (i.e., deviation >2°C). If rejected: do not accept cargo into trailer; notify Dispatch and Quality Manager immediately; complete QA-TEMP-002 as rejected and arrange corrective action (return to shipper or corrective pre-cool) before acceptance.

6.3 In-transit monitoring
- Every temperature-controlled shipment must contain a SensoGuard TM-400. Real-time monitoring is active and alerts are forwarded to Dispatch per TECH-SPEC-001 thresholds. Dispatch maintains 24/7 monitoring roster.
- On receipt of an alert (temperature excursion, logger fault, or communication failure), Dispatch shall: 
  a) Attempt immediate contact with Driver and confirm alarm. 
  b) If transient and within minor deviation limits (<2°C and <30 min) document event in QA-TEMP-002, continue transit, notify Quality Manager. 
  c) If major deviation (>2°C or >30 min) follow Exception Handling (section 7).
- Drivers must log corrective actions taken en route (e.g., setpoint change, trailer door closed, stop to investigate) and update Dispatch.

6.4 Delivery verification
- Upon arrival the delivering driver presents a printed temperature log from the SensoGuard TM-400 for customer review. Customer must sign the printed temperature log printout confirming receipt and temperature acceptance. Sign-off is recorded on QA-TEMP-002 and electronically in the transport management system (TMS).
- Remove the SensoGuard TM-400 from the consignment and return to Dispatch for data download. Photographs of logger removal and handover must be taken. Data are downloaded to the central QA server and stored with the transport record.

7. Exception handling and deviations
7.1 Minor deviation (Temperature deviation <2°C and duration <30 minutes)
- Document event on QA-TEMP-002 and notify Quality Manager.
- Shipment may be delivered at the discretion of the driver and Dispatch, provided product integrity is not otherwise compromised. Customer should be informed upon delivery.

7.2 Major deviation (Temperature deviation >2°C or duration >30 minutes)
- Immediately quarantine product. Do NOT deliver without express written authorization from Quality Manager.
- Notify customer within 1 hour of confirmation of the deviation and provide preliminary details (time, extent, actions taken).
- For pharmaceuticals (GDP): any deviation, regardless of magnitude or duration, triggers a full GDP deviation investigation using Form QA-GDP-001. Notify the customer's Qualified Person (QP) and provide full temperature record within 24 hours. The customer's QP determines final product disposition.
- LogiCore will provide all available data (SensoGuard raw data, vehicle telematics, QA-TEMP-002, photographs) to customer and to internal QM for investigation. Maintain chain-of-custody documentation.

8. Data retention and confidentiality
- Temperature records and associated documentation shall be retained for a minimum of 3 years for non-pharmaceutical shipments. For pharmaceutical shipments subject to GDP, records shall be retained for 5 years in accordance with GDP requirements (Annex 9) and applicable national regulations.
- Personal data on forms (driver name, customer signatory) are processed per RODO; access is restricted to authorized personnel only and records are stored in secure systems with role-based access.

9. Calibration and equipment control
- All SensoGuard TM-400 data loggers shall be calibrated at intervals not exceeding 6 months against a NIST-traceable reference standard (via accredited laboratory/PCA where applicable). Calibration certificates shall be held in QA records and available for inspection by regulators (GIF/Inspekcja Farmaceutyczna, PSP, PIP) on request.
- Devices found out of tolerance shall be removed from service immediately; affected shipments shall be evaluated for impact and documented.

10. Temperature mapping and validation
- An annual temperature mapping study of representative vehicles, trailers and storage locations will be conducted in accordance with GDP Annex 9 and HACCP principles. Mapping results shall be used to define loading patterns, logger placement, and operational setpoints.

11. Training
- All personnel involved in cold chain operations (drivers, dispatch, warehouse staff) shall receive initial and periodic refresher training on this SOP, use of SensoGuard TM-400, QA-TEMP-002 completion, deviation handling and RODO requirements. Training records shall be maintained.

12. References
- Good Distribution Practice (GDP), Annex 9 (temperature mapping guidance)
- Polish legislation and guidance: Kodeks Pracy, RODO, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, Prawo Farmaceutyczne; Polish authorities: GIF (Inspekcja Farmaceutyczna), PIP, UDT, PSP where applicable.
- TECH-SPEC-001: SensoGuard TM-400 technical specification and alert thresholds
- QA-TEMP-002: Pickup and temperature record form
- QA-GDP-001: GDP deviation investigation form

13. Records and attachments
- Attachment A: QA-TEMP-002 form template
- Attachment B: QA-GDP-001 form template
- Attachment C: TECH-SPEC-001 summary

14. Revision history
- 2.4 (2025-06-01) — Updated to SensoGuard TM-400 mandatory, clarified pickup reject criteria (>2°C), defined response times (customer notified within 1 hour), data retention 3/5 years, calibration every 6 months, annual mapping requirement per GDP Annex 9.

Approved by: Quality Manager, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Date: 2025-05-20
Distribution: Controlled. Printed copies are QA controlled. Electronic master copy is retained in QA Document Control.

title: SOP-WH-004 — Customer Returns Processing (Procedura obsługi zwrotów klientów)
text:
SOP Number: SOP-WH-004
Version: 2.0
Effective Date: 2025-04-01
Owner: Warehouse Manager, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Approved by: Head of Operations

1. Purpose
This Standard Operating Procedure defines the mandatory process for receipt, assessment, disposition and reporting of all customer returns across all product categories handled by LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (headquarters Warszawa; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań). The procedure ensures compliance with internal quality rules, financial controls and applicable Polish and EU regulations (Kodeks Pracy, RODO, Prawo Farmaceutyczne, Ustawa o odpadach, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym).

2. Scope
Applies to all customer returns (domestic and Central Europe) received at any LogiCore facility, for all product categories including FMCG, industrial, chemical and pharmaceutical goods. Excludes returns managed directly under special client contracts that specify alternative processes.

3. Definitions
- Return Authorization (RET): authorized return document issued by Customer Service in SAP. Return numbers follow format RET-YYYY-NNNN.
- Grades A–D: condition assessment categories (see section 5).
- WMS: Warehouse Management System used to book and track returns and flags.
- DGSA: Dangerous Goods Safety Advisor.

4. Responsibilities
- Customer Service: receive customer contact, validate claim, issue RET number in SAP, provide return label.
- Receiving Team (Bay 11): accept returns, perform initial verification and scan label into WMS/SAP.
- Quality/Inspection: perform condition assessment and assign Grade A–D.
- Warehouse Operations: execute restocking, repackaging, quarantine or disposal tasks as directed.
- Finance: approve manual credit notes for Grade C/D within 5 business days.
- Environmental/Compliance: manage waste register and contractor coordination.
- Customer Success Manager: receive monthly returns analysis.

5. Return Authorization
- Customer contacts Customer Service by phone/email/portal.
- Customer Service validates order and authorizes return when eligible under T&C.
- SAP issues a unique RET number formatted RET-YYYY-NNNN; the RET is emailed to customer with return label and dispatch instructions (complying with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym for DG shipments).
- RODO: personal data shared in this process must be minimised and processed per company privacy policy.

6. Receipt and Initial Verification
- Dedicated returns dock: Bay 11 at each depot. All returns must be routed to Bay 11.
- Receiving staff scan the return label into WMS and verify the scanned RET against SAP authorization.
- If RET mismatch or missing, do not accept; notify Customer Service immediately and quarantine the shipment pending resolution.

7. Condition Assessment and Grading (Quality/Inspection)
- Grade A — Original packaging intact, product undamaged: eligible for restock to sellable inventory.
- Grade B — Packaging damaged but product intact: repackage in repackaging queue, then restock.
- Grade C — Product damaged, contaminated or expired: not restockable; proceed to disposal or recycling.
- Grade D — Hazardous/contaminated (chemical, biohazard, unknown): move to quarantine area; notify Compliance. If chemical, DGSA assessment required before any further movement.
- Pharmaceutical returns: follow Prawo Farmaceutyczne and guidance from GIF; many pharma returns require separate quarantine and destruction protocols.

8. WMS Processing
- Book return in WMS with appropriate reason code: wrong item, damaged in transit, quality issue, customer changed mind, recall, other.
- Apply quality hold flag where applicable (Grade B–D).
- Capture photos and inspection notes in WMS; link to SAP RET number.

9. Financial Handling and Credit Notes
- Grade A/B: credit note auto-triggered in SAP once WMS confirms successful restocking/repackaging; Customer Service notifies customer.
- Grade C/D: credit note requires manual approval by Finance (accounts receivable). Finance must process approval and issue credit within 5 business days of final disposition confirmation.
- All crediting actions must be logged to the RET record and archived per financial retention policy.

10. Restocking and Repackaging
- Grade A items: restock to sellable inventory within 24 hours of inspection.
- Grade B items: enter repackaging queue; complete repackaging and restock within 48 hours. Repackaging performed in designated clean area following BHP (OSHA-equivalent) and Kodeks Pracy requirements for worker safety.

11. Disposal and Waste Management
- Grade C items: record entry in company waste register (Ustawa o odpadach). Tag items with waste code and disposition method (recycling, destruction).
- Licensed waste contractor collects disposals monthly; manifest and contractor certificates retained in Environmental folder.
- For pharmaceuticals, follow GIF-prescribed destruction chains; retain documentation for audits.

12. Hazardous/Quarantine Process
- Grade D items: immediate quarantine in designated hazardous area; restrict access; notify DGSA and Safety Officer.
- DGSA performs risk assessment; if transport required, ensure compliance with ADR and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.
- Notify PSP if necessary for fire/chemical risk; follow PIP guidance for worker safety.

13. Reporting and Monthly Analysis
- Generate monthly Returns Report from WMS/SAP. Report contents: total returns, quantities by Grade, reason code distribution, financial impact (PLN/EUR), and open RETs.
- Deliver report to Customer Success Manager and Head of Operations by 5th business day of each month.
- Perform trend analysis by customer, product SKU, depot and reason; identify recurring causes and recommend corrective actions (root cause, client communication, carrier claims).

14. Records and Retention
- Maintain RET records, inspection photos, credit notes, waste manifests and contractor certificates for minimum 5 years or as required by law.
- Protect customer personal data per RODO; restrict access to authorized staff only.

15. Training and Review
- Warehouse, Receiving and Quality staff must complete returns handling training annually; records kept in HR training matrix.
- SOP reviewed annually or after significant incident; next review due 2026-04-01.

References
- Kodeks Pracy, RODO, Prawo Farmaceutyczne, Ustawa o odpadach, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym.
- Regulatory bodies: GIF (pharma), PIP, PSP. Internal systems: SAP (RET issuance), WMS (tracking).

Appendices
- Appendix A: RET number format and sample email template.
- Appendix B: Return reason codes and mapping to SAP/WMS.
- Appendix C: Waste register template and contractor checklist.

End of SOP-WH-004.

title: Cross-Docking Procedure — Time-Critical Shipments (SOP-TR-005)
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Cross-Docking Procedure for Time-Critical Shipments
SOP Number: SOP-TR-005 | Version: 1.3 | Effective: 2025-02-15
Headquarters: Warszawa. Depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań. Scope: operations across Poland and Central Europe.

1. Purpose
This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) defines the process for handling cross-docking of time-critical shipments (flow-through consolidation) to ensure safe, compliant, and timely transfer of goods from inbound to outbound vehicles without putaway into storage. It supports compliance with Kodeks Pracy (BHP obligations), RODO for data handling, Prawo Farmaceutyczne for medicinal products, and Ustawa o transporcie drogowym for transport documentation.

2. Definition
Cross-docking: goods transferred directly from inbound vehicle to outbound vehicle without putaway into rack/storage. Goods pass through the facility in a flow-through manner under controlled custody until loading completes.

3. Eligibility
Eligible for cross-dock processing: pre-sorted shipments, time-critical deliveries (customer SLA or per contract), and flow-through consolidation loads where manifest pre-allocation exists. Exclusions: unsorted mixed pallet loads, consignments requiring quarantine, quality inspection (unless pre-cleared), hazardous materials without explicit cross-dock approval, consignments subject to Prawo Farmaceutyczne that require controlled chain-of-custody beyond reefer cross-dock rules.

4. Responsibilities
- Warehouse Supervisor (Shift Lead): overall execution, escalation at 3 hours, resource allocation. 
- Receiving Team: inbound checks per SOP-WH-001 abbreviated below.
- Staging/Transfers Team: direct movement and dock-to-dock transfer.
- Loading Team: final loading, CMR issuance (or local consignment note), WMS transactions.
- Fleet Coordinator: confirm ETAs via fleet tracking and pre-assign outbound vehicle.
- Quality (QA): for temperature-sensitive and regulated goods, confirm handling per Prawo Farmaceutyczne and company cold-chain protocol.

5. Pre-arrival Notification
- Minimum pre-notification: 2 hours prior to inbound arrival. 
- Inbound vehicle ETA must be confirmed via LogiCore fleet tracking or carrier TMS. 
- Outbound vehicle must be pre-assigned and confirmed on the cross-dock manifest prior to inbound arrival. Failure to confirm triggers immediate escalation to Fleet Coordinator and Warehouse Supervisor.

6. Dock Reservation
- WMS must allocate adjacent inbound and outbound docks to minimize travel distance and handling. 
- Dock reservation is recorded in WMS as "Cross-Dock Reserved" with dock adjacency flag. 
- For temperature-controlled shipments, assign insulated/refrigerated dock with direct access to both vehicles.

7. Procedure (step-by-step)
1) Inbound arrival — standard receiving checks (SOP-WH-001 abbreviated): document check, seal verification, pallet count and visual damage inspection. Any discrepancy is recorded and signed; minor damages noted on manifest, major variances escalate to QA and Fleet Coordinator.
2) Sorting — units are sorted by destination route/customer according to the cross-dock manifest. Sorting must be completed at the inbound dock or immediate adjacent staging area. Manifest must be physically and electronically available (RODO rules apply for any personal data).
3) Direct transfer — forklift or approved material handling equipment moves pallet from inbound dock to staging position immediately adjacent to outbound dock, minimizing double handling. Transfers must comply with UDT-certified equipment usage and operator BHP training per Kodeks Pracy.
4) No putaway — goods must not enter rack storage. All cross-dock items retain status "flow-through" in WMS until shipped. Any deviation requires Supervisor approval and reclassification in WMS.
5) Outbound loading — loading performed per standard loading procedure; load sequence follows route optimization. CMR (or applicable consignment document) is generated and attached. For domestic / EU operations invoice/CMR in EUR per contract; internal cost tracking in PLN.
6) Maximum dwell time — 4 hours from inbound registration to outbound vehicle departure. This is mandatory for time-critical consignments.
7) Escalation if delayed — at 3 hours elapsed, Warehouse Supervisor reviews and assigns priority resources (additional forklifts, manual handlers, expedite outbound vehicle). If departure cannot be achieved within 4 hours, notify Customer Service and Carrier; record reason in incident log and report KPI variance.

8. Temperature-Controlled Cross-Dock
- Goods must not leave the controlled environment. For reefers: transfer must be reefer trailer → insulated dock → reefer trailer with continuous temperature monitoring. Doors may not remain open longer than technical safe interval documented in temperature log. QA must witness pharmaceutical shipments in accordance with Prawo Farmaceutyczne and GIF guidance if applicable.

9. WMS / SAP Transactions
- Perform goods receipt + goods issue in a single workflow to reflect immediate transfer. Use movement type 301 (or local equivalent configured for cross-dock flow) in SAP to ensure inventory accuracy and zero putaway posting. All transactions must be timestamped and user-attributed (RODO-compliant audit trail).

10. Performance Targets and Reporting
- Target: 95% of cross-dock shipments depart within the 4-hour window. 
- Daily report produced by WMS: dwell times, on-time departures, exceptions. Monthly KPI review presented to Depot Manager and Regional Logistics Director.

11. Safety, Compliance, and Records
- All handling complies with Kodeks Pracy (BHP), UDT requirements for equipment, and PIP inspections. Documents (manifests, CMRs, temperature logs, incident reports) retained per company records retention schedule and RODO retention rules. 

12. Training and Audit
- Staff performing cross-dock operations must be trained and certified on this SOP, forklift operation, cold-chain handling, and relevant regulatory requirements. Internal audits on cross-dock compliance conducted quarterly; corrective actions documented.

Contacts: Warehouse Supervisor, Depot Manager, Fleet Coordinator (LogiCore contact list maintained in intranet).

Change History: v1.0 initial; v1.3 minor clarifications on WMS workflow and temperature-controlled transfers.

End of SOP-TR-005.

title: SOP-TR-006 — Driver Pre-Trip Inspection and Departure Checklist
text:
SOP Number: SOP-TR-006
Version: 3.0
Effective Date: 2025-01-01
Prepared by: LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Fleet & Operations
