Applicable to: All LogiCore drivers prior to every trip (domestic Poland and Central Europe operations)
References: Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, RODO, Ustawa o systemie tachografów, Prawo Farmaceutyczne (for ADR/pharma loads), PIP, PSP, UDT guidance.
Purpose
To ensure vehicle roadworthiness, driver and public safety, regulatory compliance and integrity of transported goods by standardising the pre-trip walk-around inspection and departure procedure for all LogiCore vehicles.
Scope
This SOP applies to all drivers employed by LogiCore Sp. z o.o. operating from Warszawa HQ and depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań. It covers every departure — internal movements, national and international trips.
Responsibilities
- Driver: perform full pre-trip inspection and complete TR-PRE-001 (paper) or digital checklist in driver app before departure; do not depart with critical defects.
- Fleet Manager: review reported defects, authorise repairs and log maintenance actions in fleet system.
- Dispatch: confirm route, accept ETA updates, and coordinate repairs or replacement vehicles if required.
- BHP / Safety Officer: investigate repeated defects, coordinate training, and report to regulatory bodies if needed.
Procedure — Vehicle Walk-Around Inspection (Exterior)
1) Tires
  - Visual check for cuts, bulges, sidewall damage and foreign objects. Check tread depth at several points: minimum legal 1.6 mm, company standard minimum 3.0 mm for departure. If any tyre < 3.0 mm but ≥ 1.6 mm consult Fleet Manager; if < 1.6 mm do NOT depart.
  - Use a pressure gauge to verify tyre pressure on front, rear axle(s) and trailer (where applicable). Record pressures if outside vehicle/tyre manufacturer limits.
2) Lights and Reflectors
  - Verify operation of all lights: low/high headlights, position lights, indicators (left/right), hazard lights, brake lights, reverse lights, side marker lights, rear reflectors and number plate light. Any non-functioning light is a critical defect — vehicle must NOT depart.
3) Body and Chassis
  - Inspect body for new damage since last trip. Report any new structural damage immediately to Dispatch and complete TR-PRE-001.
4) Underside
  - Visual check for fluid leaks (engine oil, coolant, fuel, hydraulic fluids, air system leaks). Small damp spots must be reported; active leaks of oil, coolant, fuel or air are critical — vehicle must NOT depart.
5) Coupling (tractor-trailer)
  - Confirm king pin is locked, fifth wheel secured, trailer landing gear fully raised and locked. Check air and electrical connections are secure and locked, glad hands connected and colour coded. Ensure safety locking pin(s) engaged.
6) Load Securing
  - Verify straps, chains and tensioning (ratchets) are in good condition and fully functional. Edge protectors must be used where straps contact cargo edges. Report missing/damaged securing equipment and do not depart if cargo is insecure.
Cab Checks (Interior)
- Mirrors & Cameras: adjust mirrors and camera settings for full view; clean lenses.
- Windscreen/Wipers: windscreen clean and free of major chips that obstruct view; wipers functional; washer fluid level adequate for expected route/conditions.
- Seatbelt: check for wear and ensure buckle/latch operate correctly.
- Dashboard: start engine and confirm no persistent warning lights (brake, ABS, engine, oil pressure, steering) remain illuminated after warm-up. Any brake, steering or critical system warning = critical defect; vehicle must NOT depart.
- Fuel: confirm fuel level meets minimum departure requirement — at least 1/4 tank. If below, refuel prior to departure unless alternative instructions from Fleet.
- AdBlue: verify sufficient AdBlue level according to expected range; if low, refuel to avoid immobilisation on route.
Document Verification
- Driving licence: valid, correct categories for vehicle type; review expiry date.
- ADR certificate: present and valid if transporting dangerous goods; verify compatibility with cargo and Prawo Farmaceutyczne/ADR rules where relevant.
- CMR (waybill): one for each international shipment; ensure details match cargo and seals.
- Delivery schedule: printed or loaded within the LogiCore driver app; routes and stop sequence confirmed.
- Vehicle registration and insurance documents: present in cab (or digitally accessible) and valid.
- Tachograph: driver card inserted and functioning; set activity to “driving” when commencing movement in accordance with Ustawa o systemie tachografów and company policy.
Route Review & Departure Preparations
- Load planned route into the driver app; verify route for road works, low bridges, weight/axle restrictions and known closures; consult Dispatch for alternative routes if restrictions apply.
- Confirm ETA for all stops via app and with Dispatch. If delays or deviations are anticipated, inform Dispatch immediately.
Departure Scan & Tracking
- At facility exit scan vehicle barcode or QR code at exit gate using the driver app (or terminal). Scanning action triggers GPS tracking activation and marks official departure time in fleet management system.
ETA Confirmation
- Driver must confirm estimated arrival times for all scheduled stops through the app prior to leaving depot. Any changes en-route are reported via the app or by contacting Dispatch.
Checklist Completion
- Complete pre-trip checklist TR-PRE-001: paper form (retain until scanned into fleet system) or digital checklist workflow in the driver app. All items must be completed and any defects logged with photographs where possible. Digital records are retained in accordance with company retention policy and RODO.
Non-Compliance & Critical Defects
- If any critical defect is identified (brakes, steering, lights not working, tyre below legal minimum 1.6 mm, active fluid leaks, coupling failure), the vehicle MUST NOT DEPART.
  - Immediate steps: secure vehicle, notify Dispatch and Fleet Manager, document defect on TR-PRE-001 (paper/digital), take photos, arrange maintenance or replacement vehicle.
  - Repeated or systemic safety issues to be escalated to BHP / Safety Officer; serious incidents reported to relevant authorities (PIP, PSP, UDT) in accordance with law.
Record Keeping & Audit
- All completed TR-PRE-001 forms (digital or scanned paper) are stored for a minimum of 24 months in the fleet management system in compliance with RODO and audit requirements. Fleet Manager will review monthly sample checks and report results to Operations Director.
Training & Compliance
- Drivers receive initial training and periodic refresher on this SOP, tachograph rules, ADR requirements and load securing (BHP training). Non-compliance will be managed under company disciplinary procedures and documented.
Change Control
- Version 3.0 supersedes prior versions and becomes effective 2025-01-01. Any changes require Fleet Manager approval and notification to drivers per Kodeks Pracy consultation requirements.
Approval
Fleet Manager — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Operations Director — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Date: 2025-01-01
Form Reference: TR-PRE-001 (paper) / Digital driver app checklist
End of SOP-TR-006

title: ISO 9001:2015 Internal Audit Summary Report — Warehouse & Transport Quality (Warszawa & Katowice)
text:
LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — ISO 9001:2015 Internal Audit Summary Report
Audit reference: IA-QMS-2025-003
Scope: Warehouse operations (receiving, storage, picking, shipping) and transport quality at Warszawa and Katowice sites
Audit dates: 15–19 September 2025
Lead Auditor: Sabine Fischer (external, TÜV Rheinland Polska, Lead Auditor Certificate #TRP-2019-4521)
Audit team: Sabine Fischer (Lead, external); Jan Kowalczyk (Internal Auditor, LogiCore QMS); Dr. Anna Nowak (External Auditor, GDP specialist)
Standards referenced: ISO 9001:2015 clauses 4–10; GDP (EU Good Distribution Practice) where applicable; relevant Polish regulations (Prawo farmaceutyczne, RODO for customer data, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym) and national guidance from GIF for pharmaceutical handling.
Methodology: document review, process observation, staff interviews, sampling of records. 35 interviews conducted across shifts and functions (receiving, storage, picking, shipping, transport planners, drivers, quality and safety). Samples: calibration records, CAR register, customer complaints, delivery notes, temperature logs, vehicle checklists, personnel training records, GDP temperature excursion records.
Executive summary:
- Overall conclusion: Certification status — MAINTAINS CERTIFICATION
- Non-conformities: 3 minor (NC-001, NC-002, NC-003); 0 major non-conformities
- Observations / opportunities for improvement: 5
- Strengths: robust receiving controls, refrigerated transport monitoring, clear segregation of hazardous/regulated goods, engaged staff and demonstrable management commitment to continual improvement
Summary narrative: The audit of Warszawa and Katowice sites found a generally effective QMS aligned to ISO 9001:2015 clauses 4–10 and GDP expectations for controlled-temperature operations. Processes for receiving, inventory control, and transport planning are implemented and largely effective. Minor gaps were identified in specific records and in timeliness of corrective actions and complaint responses. These are considered handleable through timely corrective action; no systemic failures or major non-conformities were found.
Non-conformities (detailed):
1) NC-001 (Minor) — Clause 7.1.5 (Monitoring and measuring resources)
- Finding: Calibration records incomplete for 2 of 8 temperature sensors at Katowice (site code: KAT). Sensors TM-K-003 and TM-K-007 show calibration due in August 2025 not performed; no calibration certificate on file. Daily temperature logs are present and alarms functioned, but traceability to a valid calibration certificate is missing.
- Evidence: Calibration register, sensor label photos, temperature logs 01–31 Aug 2025.
- Root cause (preliminary): Technician vacancy in Katowice maintenance team during August; calibration scheduling not reassigned.
- Impact: Reduced traceability of measurement assurance for refrigerated storage during the period between scheduled calibration and audit.
2) NC-002 (Minor) — Clause 10.2 (Nonconformity and corrective action)
- Finding: Corrective action register (CAR) not updated since July 2025. Four CAR entries originating Q2 2025 remain marked 'open' without progress notes or implemented verification evidence.
- Evidence: CAR register printout dated 01 Sep 2025; no activity stamps or attachments for CAR IDs CAR-2025-014, CAR-2025-019, CAR-2025-023, CAR-2025-027.
- Process owner: Quality Manager (LogiCore — Quality Department).
- Root cause (preliminary): Inadequate resource allocation for CAR follow-up and deficient discipline in log maintenance following reorganization of Quality team.
3) NC-003 (Minor) — Clause 8.2.1 (Customer communication)
- Finding: Customer complaint response time exceeded defined 48-hour target in 2 of 12 sampled cases. Cases: CC-2025-089 (response 72 hours), CC-2025-103 (response 56 hours).
- Evidence: Complaint register, email timestamps, root cause analyses for the two complaints.
- Impact: Potential customer dissatisfaction and non-conformance to internal customer care KPI.
Observations and Opportunities for Improvement (5):
1) Observation O-001: Digital temperature alarm escalation logs exist but lack documented second-tier escalation matrix (who is notified after site on-call). Suggestion: Implement a documented escalation procedure with names/roles and SLA targets; link to RODO-compliant contact directory.
2) Observation O-002: Vehicle pre-trip checklists at Katowice are completed but several driver signatures are smudged/illegible. Suggestion: Adopt electronic signatures via mobile app to ensure traceability and timestamping; aligns with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym record expectations.
3) Observation O-003: Picking error rate trending down but root cause analyses are not standardized. Suggestion: Standardize a 5-why template in the CAR system to improve corrective measures and management review input.
4) Observation O-004: Training records for temporary staff stored as paper forms; no centralised electronic index. Suggestion: Migrate temporary workforce training records to central LMS or QMS folder to improve auditability and integration with Kodeks Pracy obligations for training BHP.
5) Observation O-005: GDP-specific documentation for pharmaceutical consignments is complete; however, transport contingency plans for temperature excursions refer to external service providers without SLA references. Suggestion: Add signed SLAs or memorandum of understanding (MOU) with contingency providers and reference them in transport SOPs.
Positive findings / Strengths:
- Receiving process: Strong verification at gates, clear segregation of quarantined vs accepted goods, photographic evidence used for exception handling.
- Temperature control: Continuous monitoring in refrigerated zones with automated alarms and historical trend records; integration with TMS for transport-temp monitoring is established.
- Management commitment: Visible top management involvement — QMS resources, investment in forklifts and racking, and regular QBR references.
- Staff competence: Interviewed personnel (35 interviews) demonstrated process knowledge and ownership; drivers displayed familiarity with GDP cold-chain requirements.
Corrective Action Plan (per non-conformity):
NC-001 — Calibration records incomplete
- Root cause: Technician vacancy August 2025; no reassignment of calibration tasks.
- Corrective action: Immediate: Procure external accredited calibration service to retroactively calibrate TM-K-003 and TM-K-007 and upload certificates. Medium term: Update calibration schedule and SOP to require reassignment of tasks upon vacancy; notify central QMS of any planned vacancies.
- Responsible person: Maintenance Supervisor, Katowice — Paweł Zieliński; Quality Manager to verify.
- Deadline: Retrospective calibration and upload by 30 Sep 2025. SOP update by 15 Oct 2025.
- Verification method: Review of calibration certificates uploaded to QMS; spot check of on-site sensors; audit closure verification by Lead Auditor or delegate.
NC-002 — CAR register not updated
- Root cause: Quality team reorganization and insufficient resourcing for CAR follow-up.
- Corrective action: Immediate: Assign CAR custodians for open CARs with mandatory status update within 7 calendar days. Recruit/allocate one FTE for CAR coordination. Implement weekly CAR review meeting and update log entries with evidence.
- Responsible person: Quality Manager — Magdalena Wiśniewska.
- Deadline: Initial updates by 26 Sep 2025; FTE allocation by 31 Oct 2025.
- Verification method: QMS review of CAR entries with attachments; management review minutes showing CAR status; internal follow-up audit.
NC-003 — Complaint response time exceeded
- Root cause: Overreliance on manual routing of complaint emails; lack of automatic alerts for SLA breaches.
- Corrective action: Implement automated complaint triage in CRM/QMS to flag SLA breaches and escalate after 24 hours. Retrain customer service and operations on 48-hour target and recordkeeping.
- Responsible person: Customer Service Manager — Tomasz Brzeziński; IT support for CRM changes.
- Deadline: CRM rule changes and pilot by 31 Oct 2025; staff retraining completed by 15 Nov 2025.
- Verification method: Complaint register audit showing closed-cycle times within SLA for a sample of 20 complaints; system logs of escalations.
Follow-up and verification: Evidence of implemented corrective actions to be submitted to Quality Department and will be verified by internal audit in November 2025 and by surveillance audit in March 2026.
Next audits:
- Surveillance audit (internal/external): March 2026 — focusing on implementation of corrective actions and continued conformity to clauses 4–10 and GDP where applicable.
- Recertification: September 2026 — full-scope recertification audit.
Audit limitations: Scope limited to Warszawa and Katowice warehouse and transport quality processes. Sampling was representative but not exhaustive. No pharmaceutical wholesale audit beyond GDP transport processes was within scope.
Audit conclusions: The QMS at the audited sites demonstrates effective operation and continual improvement culture. Identified minor non-conformities require documented corrective actions and timely verification. There are no findings that would require suspension of certification. Auditors recommend maintaining ISO 9001:2015 certification with targeted follow-up on the corrective actions described.
Auditor sign-off:
Sabine Fischer
Lead Auditor (TÜV Rheinland Polska), Certificate #TRP-2019-4521
Date signed: 26 September 2025
Distribution list:
- CEO, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa
- Quality Manager, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
- Site Managers: Warszawa, Katowice
- Maintenance Supervisor, Katowice
- Customer Service Manager
- HR Manager (training record actions)
- External certification body (TÜV Rheinland Polska) — for surveillance scheduling
End of report.

title: ADR Compliance Self-Assessment Report — Annual Review (ADR-SA-2025-001) — LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
Reference: ADR-SA-2025-001 (ADR 1.8.3.3 annual report requirement)

Prepared by: Jan Kowalski, DGSA (Doradca ds. bezpieczeństwa przewozu towarów niebezpiecznych)
Certificate: DGSA-PL-2021-0847
Position: DGSA, LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Marszałkowska 112, 00-950 Warszawa
Depots: Warszawa (HQ), Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań
Period covered: January 1, 2025 – September 30, 2025
Scope: Road transport operations involving dangerous goods under ADR for Class 3 (flammable liquids), Class 6.1 (toxic), Class 8 (corrosive), Class 9 (miscellaneous). Primary contractual activity under the ChemTrans framework agreement (Central Europe operations). This self-assessment is prepared in accordance with ADR 1.8.3.3 and internal LogiCore Sp. z o.o. procedures.

Executive summary
This ADR Compliance Self-Assessment reviews LogiCore's activities for the period January–September 2025. The assessment covers vehicle certification, driver qualifications, vehicle equipment, marking and placarding, transport documentation, emergency preparedness, training and corrective action follow-up. Overall status is assessed as SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIANT. Two corrective actions are open: renewal of two drivers' zaświadczenie ADR and an update to the transport document template to ensure consistent emergency phone number formatting.

1. Regulatory basis
This review references ADR (European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road), in particular ADR 1.8.3.3 regarding the annual report by the DGSA, ADR equipment requirements (8.1.4, 8.1.5), applicable Polish regulations (Prawo przewozowe where relevant, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym), and internal procedures aligned with RODO for personal data contained in transport documents.

2. Vehicle fleet status
As of 30.09.2025 LogiCore operates a dedicated ADR-certified fleet assigned to ChemTrans operations. Fleet summary:
- Total ADR-certified vehicles assigned: 12
- All Świadectwa ADR are currently valid (expiry dates listed):
  1) WAR-1A23 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 14.11.2026
  2) KAT-2R89 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 02.02.2027
  3) GDA-3T45 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 30.09.2026
  4) WRO-4P67 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 18.06.2027
  5) POZ-5L01 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 21.12.2026
  6) WAR-6B12 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 05.03.2027
  7) KAT-7D34 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 12.08.2026
  8) GDA-8F56 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 09.10.2026
  9) WRO-9H78 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 27.01.2027
 10) POZ-0J90 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 03.05.2027
 11) WAR-1K21 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 16.11.2026
 12) KAT-2L43 — Świadectwo ADR expiry 29.07.2027
All vehicle certificates were reviewed and copies are retained in the fleet compliance file (electronic and hard copy at HQ). No expired Świadectwa were identified within the reporting period.

3. Driver certification and staffing
Total drivers assigned to ADR routes: 30
- Drivers with valid zaświadczenie ADR: 28
- Drivers with expired zaświadczenie ADR: 2 (non-compliant)
  - Tomasz Nowak — ADR certificate expiry: 15.08.2025
  - Piotr Zieliński — ADR certificate expiry: 22.08.2025
Both drivers have been scheduled for renewal training (periodic update) in October 2025; training bookings confirmed with certified training center (certificate of booking retained). Interim mitigations: both drivers have been temporarily reassigned to non-ADR routes until certification is restored to ensure compliance with ADR and Polish Kodeks Pracy obligations for worker safety. Status: NON-COMPLIANT (open corrective action CA-ADR-2025-01). DGSA has recorded individual risk assessment and reallocation notes in personnel files.

4. Vehicle equipment inspections
Periodic inspections conducted in accordance with ADR 8.1.4 and 8.1.5 checklist. All 12 vehicles were inspected during scheduled maintenance cycles. Findings:
- One non-conformity: vehicle KAT-2R89 was found missing the second warning triangle (required spare/secondary warning device for some shipments). Action: replacement part fitted the same day (inspection follow-up documented 11.03.2025). No further equipment deficiencies observed. Records of inspections, checklists and photographic evidence are retained in the vehicle maintenance folder.

5. Labelling, placarding and package marking
Placarding and hazard marking spot-check: 40 shipments randomly selected across Q1–Q3 2025 (domestic and cross-border). Result: 100% compliant with ADR placarding (hazard labels, orange plates where applicable, UN numbers). Drivers and loading staff demonstrated awareness of placarding requirements during supervisory checks.

6. Transport documentation
Sampled transport documents: 50 (bill of lading / TDS / waybills) covering Class 3, 6.1, 8 and 9 loads. Findings:
- 48 documents correct (96%) — included proper UN numbers, proper shipping names, packing group, number and type of packages, gross mass, emergency information in accordance with ADR 5.4.
- 2 documents (4%) contained a formatting error in the emergency contact phone number (international prefix formatting inconsistent). Both errors were minor, did not affect the substance of emergency response, and were corrected at the time of discovery. Action: update to transport document template to enforce standardized international phone format (+48 XXX XXX XXX). Corrective action logged as CA-ADR-2025-02.

7. Emergency preparedness and incidents
- Tabletop exercises conducted: 2 (April 15, 2025; August 21, 2025) involving operations, site BHP, local PSP liaison, and DGSA. Scenarios included tank leakage of Class 3, and a mixed load incident with potential toxic release (Class 6.1). Outcomes: response times and communication chains validated; minor improvements identified and implemented (immediate amendment to spill kit placement at Katowice depot).
- Real incidents: 0 incidents involving dangerous goods release during the reporting period. All near-miss and safety observations recorded in internal HSE system and reviewed monthly by the DGSA and HSE manager.

8. Training and competence
- ADR driver periodic refresher training: completed for all ADR drivers (28 with current certificates) during March 2025 (records retained). Training covered segregation, packing, documentation, emergency procedures and RODO handling of personal data on transport documents.
- Warehouse staff ADR awareness: completed February 2025 for all personnel at depots (Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań). Content covered BHP, basics of ADR marking/placarding, and initial emergency response measures.

9. Compliance status and corrective actions
Overall assessment: SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIANT.
Open corrective actions:
- CA-ADR-2025-01: Renewal of zaświadczenie ADR for Tomasz Nowak and Piotr Zieliński. Mitigation: temporary reassignment to non-ADR routes; training booked for October 2025; certificates to be filed upon receipt. Target closure: 31.10.2025.
- CA-ADR-2025-02: Update of transport document template to enforce international emergency phone number formatting and an automated validation rule in the TMS. Target closure: 30.11.2025.
All other observations closed or implemented during the period (equipment replacement, spill kit reallocation, document corrections).

10. Recommendations from DGSA
1) Prioritise a renewable calendar for driver ADR certificates tied into HR and TMS alerts to prevent lapses; integrate with payroll/rota to avoid inadvertent ADR assignments to non-certified drivers. (Action owner: HR & Fleet Manager)
2) Implement automated validation on transport documents within the TMS for emergency contact format and required ADR fields (UN no., packing group, number of packages, gross mass). (Action owner: IT)
3) Maintain quarterly mini-tabletop exercises at each depot to ensure local PSP and PSP-standby contacts remain current; rotate scenarios to cover Class 6.1 toxic releases. (Action owner: HSE)
4) Continue to monitor equipment inventories during scheduled maintenance and introduce a two-weekly visual check by drivers before departure (driver checklist to be updated). (Action owner: Fleet)
5) Reinforce RODO-compliant handling of driver personal data and transport manifests, ensuring secure transfer for cross-border operations. (Action owner: Compliance)

11. Declaration
I declare that, to the best of my knowledge and based on records reviewed, this self-assessment accurately reflects the status of LogiCore Sp. z o.o.'s ADR compliance activities for January–September 2025 and identifies the actions necessary to achieve full compliance. This report is prepared in accordance with ADR 1.8.3.3 and internal company procedures.

Prepared by:
Jan Kowalski
DGSA — Doradca ds. bezpieczeństwa przewozu towarów niebezpiecznych
Certificate: DGSA-PL-2021-0847
LogiCore Sp. z o.o., Warszawa
Date: 10 October 2025 (report compiled following period-end review of January–September 2025 activities)

Distribution: Management Board, Head of Operations, HSE Manager, Fleet Manager, HR, Depot Managers (Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań), retained in Compliance archive (electronic and printed)

End of report.

title: GDPR/RODO Impact Assessment - Fleet Telematics (Ocena skutków dla ochrony danych) - LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
text:
Reference: DPIA-2025-003
Prepared by: Inspektor Ochrony Danych (IOD) — Marta Kowalczyk, in consultation with CTO and Fleet Manager
Date: 2025-02-10
System assessed: Fleet GPS tracking + driver behavior monitoring (see TECH-SPEC-002)
Organisation: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., headquartered in Warszawa, depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań. Operations across Poland and Central Europe.

1. Cel dokumentu
Niniejsza Ocena Skutków dla Ochrony Danych (DPIA / Ocena skutków dla ochrony danych) została sporządzona w celu zidentyfikowania i oceny ryzyk związanych z wdrożeniem systemu telematycznego monitoringu floty (dalej: "System") zgodnie z wymogami RODO (GDPR) oraz krajowymi przepisami (m.in. Kodeks Pracy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym). Dokument opisuje środki minimalizujące ryzyko, uzasadnienie prawne przetwarzania oraz wynik konsultacji wewnętrznych.

2. Zakres i opis Systemu
System: fleet GPS tracking + driver behavior monitoring (szczegóły techniczne w TECH-SPEC-002).
Funkcje systemu obejmują: rejestrację pozycji GPS w 30-sekundowych odstępach podczas jazdy, pomiar prędkości, wykrywanie zdarzeń gwałtownego hamowania/przyspieszania/skręcania (harsh events), monitorowanie zużycia paliwa, odczyt parametrów silnika przez CAN bus, sygnał czujnika drzwi oraz pomiar temperatury w jednostkach chłodniczych (reefer).
System nie rejestruje nagrań audio w kabinie, danych prywatnych z telefonu kierowcy ani lokalizacji poza godzinami służby (funkcja "privacy mode").

3. Podstawa prawna przetwarzania
Podstawa prawna: Art. 6 ust. 1 lit. f RODO — prawnie uzasadniony interes administratora: zarządzanie flotą, optymalizacja tras, zapewnienie bezpieczeństwa pracowników (duty of care), weryfikacja prawidłowości realizacji zleceń oraz wsparcie zgodności z obowiązkami transportowymi (tachografy, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym). Przetwarzanie jest konieczne i proporcjonalne do wskazanych celów.

4. Kategorie osób, których dane dotyczą
Data subjects: 94 active drivers employed by LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (kierowcy etatowi i długoterminowi współpracownicy). Dane dotyczące pojazdów i parametrów eksploatacyjnych są powiązane z przypisaniem kierowcy do pojazdu.

5. Zakres danych osobowych
Data collected: GPS position (30s intervals while driving), speed, harsh events (braking/acceleration/cornering), fuel consumption, engine data (CAN bus), door sensor, temperature (reefer units).
Data NOT collected: in-cab audio, personal phone data, off-duty location (privacy mode active).
Dane techniczne są traktowane jako dane osobowe w zakresie, w jakim mogą identyfikować pracownika (lokalizacja, przypisanie do pojazdu).

6. Okresy przechowywania i polityka retencji
Retention: 90 days for individual tracking data (reduced from 1 year after Rada Pracowników consultation in Q2 2024). Agregowane statystyki i anonimizowane raporty (np. wskaźniki zużycia paliwa, efektywność tras) są przechowywane indefinitely w formie zanonimizowanej.
Proces usuwania: po upływie 90 dni surowe logi pozycji są usuwane automatycznie; kopie zapasowe są również objęte polityką retencji; posiadają szyfrowanie i limitowany dostęp.

7. Ocena konieczności i proporcjonalności
Analiza wykazała, że przetwarzanie lokalizacji i parametrów jazdy jest niezbędne w następujących celach:
- real-time tracking i informowanie klientów o statusie dostawy;
- monitorowanie bezpieczeństwa kierowców i zapobieganie wypadkom (analiza harsh events, interwencje szkoleniowe);
- optymalizacja tras i oszczędność paliwa (cel środowiskowy i ekonomiczny);
- uzupełnienie dowodów eksploatacyjnych w kontekście tachografów i wymogów prawnych.
Środki zaprojektowane w Systemie minimalizują ingerencję w prywatność (np. privacy mode), ograniczają zakres danych do niezbędnego oraz stosują pseudonimizację na poziomie raportów.

8. Ocena ryzyka i wpływu na prawa i wolności osób
Wstępna ocena: MEDIUM risk — stały monitoring lokalizacji pracowników stanowi istotne ryzyko dla prawa do prywatności i autonomii pracownika.
Potencjalne negatywne skutki: nadmierna inwigilacja, wykorzystanie danych w postępowaniach dyscyplinarnych bez właściwej procedury, nieautoryzowany dostęp, wyciek danych lokalizacyjnych.

9. Środki ograniczające ryzyko (technical & organisational measures)
- Privacy mode button: kierowca może aktywować tryb prywatny podczas użycia pojazdu w celach osobistych poza godzinami służby; w trybie tym rejestrowane są jedynie czasy startu i zakończenia, bez zapisu pozycji.
- Data minimization: zbierane są tylko dane operacyjne niezbędne do realizacji celów; brak monitoringu komunikacji osobistej.
- Access controls: dostęp do surowych danych lokalizacyjnych mają jedynie Fleet Manager i Dispatch; dostęp jest logowany; HR nie ma bezpośredniego dostępu — wymagana jest uzasadniona pisemna prośba zatwierdzona przez IOD i CTO.
- Pseudonymization: oceny zachowań kierowców (score) są agregowane i pseudonimizowane w raportach; indywidualne profile wykorzystywane wyłącznie do działań szkoleniowych i po ocenie celowości.
- Encryption at rest and in transit: szyfrowanie danych na urządzeniach telematycznych, serwerach oraz przy transferze; klucze dostępowe zarządzane przez dział IT z rejestrem dystrybucji.
- Retention & deletion controls: automatyczny proces usuwania danych po 90 dniach, audytowane procedury czyszczenia kopii zapasowych.
- Logging and auditing: rejestry dostępu do danych, okresowe przeglądy zgodności i audyty wewnętrzne.
- Szkolenia i instrukcje: obowiązkowe szkolenia BHP i RODO dla Fleet Managerów oraz dispatcherów.

10. Konsultacje i zaangażowanie interesariuszy
Konsultacje z Rada Pracowników: przeprowadzone; protokół konsultacji z dnia 2024-06-15. W wyniku dialogu okres retencji surowych danych skrócono z 12 miesięcy do 90 dni oraz wprowadzono mechanizm privacy mode.
Konsultacja z PUODO (Prezes Urzędu Ochrony Danych Osobowych): nie wymagana — zgodnie z art. 36 RODO, obowiązek konsultacji z organem nadzorczym nie zachodzi, ponieważ wdrożone środki minimalizujące i ocena ryzyka redukują pozostałe ryzyko do akceptowalnego poziomu.

11. Wnioski i rekomendacje
Podstawa prawna Art. 6(1)(f) RODO jest adekwatna. Przyjęte środki techniczne i organizacyjne znacząco ograniczają ryzyko do poziomu medium-akceptowalnego. Zaleca się:
- coroczną rewizję DPIA lub wcześniejszą w przypadku istotnej zmiany Systemu (nowe czujniki, dłuższe okresy retencji, integracje z HR);
- utrzymanie dialogu z Rada Pracowników przy każdej zmianie parametrów monitoringu;
- kontynuowanie praktyki ograniczania dostępu HR i formalizacji procedur udostępniania danych.

12. Review & monitoring
Review date: annually or upon significant system change. IOD will conduct periodic checks and report findings to Management Board and Rada Pracowników.

13. Approval
Prepared by: Marta Kowalczyk — Inspektor Ochrony Danych (IOD)
Consulted with: CTO (technical architecture), Fleet Manager (operational processes)
Approval: Management Board, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.

Załączniki: TECH-SPEC-002 (technical specification), Protokół konsultacji Rady Pracowników z 2024-06-15, Polityka Retencji Danych.

Niniejszy dokument stanowi formalną ocenę wpływu na ochronę danych osobowych dla Systemu monitoringu floty LogiCore Sp. z o.o. i powinien być stosowany jako podstawa do dalszych decyzji technicznych i organizacyjnych związanych z eksploatacją Systemu.

title: Annual Fire Safety Inspection Report — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Warszawa Site
text:
Report reference: FIRE-2025-001

Prepared for: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Przykładowa 12, 00-001 Warszawa (Warszawa site)
Inspection dates: 8–9 October 2025
Inspector: inż. Marek Bauer, rzeczoznawca ds. zabezpieczeń przeciwpożarowych, License #RZ-2018-0234
External assessor: Zakład Ekspertyz PPOŻ "Bauer i Wspólnicy"
Regulatory basis: Ustawa o ochronie przeciwpożarowej; Rozporządzenie MSWiA w sprawie ochrony przeciwpożarowej budynków, innych obiektów budowlanych i terenów

1. Purpose and scope
The purpose of this annual inspection was to verify the condition and operational readiness of active and passive fire protection systems at the LogiCore Warszawa site, in accordance with the cited legal acts and relevant technical standards. The inspection covered Warehouse A (6,800 m²), Warehouse B (4,200 m²) and the Office Building (1,200 m²). The scope included visual inspection, functional testing of fire detection and alarm systems, sprinkler verification, portable extinguishers, emergency lighting and evacuation routes. Tests were conducted during normal working hours with cooperation from site BHP and facility maintenance personnel.

2. Summary of findings
Overall result: PASSED with two observations (both corrected on site).
All inspected buildings meet minimum statutory requirements defined by the Ustawa o ochronie przeciwpożarowej and Rozporządzenie MSWiA concerning fire detection, alarm, extinguishing systems and means of escape. No significant non-conformities that would prevent safe operation were identified. Documentation and maintenance records were generally complete and demonstrate regular servicing consistent with RODO-compliant recordkeeping.

3. Facilities inspected
- Warehouse A: 6,800 m² — distribution and palletized storage, high-bay racking.
- Warehouse B: 4,200 m² — ambient and cold storage anterooms; mixed cargo.
- Office Building: 1,200 m² — administrative offices, meeting rooms, staff facilities.

4. Detailed inspection results by area
Warehouse A (6,800 m²)
- Portable extinguishers: One powder extinguisher (gaśnica proszkowa GP-6) assigned to Zone C-14 presented an expired service record (last service 2024-04-12, due 2025-04-12). This constituted a maintenance observation. The extinguisher was replaced with a serviceable unit during the inspection. New service certificate attached to site logbook. Observation CLOSED.
- Means of egress: Emergency exit 3 was found partially obstructed by two pallets placed in the escape route. This was a breach of Rozporządzenie MSWiA requirements regarding unobstructed escape paths. The pallets were removed immediately upon request and the area cleared. Supervisor on duty was notified and corrective action recorded. Observation CLOSED.
- Sprinkler system: Wet-pipe sprinkler network serving Warehouse A was subjected to a functional test. Eight representative sprinkler heads were tested for activation characteristics; all activated within manufacturer and design specification. Coverage verified against drawings; no coverage gaps observed. Pressure test results: passed within allowable tolerances.
- Fire alarm panel: Main FACP in Warehouse A operating and communicating with remote monitoring. Zones correctly mapped; event logs consistent with recent maintenance entries.

Warehouse B (4,200 m²)
- Portable extinguishers and fixed installations: All confirmed current, serviced and appropriately located per plan.
- Smoke detector: In the cold storage anteroom an optical smoke detector displayed an intermittent fault code during test. Detector remained generally functional but fault indicates potential contamination or wiring issue influenced by low temperatures. Maintenance ticket raised with priority status and recommended replacement or relocation to mitigate false/latent faults.
- Sprinkler system: Wet-pipe system verified adequate; no signs of leakage or obstruction.

Office Building (1,200 m²)
- General: All fire protection equipment inspected and found serviceable. Records complete.
- Fire drill log: Reviewed. Last full evacuation drill recorded on 12 September 2025. Evacuation time: 3 minutes 42 seconds — within the site target of 4 minutes. Participants and observations duly noted in logbook.

5. System tests (site-wide)
- Fire alarm system: Bosch FPA-5000 control panel — full loop test performed. System response from manual activation to full building audible/visual alarm: 2.3 minutes (target <3 minutes). All connected smoke and heat detectors tested during inspection and found functional. Zone mapping correct; annunciation at central panel corresponded to physical zones.
- Fire suppression: Sprinkler systems (wet pipe) in Warehouses A and B verified for adequate coverage and hydraulic performance. Pressure tests (static and operational) recorded values within design parameters; no corrective measures required.
- Emergency lighting: Emergency luminaires and illuminated exit signage tested on battery backup. All emergency lights illuminated upon simulated mains failure. Two exit signs exhibited reduced luminance (dim) and were marked for replacement; replacement ordered and expected delivery within 14 days. Temporary measures (auxiliary signage checks) applied until replacement.

6. Observations and corrective actions
Two observations were recorded during the inspection:
1) Expired extinguisher (Warehouse A, Zone C-14) — replaced during inspection; documentation updated. Observation CLOSED.
2) Blocked emergency exit (Emergency Exit 3, Warehouse A) — pallets cleared immediately; supervisor notified and instruction to reinforce housekeeping provided. Observation CLOSED.
Additional non-conformance: Intermittent fault on smoke detector in Warehouse B cold storage anteroom — maintenance ticket raised (Priority: medium-high). Recommendation to inspect wiring and evaluate relocation/replacement to a cold-room rated detector.

7. Recommendations
1) Implement a monthly visual check log for all escape routes and exits, to be signed by the shift supervisor, to prevent recurrence of temporary obstructions. This should be appended to the BHP site diary and available during audits (supports compliance with Rozporządzenie MSWiA and Kodeks Pracy obligations).
2) Replace the dim exit signs within 14 days and review battery maintenance cycle for older luminaires; consider progressive replacement to LED fixtures with extended battery life and lower maintenance needs.
3) For the cold storage anteroom detector: replace with a cold-rated detector or relocate outside the anteroom where environmental conditions do not compromise sensitivity; conduct follow-up test post-replacement to confirm stability.

8. Documentation reviewed
- Maintenance/service certificates for portable extinguishers, sprinkler and alarm systems
- Fire alarm event logs and FPA-5000 printouts
- Sprinkler hydraulic test reports
- Emergency lighting test records
- Fire drill records and evacuation timing logs

9. Next inspection
Annual re-inspection recommended in October 2026 or earlier if significant facility changes occur (racking, occupancy, process changes) or after any major incident.

10. Inspector certification
I hereby certify that the inspection described in this report was conducted by me, in accordance with applicable Polish law and recognised technical standards. Findings are true and accurate to the best of my knowledge.

Signed:
inż. Marek Bauer
rzeczoznawca ds. zabezpieczeń przeciwpożarowych
License #RZ-2018-0234
Zakład Ekspertyz PPOŻ "Bauer i Wspólnicy"
Date of report: 15 October 2025

Distribution: LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — Site Manager (Warszawa), BHP Department, Maintenance Supervisor, Corporate Compliance

title: Fuel Supply Agreement Summary — Contract PF-2025-001 between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and PetroFlex Sp. z o.o.
text:
FUEL SUPPLY AGREEMENT – SUMMARY / ABSTRACT
Contract No.: PF-2025-001
Parties: LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (Buyer) — headquartered Warszawa, depots in Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław — and PetroFlex Sp. z o.o. (Supplier).
Effective Date: 1 January 2025
Term: Initial term of 24 months (2 years) with automatic renewal for successive 12-month periods unless either party provides written notice of termination at least ninety (90) days prior to the then-current term expiry.
Scope of Agreement
- Subject matter: Supply and delivery of motor diesel (EN 590) and diesel exhaust fluid (AdBlue compliant with ISO 22241) for use in LogiCore’s vehicle fleet and depot storage tanks located in the Republic of Poland.
- Delivery locations (depots): Warszawa, Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław. Supplier to service all four depots under a single contractual framework.
Contract Volumes
- Diesel (EN 590): Minimum supply obligation of 8,000 L per depot per week (total minimum weekly volume 32,000 L). Representative monthly minimum: 128,000 L (calculated on typical four-week month basis). Parties acknowledge operational fluctuations but the Supplier guarantees the minimum volumes unless otherwise agreed in writing.
- AdBlue (ISO 22241): Minimum aggregate supply 2,000 L per calendar month across the four depots.
Pricing, Indexation and Fees
- Diesel price: Base market price indexed to Platts NWE Diesel (CIF NWE Cargoes). Indexation to be applied on a weekly basis. The quoted index price shall be adjusted to Buyer’s delivery points using agreed logistics differentials. In addition to the indexed price, an agreed handling fee of EUR 0.03 per litre (EUR 0.03/L) shall be added to each diesel delivery invoice.
- AdBlue price: Fixed price of EUR 0.35 per litre (EUR 0.35/L) for the duration of the Agreement, excluding VAT and any applicable duties.
- All contract prices are denominated in EUR for cross-border consistency. Local taxes, excise duties and levies, if applicable per Polish law and Ustawa o podatku akcyzowym, will be invoiced as required by law and listed separately.
Delivery, Scheduling and Emergency Supply
- Routine delivery: Tanker delivery according to an agreed weekly schedule per depot. Supplier to provide planned schedule no later than seven (7) days in advance and confirm routing and vehicle details in accordance with Prawa przewozowego and transport permits.
- Ordering: Buyer shall place routine orders with a minimum of forty-eight (48) hours prior notice to scheduled delivery. Orders to be delivered per confirmed schedule.
- Emergency delivery: Supplier shall provide emergency deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours of Buyer’s request subject to availability. Emergency delivery will incur a one-time surcharge of EUR 200 per emergency call-out (in addition to per-litre charges) unless emergency arises from Supplier default, in which case Buyer’s costs for alternative supply are reimbursable by Supplier as set out under Penalties.
Quality Assurance and Compliance
- Diesel specification: All diesel supplied must conform to EN 590:2013+A1:2017 (or later applicable standard adopted in Poland/EU). AdBlue must conform to ISO 22241.
- Documentation: Each delivery must be accompanied by a Certificate of Quality (CoQ) and delivery note (list przewozowy / CMR as applicable), listing batch number, supplier, testing laboratory, and results for parameters required by EN 590 / ISO 22241.
- Sampling and testing: LogiCore reserves the right to take independent samples at point of delivery or from storage tanks and to have samples tested at an independent laboratory. If results indicate non-conformance, Supplier shall, at its cost, promptly replace or remove the non-conforming fuel and reimburse Buyer for demonstrable direct costs (including costs of alternative supply and vehicle downtime). Supplier shall also bear costs of confirmation testing if non-conformity is established.
- Safety and legal compliance: Supplier warrants compliance with applicable Polish and EU laws governing transport and supply of fuels (including Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, ADR where applicable) and environmental standards; Supplier must hold all licences and permits required for storage, transport and sale of fuels.
Payment and Invoicing
- Payment terms: Buyer shall pay invoices within fourteen (14) calendar days from delivery date (data dostawy). Late payments will accrue interest in accordance with Polish law and agreed contractual provisions.
- Invoicing: Supplier to issue electronic invoices (faktura elektroniczna) and send to faktura@logicore.pl. Invoices must reference Contract PF-2025-001, delivery note numbers and include full VAT breakdown in accordance with Polish VAT law.
Shortfall, Penalties and Remedies
- Short delivery: If Supplier fails to deliver the minimum contracted diesel volume per depot per week, Supplier shall pay a contractual penalty of EUR 0.10 per litre for the shortfall against the minimum weekly quantity. In addition, Supplier shall reimburse Buyer for any additional demonstrable costs incurred by Buyer to source replacement fuel on the market (emergency purchase costs), including transportation and premium prices paid, as substantiated by receipts.
- Repeated shortfalls (three or more occurrences in a rolling six-month period) constitute material breach and entitle Buyer to exercise termination rights for cause.
Tanks, Maintenance and Calibration
- Tank maintenance: PetroFlex is responsible for annual (minimum once per 12 months) cleaning, inspection and calibration of Buyer’s above-ground and in-scope underground storage tanks used for deliveries under this Agreement. The costs of routine annual cleaning, inspection and calibration activities are included in the EUR 0.03/L handling fee. Any remedial works beyond routine cleaning (e.g., repairs due to defects not caused by Supplier) to be agreed and invoiced separately.
Liability, Environmental Matters and Permits
- Permits: Supplier warrants it holds and will maintain all permits required for the transport, handling and delivery of fuels in Poland and across Central Europe as applicable, including ADR compliance and any local municipal requirements at depot sites.
- Spill and delivery liability: Responsibility for cargo and any environmental damage during transport and delivery rests with Supplier (carrier liability) until the moment fuel physically passes over into Buyer’s storage tank (formal acceptance). Supplier must immediately notify Buyer and relevant authorities (PSP, Inspektorat Ochrony Środowiska) of any spill or environmental incident and shall bear costs of containment, remediation and fines attributable to Supplier’s operations.
Insurance
- Supplier shall maintain at its own cost carrier’s third-party liability insurance (OC przewoźnika) with minimum limits of EUR 2,000,000 (two million euros) per occurrence for the duration of the Agreement, and shall provide certificates of insurance upon request.
Termination and Suspension
- Ordinary termination: Either party may terminate the Agreement with three (3) months written notice prior to the intended termination date (notwithstanding automatic renewal provisions).
- Immediate termination: Buyer may immediately suspend deliveries and terminate the Agreement for material breaches including, but not limited to, repeated quality failures, delivery of fuel not conforming to EN 590/ISO 22241, failure to maintain required permits or insurance, or any act creating an imminent threat to health, safety or the environment (safety violation). Immediate termination to be without prejudice to Buyer’s right to damages and recovery of costs.
Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
- Governing law: This Agreement and all matters arising from it shall be governed by Polish substantive law.
- Dispute resolution: Parties agree to submit disputes to arbitration before the Sąd Arbitrażowy przy Krajowej Izbie Gospodarczej (Sąd Arbitrażowy przy KIG), Warsaw seat, in accordance with its rules. Interim relief may be sought from common courts where appropriate.
Miscellaneous
- Notices: All contractual notices shall be in writing and sent to the registered addresses of the Parties or to designated commercial contacts. Buyer invoices and accounting correspondence to be directed to faktura@logicore.pl as primary electronic invoice address.
- Confidentiality and data protection: Parties shall treat commercial terms and any personal data exchanged in connection with execution of this Agreement in accordance with RODO and internal confidentiality rules.
This summary is intended as an executive abstract of the principal commercial and operational terms of Contract PF-2025-001. The full Agreement sets out detailed obligations, definitions, performance metrics, force majeure, indemnities, and schedules referenced above and shall prevail in the event of any inconsistency.
Prepared by: Legal Department, LogiCore Sp. z o.o.
Date: 2024-12-15

title: Fleet Maintenance Contract — Summary of Terms and Service Level Agreement (MS-2025-001)
text:
Contract Summary: Fleet Maintenance Contract (MS-2025-001)

Parties
- Client: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Centralna 12, 00-001 Warszawa, Poland. Headquartered in Warszawa with depots in Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław and Poznań; operating across Poland and Central Europe.
- Service Provider: AutoService24 Sp. z o.o., registered office: ul. Serwisowa 3, 01-100 Warszawa.

Effective Date and Term
- Effective Date: 1 April 2025 (2025-04-01).
- Term: three (3) years, terminating 2028-03-31 unless earlier terminated per contract provisions.

Scope of Agreement
- Preventive and corrective maintenance for Client fleet of forty-five (45) commercial units: 32 tractors (ciągniki siodłowe) and 13 trailers (naczepy).
- Maintenance to conform to manufacturer recommendations for MAN, Volvo and DAF vehicles. Preventive maintenance schedule includes:
  - Oil change: every 60,000 km.
  - Brake inspection (including pads, discs, drums): every 90,000 km.
  - Full service (comprehensive inspection and servicing): annually (or as required by manufacturer mileage thresholds).
- Coverage includes scheduled workshop repairs and mobile/roadside interventions as detailed below.

Response Times and Scheduling
- Roadside assistance (mobilna pomoc drogowa): maximum response time 4 hours, available 24/7 for entire fleet coverage area.
- Scheduled service (warsztatowe serwisy): appointment response within 24 hours on business days (Mon–Fri).
- Annual przegląd (technical inspection) preparation/booking: Service Provider to reserve a slot with minimum 5 business days advance booking to ensure legal przegląd and documentation readiness.

Service Scope (Included)
- Engine oil and filter changes (OEM/OEM-equivalent parts).
- Brake service: pads, discs, drums replacement and adjustments; brake system hydraulic/air checks.
- Tire management: rotation, seasonal change (letnie/zimowe), replacement and storage handling if agreed.
- Electrical diagnostics: ECU checks, alternator, starter, lighting systems.
- Air system maintenance: compressors, reservoirs, valves, trailer air lines.
- Annual TÜV/przegląd preparation and assistance with necessary corrective works to pass inspection.
- AdBlue system service: sensors, dosing units, tank checks and injector cleaning as required.

Exclusions (Not Included)
- Bodywork and structural repair of damage (blacharka, lakierowanie) — referred to specialist workshops.
- Tachograph calibration and certification (separate UDT-authorized contract) — tachograph calibration explicitly excluded.
- Interior cleaning and detailed valeting of cabins.
- Accessories not integral to vehicle operation (e.g., aftermarket furnishings, non-standard telematics hardware) unless otherwise agreed.

Parts and Warranty
- Parts supplied to be OEM or OEM-equivalent quality; all parts to meet EU and manufacturer standards.
- Parts and labour warranty: twelve (12) months or 50,000 km, whichever occurs first.
- Consumables (oils, filters) covered under parts provisions.

Commercial Terms
- Monthly retainer fee: EUR 185 per vehicle. For 45 vehicles the monthly retainer equals EUR 8,325.00 (EUR 185 x 45).
- Parts and consumables invoiced at cost + 15% markup (to cover procurement, logistics and handling).
- Invoicing monthly in EUR; payment terms: net 30 days from invoice date.
- Taxes, duties and statutory charges handled according to applicable law (Poland/EU).

Service Locations and Resources
- Primary workshop: Warszawa (headquarters/central workshop).
- Secondary workshop support: Katowice depot for Silesian operations; mobile service unit(s) for roadside assistance across Poland and Central Europe.
- Service Provider to maintain records compliant with RODO for personal data, and to observe health & safety rules (BHP), Kodeks Pracy and applicable employment law for assigned staff.

Performance Metrics (KPIs)
- Vehicle availability: maintained >96% measured monthly (availability = operational hours / total required operational hours).
- First-time fix rate: >90% (repairs completed without follow-up visit to remedy the same defect).
- Mean time to repair (MTTR) for non-major repairs: <8 hours (clock hours from workshop admission or start of intervention).

Penalties and Remedies
- If monthly vehicle availability falls below 94% in any calendar month, Service Provider will receive a 10% reduction of the monthly retainer for that month as liquidated damages.
- Repeated KPI failures (three months below thresholds in any 12-month period) permit Client to require a corrective action plan and, if unresolved, to terminate for breach per contract provisions.

Reporting and Governance
- Monthly maintenance report: details of interventions, parts used, downtime per unit, KPI performance, warranty claims and cost summary.
- Quarterly fleet health assessment: trend analysis, root-cause analysis of repeated failures, projected maintenance spend and suggested mitigations.
- Annual fleet replacement recommendation: technical summary recommending replacement candidates (based on mileage, recurring faults, economic analysis), to assist LogiCore fleet planning and CAPEX decisions.
- Regular coordination meetings: monthly operational call and quarterly executive review.

Compliance and Regulatory
- Service Provider to comply with Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, relevant EU regulations, and to coordinate inspections with UDT, PIP and PSP where required.
- Any handling of hazardous materials, fluids or expired parts to follow environmental and waste disposal regulations and GIF/PIP guidance where applicable.

Termination and Dispute Resolution
- Termination for material breach after 30-day cure period; early termination fees as specified in full contract.
- Disputes to be resolved by negotiation; if unresolved, competent courts in Warszawa (Sąd Rejonowy dla m.st. Warszawy) and Polish law to govern, language of contract: English with Polish translation provided for legal/regulatory filings.

Signatures
- This summary forms part of the full Fleet Maintenance Contract MS-2025-001 between LogiCore Sp. z o.o. (Client) and AutoService24 Sp. z o.o. (Service Provider). Full detailed contract contains operational annexes, SLA schedule, price list, and attachments including vehicle list and depot addresses.

Prepared by: Legal Department, LogiCore Sp. z o.o. — For internal use, clearance level 2. References: Kodeks Pracy, RODO, Ustawa o transporcie drogowym, UDT requirements and manufacturer service bulletins (MAN, Volvo, DAF).

title: Cleaning and Pest Control Service Agreement — CP-2025-001 (Warszawa Depot)
text:
CLEANING AND PEST CONTROL SERVICE AGREEMENT
Agreement No.: CP-2025-001
Effective Date: 1 February 2025
Parties:
  - LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. Centralna 10, 00-123 Warszawa ("LogiCore"),
  - CleanPro Facility Services Sp. z o.o., ul. Serwisowa 5, 01-234 Warszawa ("CleanPro").
Introduction
  This Agreement sets out the terms by which CleanPro will provide cleaning services and integrated pest management (IPM) at LogiCore's Warszawa depot (Warehouse A, Warehouse B and Depot Office). The Parties acknowledge the need for compliance with HACCP requirements, applicable Polish law (including Kodeks Pracy, RODO obligations where processing of personal data occurs, and relevant sanitary and pharmaceutical rules where applicable) and industry standards (ISO, Good Storage Practice).
Term
  The Agreement commences on 01.02.2025 for an initial term of 24 months ("Term") and shall automatically renew for successive 12‑month periods unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal at least 90 days prior to the then-current term expiry.
Scope of Services
  CleanPro shall provide routine and deep cleaning plus integrated pest management for the Warszawa depot at the locations specified (Warehouse A, Warehouse B, Office). Services shall be delivered in accordance with the schedule below and with HACCP-compliant materials in Zones A-B (food storage).
Cleaning Schedule
  a) Warehouse zones (A and B): weekly deep clean including industrial vacuuming, wet mop of floors, rack surface dusting and removal of debris. Daily spot cleaning to address spills, foreign material and immediate contamination risks.
  b) Office areas: daily general cleaning (vacuuming, desk wipe-down with disinfectant, bathroom sanitisation, kitchen area cleaning). Weekly interior window cleaning (doors and high-touch glass surfaces included).
  c) Loading docks and external receiving: daily sweeping and clearing of debris. Weekly pressure wash of dock surfaces and ramps, weather permitting and coordinated with operations to avoid disruption.
  d) Cold storage: monthly defrost and deep clean of cold rooms, conducted in coordination with LogiCore warehouse scheduling to minimise temperature disruption and product risk.
Pest Control (Integrated Pest Management — IPM)
  a) Rodent control: installation and maintenance of 24 exterior bait stations and 12 electronic monitoring traps inside warehouses. Stations and traps will be checked monthly and after any incident.
  b) Insect control: installation of 8 UV light traps at primary warehouse entrances and shift-room entrances. Electric fly killers to be installed and maintained in designated food-handling zones.
  c) Response SLA: CleanPro shall respond to critical pest incidents (e.g., live rodent sighting within warehouse storage areas) within four (4) hours of notification. For non-critical events (e.g., increased insect activity) response within twenty-four (24) hours.
  d) Quarterly pest audit report: CleanPro will submit a comprehensive pest audit to LogiCore's Quality Manager within ten (10) business days after the end of each quarter, including trap/bait activity logs, corrective actions, trend analysis and recommendations.
HACCP and Safety Compliance
  All cleaning chemicals and consumables used in Zones A-B (food storage/handling areas) shall be food-safe and suitable for HACCP environments. CleanPro shall provide Karty Charakterystyki (Material Safety Data Sheets — MSDS) for all substances used prior to first use and upon any formulation change. Procedures shall align with HACCP principles and any relevant Prawo Farmaceutyczne requirements for pharmaceutical storage areas if applicable.
Fees and Payment
  Monthly fee: PLN 18,500 (cleaning PLN 14,500; pest control PLN 4,000). Invoices submitted monthly in arrears, payable within thirty (30) days of receipt. Fees are exclusive of VAT where applicable.
Penalties and Liability
  If a failed HACCP or ISO audit of the Warszawa depot is determined by LogiCore to be attributable to cleaning or pest control deficiencies under CleanPro's responsibility, CleanPro will pay a contractual penalty of PLN 8,000 per instance. CleanPro shall maintain third-party liability insurance (OC) with minimum coverage of PLN 2,000,000 and provide proof of insurance upon execution and upon renewal.
Key Personnel and Availability
  CleanPro will assign a dedicated Site Supervisor for Warehouse A who will be the primary on-site contact. The Site Supervisor shall be reachable by phone between 06:00 and 22:00 daily. CleanPro will provide names, contact details and backup personnel for continuity.
Reporting and Records
  CleanPro will maintain monthly service logs, pest monitoring charts, chemical usage records and MSDS in both electronic and hardcopy on site for audit by LogiCore, PIP or other competent authorities (e.g., SANEPID) and will retain records for a minimum of 3 years unless law requires longer retention.
Compliance with Law and Access
  CleanPro warrants compliance with applicable Polish law (including Kodeks Pracy for personnel, RODO for any personal data processing, health & safety regulations and relevant standards). LogiCore reserves the right to inspect services and require corrective actions where non-compliance is identified.
Termination and Force Majeure
  Either Party may terminate for material breach if the breaching Party fails to remedy the breach within thirty (30) days of written notice. Termination for convenience by either Party requires ninety (90) days' notice. Force majeure events shall suspend obligations for the duration of the event; Parties will use reasonable efforts to mitigate impacts.
Signatures
  This Agreement is executed by authorised representatives of the Parties.

For LogiCore Sp. z o.o.: _______________________  Date: __________
For CleanPro Facility Services Sp. z o.o.: ________  Date: __________

Annexes: Service schedule detail, map of bait station locations, MSDS register, SLA contact list, quarterly audit template.

title: Cyber Insurance Policy Summary — LogiCore Sp. z o.o. / Policy CYB-2025-ELG-001
text:
Cyber Insurance Policy Summary
Policy No.: CYB-2025-ELG-001
Insurer: PZU Cyber Protection (PZU S.A.)
Policyholder: LogiCore Sp. z o.o., ul. (Headquarters) Warszawa; depots: Katowice, Gdańsk, Wrocław, Poznań. Operacje: Polska i Europa Środkowa.
Policy Period: 2025-01-01 to 2025-12-31. Automatic renewal subject to underwriting review.
Coverage Limits: EUR 5,000,000 per incident; EUR 10,000,000 aggregate per policy period.
Premium: EUR 28,000 per year.
Deductible (Excess): EUR 15,000 per incident.

1. Scope of Coverage (Summary)
This policy provides first- and third-party cyber cover for LogiCore Sp. z o.o. and covers cyber events occurring during the policy period subject to terms, conditions and exclusions below.

A. Ransomware
- Payment of reasonable costs for data decryption and restoration.
- Ransom payment covered only with prior written approval from PZU Cyber Protection and subject to anti-money laundering checks and sanctions screening.
- Business interruption (BI) consequent to ransomware is covered under the BI section (see below).

B. Data Breach / Privacy Event
- Notification costs required under RODO (Art. 34) and related communications to affected data subjects.
- Forensic investigation to determine scope and root cause.
- Credit monitoring and identity protection services for affected individuals where appropriate.
- Legal defense costs for claims brought by data subjects or customers.
- Cooperation with PUODO (Prezes Urzędu Ochrony Danych Osobowych) inspections and provision for reasonable response costs; note regulatory fines themselves may be non-insurable under Polish law and are subject to exclusion as noted.

C. Business Interruption (BI)
- Indemnity for loss of revenue resulting from a covered cyber incident that disrupts LogiCore’s operations.
- Waiting period: 72 hours from time of interruption.
- Daily indemnity calculated on average daily revenue (historical 12-month average unless agreed otherwise) up to policy limits.

D. Regulatory Fines — Defense
- Payment of legal costs to defend LogiCore against regulatory enforcement actions by PUODO or other competent authorities.
- Explicit note: monetary fines/penalties imposed under PUODO or other authorities may be excluded or limited consistent with Polish law; insured should seek legal advice regarding insurability of fines.

E. Third-Party Liability
- Coverage for claims by customers, partners or third parties arising from a data breach or cyber event originating in LogiCore systems, including defense costs and settlements up to limits.

F. Crisis Management & Communications
- Costs for PR and communication consultants and customer notification services to manage reputation and stakeholder communications following a covered incident.

2. Exclusions (principal)
- Nation-state cyber operations, cyber warfare, acts of state-sponsored attackers.
- Losses arising from known/unremediated vulnerabilities where a patch or fix was available and not applied within 90 days prior to the incident.
- Social engineering / fraud losses in excess of EUR 50,000 (losses below this threshold may be considered; separate crime/fidelity insurance is recommended for full coverage).
- Intentional wrongful acts by senior management (directors/officers) of LogiCore.
- Losses resulting from failure to maintain the minimum security standards set out below.
- Any penalties/fines expressly prohibited from insurance by applicable law.

3. Security Requirements (Conditions Precedent and Ongoing Conditions)
Coverage is conditional on LogiCore maintaining and evidencing the following minimum controls. Failure to comply may void coverage for related losses.
- Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled on all external-facing systems and administrative accounts. Verification to be performed annually.
- Annual penetration test conducted by a certified independent firm (OWASP/CREST or equivalent); full report to be submitted to PZU within 30 days of completion (confidential handling arranged).
- Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) deployed on all company-managed endpoints with centralized logging and retention policy.
- Backup strategy aligned with 3-2-1 rule (3 copies, 2 different media types, 1 copy offsite). Backups must be tested for recoverability at least quarterly; test results retained.
- Security awareness training for all employees at least once annually, including phishing simulations and recordkeeping of attendance/metrics.
- Documented Incident Response Plan (IRP) that is tested (tabletop or live) at least annually. After each test or real incident, an after-action report must be prepared and retained.

4. Claims Notification & Handling Procedure
- Immediate action: In the event of suspected or confirmed incident, LogiCore must: (a) notify PZU Cyber Protection within 24 hours of discovery (or as soon as practicable), (b) preserve logs, devices and evidence, and (c) not alter systems except to contain the incident and preserve forensic integrity.
- Forensics: Engagement of an insurer-approved forensic investigation firm is required for coverage of forensic costs. If LogiCore engages an external firm prior to insurer consent, costs may be conditional on retrospective approval.
- Cooperation: LogiCore must cooperate with PZU and any appointed counsel or investigators, provide requested documentation and assist in regulatory notifications (e.g., RODO Art. 34 notices).
- Ransom payments: Any ransom payment requires prior written approval from PZU and compliance with applicable sanctions/AML checks.

5. Operational & Legal Notes
- Policy governed by applicable laws (Poland/EU). Where a conflict exists between policy wording and mandatory national law (e.g., insurability of fines), national law prevails.
- This summary does not amend or replace the full policy wording. Refer to full policy documents issued by PZU S.A. (PZU Cyber Protection) for complete terms, conditions, definitions and exclusions.

Document prepared for internal use by LogiCore Sp. z o.o. IT / Risk Management. For questions or to initiate a claim contact: IT Security Manager, LogiCore; Policy Administrator at PZU Cyber Protection.

Issued: 2024-12-01
Classification: Internal — IT (clearance level 3)
